A multiplier to the maximum substructure supported by the gravship.
A multiplier applied to armor penetration of attacks by this pawn.
A bonus to the quality of art produced by this pawn.
A multiplier to how fast this pawn creates art.
A multiplier applied to the chance for failed taming attempts to result in animals attacking this pawn.
attack on tame fail chance factor
How fast this pawn can create components (both industrial and advanced).
A bonus added to the quality of things built by this pawn.
A bonus to the quality of non-art crafts produced by this pawn.
How fast this pawn can work on floors.
A multiplier applied to the cooldown of melee attacks by this pawn.
How likely this pawn is to succeed at peace talks.
How fast this pawn can recruit prisoners to the colony.
How fast this pawn can do repairs.
The chance for this pawn to get a chunk when mining natural rock.
How fast this pawn can work on roofs.
How fast this pawn tailors clothes.
How fast this pawn can create weapons.
The cooldown on ranged weapons of the wearer is multiplied by this value.
A multiplier applied to the speed this person can write books at.
The chance for every fishing attempt to fish a special item instead of a regular fish is increased by this offset.
The speed at which a person can fish is multiplied by this factor.
The fishing yield of a person is multiplied by this factor.
The chance that this colonist will convert a crafted meal into a higher type (simple to fine, fine to lavish). Applies to bakes, grills and base game meals.
The chance that this colonist will add a random condiment to any crafted meal (except resurrector nanites).
How effective musical sources of enjoyment are, such as radios.
The maximum energy a shield generator can have at one time. More energy absorbs more damage.
The area covered when the energy shield is active.
The rate at which a shield gains energy as long as it is not broken.
How many times does this trap hit its targets.\n\nThe total trap damage is spread evenly across all the hits.
The minimum amount of neural heat. This is increased by maintaining certain psycasts, which give heat that never fades.
A multiplier on psyfocus cost of psycasts.
Basilicus enforces the will of the emperor wherever he goes, bestowing great powers on those who deserve it. Epic ballads become even more fantastic if everyone has access to psychic powers.
failed a medical operation and killed one of us
has been my friend since childhood
slaughtered an animal in a rage
went on a fire starting spree

A warped and twisted section of gravship substructure. Once a solid truss with decking, it has been bent out of shape by heavy stress or impact. The grav engineâ€™s levitation field still grips it, but its integrity is compromised: any further damage will cause this piece of substructure to collapse completely.

A scaffold layer built to support the framework of gravships. While part of the gravshipâ€™s structure, it cannot support buildings and slows movement significantly. It does not count toward the gravshipâ€™s substructure limit, making it ideal for walkways, access paths, or expansion staging areas.
A mechanoid-made lightweight truss structure with flooring on top that forms the deck of a swarmship. Only things on top of this substructure can be lifted by the grav engine's levitation field.
A rough floor made with trashbricks. Not pretty, but cheap to make.
Industrial-style asphalt used to create roads and streets. Very easy to traverse for people and vehicles alike.
Cheap looking, industry standard carpets for not-so-fancy places.
Plush carpet in a cozy dark shade of red.
Rich orange carpet symbolising wealth and prosperity.
Fancy dark shade of carpet for the secret evil lair.
Exquisite, sea-colored carpet for the high-tier professional environment.
Eccentric, glowing yellow carpet, shining bright in daylight.
Rich red carpet with deep crimson hue.
Deep green carpet with blue hues, for the natural yet exquisite look.
Nutty, dark carpet that brings detailed memories of home.
Subtle creamy carpet for the neutral yet posh rooms.
Natural and posh green carpet with beautiful patterns.
Neutral green carpet, excellent for hotels and beds and breakfasts.
Eccentric orange carpet for the business board rooms.
Soft purple carpet for the cozy bedroom look.
Corporate, hard to clean white carpet.

Foundations are structures that can be built on mud, quicksand etc. Foundation allows more rapid movement across these normally slowing ground types. Once built, they can support many structures, including heavy structures and any other type of wall. Foundations allow for easier building in desert and swampy biomes. Floors cannot be built atop foundations.
Beautiful wooden parquet, made with no compromises, for expressing economic status. Slow to construct.
Beautiful wooden parquet, made with no compromises, for expressing economic status. Slow to construct.
Beautiful wooden parquet, made with no compromises, for expressing economic status. Slow to construct.
Beautiful wooden parquet, made with no compromises, for expressing economic status. Slow to construct.
Field of grass with patches of soil here and there. Very pleasant to look at, it requires no resources to construct.
Beautifully trimmed grass with very high beauty value. Requires tremendous amounts of work to construct.
Old, broken apart asphalt, often found in the form of ancient highways around abandoned locations. Very easy to traverse for people and vehicles alike.
Packed dirt, slightly enhancing movement speed, often forming paths around places of interest.
Wood plank flooring for that warm, homey feeling. While it is quick to lay, it is also very flammable.
Wood plank flooring for that warm, homey feeling. While it is quick to lay, it is also very flammable.
Wood plank flooring for that warm, homey feeling. While it is quick to lay, it is also very flammable.
Toe-hugging wooly carpet in a cool blue color.
Cheap looking, industry standard carpets for not-so-fancy places.
Wooly carpet in a cozy dark shade of red.
Naturalistic-feeling green wooly carpet. Earth tones, dude.
Neutral green carpet, excellent for hotels and beds and breakfasts.
Eccentric orange carpet for the business board rooms.
Soft purple carpet for the cozy bedroom look.
Wooly carpet in a lovely rose hue.
Corporate, hard to clean white wooly carpet.
Reinforced steel flooring capable of supporting factory buildings. Slow to traverse and ugly to look at.
Rough concrete. Broken due to lack of maintenance.
Toe-hugging plush carpet in a cool blue color.
Classic burgundy carpet for that classic rich look.
Soft and silky indigo carpet, for that extra sense of pomp.
Sublime white carpet, glimmering with subtle patterns.
Naturalistic-feeling green carpet. Earth tones, dude.
Plush carpet in a lovely rose hue.

Tillage is the agricultural preparation of soil by mechanical agitation of various types, increasing terrain fertility. Tilling soil can only be done on normal soil (not rich soil). When a plant is harvested on a plowed soil, the floor becomes a normal soil again and needs to be plowed again manually.
High stake slots, default stake is 6$.
Medium priced slots, default stake is 4$.
Classic slots, default stake is only 2$, perfect for beginners.
A vending machine for guests that requires no power.
A vending machine to sell perishable food to guests.
A vending machine to sell perishable food to guests.
A standard vending machine for guests.
A cash register. Workplace for your waiters. From here you can control your restaurant.
A partially upholstered kind of bench, comfy enough to relax but not to fall asleep.
A burried bundle of electrical cables for moving power around.
Designates a spot for items from outposts to be delivered to.
An astrofuel storage tank that supplies fuel for thrusters on a gravship.

A power generator that passively draws a small amount of energy from gravitational waves passing through the gravship. Adjacent gravcore power cells will multiply their power output by 15% for each gravcore power cell.

An ultratech device used to increase the size of a gravship. It extends the area in which substructure can be built, and increases the amount of substructure that a gravship can support. Must be built near a grav engine. A gravship can support up to ten grav field extenders.
A massive astrofuel storage tank that supplies fuel for thrusters on a gravship.

An enclosed, space-efficient growing unit designed for use on gravships. Built into the floor for unobstructed movement, it features integrated sunlamps and a nutrient system that must remain powered, as plants will die if power is cut.
A small-sized box containing large uranium shells. Used by different gauss artillery turrets to reload. A box is fed directly into an adjacent gravship artillery when it needs to be reloaded.

A medium gravship-mounted energy weapon that fires short, razor-sharp bursts of cutting beam with surgical precision. Built to punch holes through critical systems, the caster trades sustained suppression for rapid, accurate strikes. Requires a linked targeting terminal with an operator to function. Uses a lot of power to fire.

A utility pack containing a large supply of compressed oxygen. When worn with vacuum-sealed apparel, it provides full immunity to vacuum by supplying breathable air. The pack only consumes oxygen while in vacuum and must be periodically reloaded. It also dramatically reduces exposure to pollution through the lungs.

A protective suit that keeps the user safe in the vacuum of space. The suit restricts the wearer's movement, considerably reducing their movement speed.\n\nExpensive and beautiful, prestige vacsuits like this is usually made for those of high status in the royal navy. Embedded eltex threads also enhance the wearer's psychic sensitivity.

A helmet that efficiently recycles the user's oxygen, allowing them to breathe in the vacuum of space for hours at a time. The process isn't perfectly efficient, so the user must periodically return to a pressurized environment.\n\nExpensive and beautiful, prestige vacsuit helmet like this is usually made for those of high status in the royal navy. Embedded eltex threads also enhance the wearer's psychic sensitivity.

A high-energy synthetic fuel. Used to power gravship thrusters and boosters, or to generate electricity in specialized systems. Extremely combustible, it burns violently even in low-oxygen environments.
A hopper-like storage structure meant to input astrofuel into the network, filling up connected tanks.
Produces power by consuming astrofuel. Much more efficient than chemfuel generators.

A large pipe for transporting high-grade astrofuel around. Unlike other pipes, itâ€™s big, bulky, acts as cover and canâ€™t be stood on. Astrofuel pipes canâ€™t be placed on workbench interaction spots and in doorways.

A specialized structure that refines chemfuel into high-grade astrofuel. It must be connected to the astrofuel pipe network and linked to at least one astrofuel tank to operate. Once connected, it automatically converts inserted chemfuel into astrofuel for use in engines and systems.
A hopper-like storage device meant to hold dispensed astrofuel for use later.
Switches astrofuel flow on and off.
A medium sized mechanoid designed to perform construction and firefighting tasks in orbit. It can perform blunt melee attacks if necessary. It is not affected by the slow space traversing speeds.

Heavily armored mechanoids adapted for zero-G warfare. Astropedes drift silently through space using gravitic stabilizers, unleashing explosive kinetic barrages from their minified mass lances. Their hovering profile makes them ideal for shipboard assaults and station breaches, though their slow movement speed leaves them vulnerable to concentrated fire.

A gravtech unit that enhances the precision and stability of astrofuel refinement. When placed next to an astrofuel synthesizer, it enables perfect 1:1 conversion from chemfuel to astrofuel, though the process takes twice as long.
A specialized device that balances and synchronizes energy flow between nearby capacitors. It passively boosts their charge and discharge efficiency to peak performance. Requires power to operate.
A compact version of a comms console that allows radio contact with other factions and orbital traders. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A miniaturized version of the biosculpter pod, designed to fit within the confined spaces of a gravship. It performs a variety of biological alterations including age reversal and pleasure-giving. The pod biotunes to its user at the end of a cycle. While biotuned, it cannot be used by anyone else, but cycles will complete 25% more quickly. Biotuning resets if the pod is unused for 80 days. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered drug lab designed for use aboard gravships. Despite its smaller frame, it’s equipped with containers, heaters, and measurement devices for producing various drugs. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered fabrication bench designed for use aboard gravships. Despite its smaller frame, it’s equipped with advanced tools for producing technological marvels from simpler materials. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A miniaturized version of the gene bank, designed to fit within the confined spaces of a gravship. It stores genepacks and makes them usable for creating new xenogerms when placed near a gene assembler. It draws power directly from the grav engine and prevents genepacks from deteriorating while slowly repairing deterioration.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered machining table designed for use aboard gravships. Despite its smaller frame, it’s ideal for assembling machinery like guns and ammunition, or breaking down dead mechanoids. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A miniaturized version of the multi-analyzer, designed to work aboard a gravship. While taking up less space, it performs the same essential function: increasing research speed when placed near a high-tech research bench and unlocking advanced projects. Each bench can only benefit from one multi-analyzer. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered biofuel refinery designed for use aboard gravships. Despite its smaller frame, it refines biological matter like wood or plant matter into chemfuel. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A miniaturized version of the high-tech research bench, designed to fit within the confined spaces of a gravship. Despite its compact size, it features the same computers and electronic equipment, providing the same research capabilities and unlocking advanced projects as its larger counterpart. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered smithy designed for use aboard gravships. Despite its smaller frame, it can be used to smith non-mechanical weapons and tools, as well as extract usable metal from slag chunks. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered stove designed for use aboard gravships. Despite its smaller frame, it contains a high-efficiency cooktop and integrated prep surface, as well as a butchering slate. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered tailor bench designed for use aboard gravships. Despite its smaller frame, this workbench comes with a sewing machine for rapid tailoring of clothes. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
Gold-rich strata crushed and recrystallized into a tight, resilient vein. Found only in deep space.
Jade-bearing vacstone compacted into a rigid, fine-grained mass. Found only in deep space.
Ultra-dense plasteel conglomerate formed in vacuum over generations; brutally tough to mine. Found only in deep space.
Silver-bearing rock compacted under extreme pressure into a dense, uniform seam. Found only in deep space.
Ancient structural mass fused into a single, ultra-dense vacstone-steel deposit. Found only in deep space.
Uranium-laden rock sintered into a nearly monolithic deposit, difficult to fracture. Found only in deep space.
An ultra-dense mass of asteroid rock and dust, compacted over millennia into a near-indestructible block. Found only in deep space.
An auxiliary interface that allows a copilot to assist in launching and piloting a gravship. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
A compact double-tiered bed designed for gravship use. Maximizes limited space by allowing two crew members to rest in the footprint of one bed.
A set of compact lockers designed for gravship crew quarters. Provides a small comfort bonus to all nearby bunk beds. Placing more than one locker near the same bed has no effect.
A crashlanded, damaged escape pod. Can be hacked open to reveal what's inside.

An interface used to monitor the maintenance status of all gravship components. It draws power directly from the grav engine.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay impact:</color> Reduces maintenance sensitivity of all gravship structures on this gravship by 0.5.
An artificial plant in a sturdy metal pot. It requires no light, water, or air, making it perfect for adding beauty to even the most inhospitable gravship environments.
An advanced computational unit that optimizes data collection during gravship travel. Increases the amount of gravdata gained from each launch.

A small, single-barrel electromagnetic cannon that launches dense uranium slugs at extreme velocities. Designed for both local bombardments and medium-range engagements, it can also target other gravships in orbit. Requires a linked targeting terminal with an operator to function. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
A monumental astrofuel storage tank that supplies fuel for thrusters on a gravship.

A massive, reinforced astrofuel thruster built with advanced alloys and ultra-dense vacstone plating. It massively increases the range and maximum size of the gravship. Thrusters require an unobstructed area free of large buildings and gravship substructure.

Requires vacstone blocks, which can be obtained from orbital asteroids.

A gravship can support up to four giant thrusters.

A massive gravtech reactor that channels and amplifies gravitational wave energy into immense electrical output. Its reinforced coils can handle extreme flux, making it one of the most potent power sources available for gravships. Adjacent gravcore power cells will multiply the power output by 15% for each gravcore power cell.

An advanced ultratech device that dramatically increases the size of a gravship.  It extends the area in which substructure can be built, and increases the amount of substructure that a gravship can support. Must be built near a grav engine. A gravship can support up to four grav field amplifiers.

A specialized thermal sink that draws all residual heat from the grav engine, instantly clearing its cooldown. The absorbed heat leaves the gravheat absorber inoperable for an extended period while it safely dissipates the stored energy. Furthermore, the specialized design provides protection from the heat of solar flares encountered in space, preventing excessive heat from bleeding into the gravship's interior.

The gravhulk is a colossal class of gravship, engineered to transport entire megacolonies, orbital platforms, or mobile city-cores across the skies of a planet. The grav engine generates lift by inverting and intensifying the effect of gravity in a field around itself. This engine can lift upwards, but does not produce forward thrust. It can't function far from a large gravity well like a planet, so it is useless for interplanetary travel.

When launched, anything on substructure that's connected to the grav engine will be transported to a new map.

Gravhulks are the pride of glitterworld infrastructure fleets, often used to deploy prefabricated settlements, floating arcologies, or deep-strata excavation hubs. Their sheer scale demands enormous production facilities and crew specialization, making them one of the rarest and most powerful gravships ever built.

A compact gravtech engine designed for short-range jumps within a planetâ€™s atmosphere. The gravjumper engine creates a brief, localized distortion in gravitational flow, allowing rapid vertical lift, but does not produce forward thrust. It can't function far from a large gravity well like a planet, so it is useless for interplanetary travel.

When launched, anything on substructure that's connected to the grav engine will be transported to a new map.

Often used by explorers, scouts, and smugglers, gravjumper engines are prized for their agility and minimal power requirements. They are simpler to produce than full gravship engines, but still rely on high-grade materials and advanced fabrication.

A complex vertical launch and stabilization platform integrated into the gravshipâ€™s core structure. The gravlift provides the immense thrust required for vertical takeoff from planetary surfaces and stabilizes orbital insertion, allowing the gravship to remain suspended in space. It also softens the crash landing greatly, reducing the damage taken and ensuring substructure does not suffer critical damage.

A miniaturized version of the mech recharger, designed to fit within the confined spaces of a gravship. Lightweight mechanoids can recharge here. During recharge, this recharger produces toxic wastepacks and stores them internally. Haulers must remove the wastepacks from time to time. It draws most of the power directly from the grav engine.

An indestructible advanced recorder integrated into the gravshipâ€™s systems. It greatly increases gravdata yield, captures any overflow that would normally be wasted, and allows stored gravdata to be converted directly into research points. A gravship can support only one black box.
Stores high amounts of electricity for later use. Constructed in the gravship substructure, gravship capacitors do not obstruct movement.

An advanced multi-frequency sensor cluster used by researchers to detect resources underground, across the planet, or in orbit. The scanner can be tuned to target specific minerals underground, or over long range, or set to scan for orbital objects. Detection rates depend on the operator's research ability. Buried resources will require deep drills to extract, while distant mineral deposits and orbital objects must be visited to be collected. Building multiple units does not increase the frequency of orbital detections. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
A reinforced storage shelf designed for use aboard gravships. Items stored in this will not affect room beauty and they won't deteriorate, even if outside.

A miniaturized version of the large mech recharger, designed to fit within the confined spaces of a gravship. Medium, heavy, and ultraheavy mechanoids can recharge here. During recharge, this recharger produces toxic wastepacks and stores them internally. Haulers must remove the wastepacks from time to time. It draws most of the power directly from the grav engine.
A structural beam around which a gravship can be constructed. It extends the area in which substructure can be built in a 5x5 square. If destroyed, it causes adjacent beams to collapse.
An interface used to analyze gravdata gathered from gravship launches and travel. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
A heavy pulse-charged weapon for mechanoid defense.
A rapid-fire gun for point-defense applications.

A low-grade subpersona specialized in gravship combat. It can link with a single gravship artillery piece and control it the same way a crewmember manning a targeting terminal can. It's combat capabilities are beyond that of even the best human gunner. A gravship can support up to two gunner subpersona cores.

A heavy pulse-charged weapon, designed for sustained suppressive fire in defense of mechanoid orbital platforms. Its upgraded capacitors and cooling systems allow it to unleash longer, more devastating bursts than standard charge blasters, saturating entire areas with surprisingly accurate shots.
A hidden, reinforced tube for transporting high-grade astrofuel around. While itâ€™s not visible, it is much more expensive to construct.
A hidden, reinforced tube for moving oxygen around. While itâ€™s not visible, it is much more expensive to construct.

A fast, human-sized combat mechanoid designed for zero-gravity warfare. It drifts effortlessly through space, closing in on the target before unleashing devastating salvos from the twin-linked javelin launcher. While the missiles strike with lethal precision, their short range forces hunters into dangerous proximity with the enemy.
Produces a large amount of power by consuming astrofuel. Much more efficient than chemfuel generators.
A powerful unit that releases large amounts of oxygen into the surrounding room, allowing enclosed areas to repressurize in space. Essential for long stays in orbit.

A compact multi-tube missile platform that launches a volley of six guided astrofuel javelins. Meant for fast, hard-hitting strikes against exposed gravships and unarmored surface forces. Requires a linked targeting terminal with an operator to function. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A large thermal regulator that absorbs heat from gravship travel and slowly converts part of it into usable power while preventing system overheating and decreasing the grav engine cooldown duration. A gravship can support up to six large heatsinks.

A massive astrofuel thruster built by the mechanoid core to propel their orbital platforms. Its construction mirrors human design but is reinforced with mechanical precision and efficiency, lined with thick vacstone plating that shrugs off extreme heat and stress. These thrusters allow mechanoid platforms to maneuver and maintain position in orbit. While unusable for human gravships, destroying one will yield a small supply of precious vacstone blocks.
A large oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
A vital device that processes harvested oxyalgae into breathable oxygen. Converts biomass into gas and pumps it into the oxygen network to supply pressurized rooms and equipment.
A centralized system that optimizes and automates routine upkeep across the gravship. It passively reduces the maintenance decay rate of all connected machinery. Requires power to work.

A triple-barrel electromagnetic artillery system built into mechanoid defense platforms. It fires dense slugs at incredible velocities, tearing through both armored gravships in orbit and ground targets with equal ease. The weapon is fully autonomous, operated by a machine brain that requires no crew. Commonly seen on orbital bombardment platforms.
A mechanoid-mounted portable mass driver artillery. It fires dense slugs at incredible velocities, capable of destroying even heavily armored gravships.
A compact fuel reservoir integrated into mechanoid platforms. It stores refined astrofuel for their systems, but when damaged it is highly unstable and prone to violent explosions.

The cold, artificial heart of a mechanoid orbital platform. Unlike human-built grav engines, these are constructed by the mechanoid core itself, optimized for ruthless efficiency and unending operation. They generate immense lift and stabilization fields that allow entire platforms to remain suspended in orbit, forming the backbone of mechanoid dominance in space. While largely incompatible with human systems and not usable for gravship construction, they can be destroyed to yield a gravcore, a rare and valuable prize for any who dare to challenge them.
A mechanized launch bay that periodically fires drop pods packed with mechanoids. These pods strike distant targets or nearby ships, allowing the platform to project its forces far beyond its hull.

A machine-built structure that sustains mechanoid platforms by breaking down cosmic debris and wreckage into usable resources. It operates at regular intervals, steadily producing metals and fuels needed to keep their orbiting strongholds supplied.
A compact defense unit that projects a localized shield around itself. The barrier activates only for a short time before collapsing, after which the device must cool down before it can deploy again.
A specialized gravship bed equipped with an integrated vitals monitor. Designed for medbays, it improves medical outcomes over normal beds. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A high-grade subcore specialized in gravship combat. It can link with a single gravship artillery piece and control it the same way a crewmember manning a targeting terminal can. Itâ€™s combat capabilities are subpar, but it allows for autonomous artillery batteries without the need for human operators.
A dense mat of harvested aquatic algae, cultivated for use in life support systems. Oxyalgae provides steady oxygen generation but spoils quickly if not processed.
A fast-growing aquatic plant cultivated in hydroponics and used to generate oxygen when converted in algae oxygenator. Rots quickly when harvested, making immediate processing essential.
A life-sustaining gas. Used to pressurize rooms, supply breathable air, or fuel specialized equipment and survival gear. Volatile when damaged.
A hopper-like storage structure meant to input oxygen from canisters into the network, filling up connected tanks.
A hopper-like storage device meant to hold dispensed oxygen in canisters for use later.

A device that draws in breathable oxygen from the surrounding atmosphere and stores it in the oxygen network. It functions only on planetary surfaces with an atmosphere and will not operate in vacuum or low-oxygen environments.
A reinforced tube for moving oxygen around. Can be placed under walls and other buildings.

A self-contained atmospheric unit that extracts trace oxygen from surrounding gases. It produces oxygen at a slow, steady rate, but requires no input materials. Needs to be connected to an oxygen tank via oxygen pipes to work.
Switches oxygen flow on and off.
An interface that allows launching and piloting a gravhulk. It draws power directly from the gravhulk engine.
An interface that allows launching and piloting a gravjumper. It draws power directly from the grav engine.

A specialized defensive turret built exclusively for gravships. It intercepts explosive projectiles and incoming drop pods within its protective radius, but cannot engage ground targets. Interception chance scales with the targetâ€™s velocity.
A mechanoid-mounted, minified, twin-linked rocketswarm launcher. It is very effective against large groups of weaker combatants, but less effective against small numbers of armored targets.

A hardened mass of rapidly-expanding polymer foam. Produced by a sealant popper, it forms an airtight plug that seals breaches in gravship rooms exposed to vacuum. While effective at restoring pressure, it is brittle and easy to destroy.

When it detects vacuum levels above 25%, this safety device pops and releases a fast-expanding sealant goo. The substance quickly solidifies, sealing gaps and breaches to restore pressure in the room. Can also be triggered manually.

A compact thermal regulator that absorbs heat from gravship travel and slowly converts part of it into usable power while preventing system overheating and decreasing the grav engine cooldown duration. A gravship can support up to ten small heatsinks.
An oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
Produces electricity from sunlight. Does not work in the dark or under artificial light. Does not obstruct movement or provide cover, but can only be constructed on the gravship substructure.
A control station dedicated to operating a single gravship artillery piece. Requires a crew member to manually aim and fire the weapon. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
A safety checkpoint indicating the entrance to vacuum environment. Colonists will not cross it without required vacuum resistance.
A wall-mounted variant of the firefoam popper. When it detects fire, it bursts and sprays fire-retardant foam in a circular area around itself. Can also be triggered manually.
A wall-mounted version of an orbital trade beacon, required for orbital trading. You can only sell goods to orbital traders if they're near an orbital trade beacon. Can be placed indoors.
A wall-mounted version of a tool cabinet. Increases work speed when placed near a workbench. One workbench can use up to two tool cabinets.
This device once expanded a gravshipâ€™s build area, but it is now shattered. Some valuable parts can still be reclaimed.
This powerful thruster once pushed gravships across space, but now it lies broken. Even ruined, it holds some useful salvage.
This tank once stored astrofuel for thrusters, but it has been destroyed. Its frame still holds recoverable materials.
This heatsink once cooled the gravshipâ€™s systems, but it has burned out. Some steel can still be recovered from the wreck.
This tank once supplied oxygen to the gravship, but itâ€™s now broken and useless. Some steel can still be salvaged from it.
This thruster once powered the gravship, but it is now wrecked beyond use. Scraps of metal and components can still be taken.
An air-cooling machine that releases cooled air around itself. Can be used to create a walk-in freezer. Cannot be adjusted and will automatically lower temperature slightly below zero.

A gas-powered tall floodlight that lights a massive area. People need light to move and work at full speed. It doesnâ€™t shine bright enough to grow crops. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
A natural source of high-pressure gas. Helixien pumps can be built on top to extract the gas.
A device that converts helixien gas into radiated heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature.
A gas-powered standing lamp that lights an area. People need light to move and work at full speed. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
A fueling port for launching one transport pod. Pod launchers can launch as a group - but the launchers must be placed adjacent to each other. Requires connection to a helixien gas system.
A huge stone crematorium which vaporizes corpses with extremely high temperatures. Operates on helixien gas.
Refines biological matter like wood or plant matter into chemfuel. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network.
Extracts usable metal from slag chunks and other mixed metal items. Operates on helixien gas.
A gas-powered station for smithing non-mechanical weapons and tools.
A gas-powered stove with an attached countertop for preparing meals.
An industrial lamp which lights an area brightly enough to grow crops, but consumes a medium amount of helixien gas. Automatically turns itself off at night.
A large steel tank for gas storage. Will explode if damaged.
A gas-powered wall light that lights an area. People need light to move and work at full speed. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
Produces power by consuming helixien gas. Must be connected to a gas pipe network.
A pipe that transmits helixien gas.
An extractor machine mounted on a pocket of helixien gas. This machine pumps out valuable gas and needs to be connected to other gas-based utilities using the pipe network.
Produces a large amount of power by consuming helixien gas. Must be connected to a gas pipe network.
A pipe that transmits helixien gas. This pipe is hidden from view and is protected from gas explosions. Much more expensive to build than regular pipes.

A legendary palm tree which yields the ambrosia fruit. Ambrosia fruit is delicious and produces a drug-like mood boost. It’s a very rare sight to see an Ambrosia tree that can be farmed for numerous years, as most common sights of Ambrosia are of the bush variety.\n\nIts rarity, beauty, and desirability are why ambrosia was named after the food of the ancient Greek gods.

A dryad caste specialized in absorbing hits during combat. Its awakening has created multiple layers of hardened bark and deadened all pain receptors. Can act as a mobile form of cover.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A dryad caste specialized in food production. Its awakening has accelerated the berry production and instills an instinct to bury parts of its peeling hide that are entirely similar to regular berry bushes.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A dryad caste specialized in carrying items. Its awakening has improved their size, carrying capacity and added functionality as a pack animal for long-distance hauling.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A dryad caste with huge claws that is specialized in aggression and damage-dealing attacks. Its awakening has improved their combat prowess and strengthened the wings, permitting them to ignore terrain movement penalties.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A caste of dryad which specializes in producing a unique shaggy, leather-like material. Its awakening has accelerated the fabric production and lengthens the shagginess, turning it an adorable, living wonder-mop that cleans up messes.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A dryad caste specialized in medicine production. Its awakening has accelerated the herbal production and gives them a large nodule that slowly accumulated neutroamine, which can be milked when full.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A curious dryad caste specialized in ambrosia production. Its awakening has accelerated the ambrosia production and allows it to permanently root itself, transforming into a hardy Ambrosia Palm tree.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A dryad caste specialized in territorialism and can launch ranged acid projectiles. Its awakening has improved their range and adds large acid reservoirs that explode when the dryad dies.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

An extremely easy-going caste of dryad which specializes in finding stone chunks. Its awakening has instilled a sense of awe and strengthened pincers, drawing envy from stone ruins and carving excavated stone chunks into blocks to build a better shell.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.

A slow, bumbling caste of dryad which specializes in wood production. Its awakening has accelerated the wood-like material production and instilled a motherly instinct to create nests made of numerous twigs.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.

A caste of dryad which specializes in producing a unique shaggy, leather-like material. Together with its Gauranlen tree, this dryad can be sheared for a soft, beautiful, insulative material on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.

A curious dryad caste specialized in ambrosia production. Together with its Gauranlen tree, this dryad can generate invigorating ambrosia on an ongoing basis. However, it is completely defenseless and requires careful attention against threats.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.

A dryad caste specialized in territorialism and can launch ranged acid projectiles. Though it can dish out tremendous damage, it is vulnerable in melee due to its oversized mouth.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.

An extremely easy-going caste of dryad which specializes in finding stone chunks. Absorbs minerals from the ground that help harden its carapace while leaving excessively large pieces aside.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.
A small nest of logs and twigs created by the awakened woodmaker dryad. Animals can sleep in it, but it can also be deconstructed for precious building materials.
A fabric produced by dryads that closely resembles thick leather in many of its properties, whilst also staying soft and fluffy to touch.

A spacertech device that separates stabilized alloypacks into steel, plasteel and gold compounds. This process takes a long time and requires a lot of power.\n\nConsider that the building will need free space behind it to produce its output!
A workbench with automated sorters and electric tools, used in recycling wastepacks into other resources. Can work without electricity at 50% of normal speed.

An industrial-tech device that condenses trash using hydraulic press into trashbricks. While it does not neutralize the trash completely, it allows for a creation of a construction resource out of it. The process requires considerable amounts of power and emits tox gas during operation.
A large, groundwater-filtering pump which slowly cleans large swathes of polluted terrain. The extracted pollution is formed into toxic wastepacks which must be hauled away.

A rapidly-working, groundwater-filtering pump which cleans polluted terrain. The extracted pollution is formed into toxic wastepacks which must be hauled away. It requires a large amount of electricity to work, but it works extremely quickly.

A hazardous package of radioactive isotopes, resulting from the recycling of toxic waste. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Radioactive Isotopacks emit high levels of radiation, posing a serious threat to the health of colonists and animals. If damaged or burned, they release a burst of dangerous radiation.

A volatile package of highly reactive chemicals, generated as a byproduct of recycling toxic waste. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Reactive Chempacks are extremely dangerous and can cause devastating explosions if not handled carefully. If damaged or burned, they may explode in a big fireball.

A repurposed package of nutrient-rich bio-material, recovered from toxic waste through advanced recycling processes. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Reclaimed Biopacks improve soil fertility if they deteriorate on standard soil. If damaged or burned, they emit a small amount of disgusting rot stink.
A workbench for recycling wastepacks into other resources. Works at 50% of the speed of an electric recycling workbench.

An amalgamation of strong and lightweight alloys, synthesized from toxic waste through complex chemical reactions. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Stabilized Alloypacks contain high-quality material for crafting advanced weapons, armor, and components. If damaged or burned, they release a small puff of non-toxic smoke.

A package of non-toxic rubbish, carefully sorted and compacted for efficient storage. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Trash poses no risk of pollution or deterioration, making it safe for long-term storage. However, if burned, trash will release a moderate amount of tox gas as a byproduct of combustion.

A stack of solid bricks, created by compressing non-toxic trash into a brick form. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Trashbricks are a durable building material for constructing walls and structures. If damaged or burned, they will release a moderate amount of toxic fumes and pollute the area. Trashbricks do not deteriorate.

An unstable package of highly concentrated toxic waste, created as a byproduct in more complex recycling projects. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Volatile toxpacks are extremely dangerous due to their instability and propensity to emit large amounts of tox gas and pollution. If burned or damaged, they release a massive cloud of deadly tox gas.

A hermetically sealed container that prevents toxic waste stored inside from dissolving and leaking into the environment. Can only hold a limited amount of waste. Does nothing to prevent the waste from burning.

An air conditioning compressor that needs to be connected to an AC control unit via air ducts. Can only be placed outside. When powered, it allows the AC network to operate two additional AC devices without losing efficiency.

A main air conditioning control unit. Connects to compressors to allow AC units to generate cool or hot air. Needs to be connected to compressors by air ducts. AC control unit dictates the desired temperature for all AC units in the same network. Installing too many AC units may decrease the efficiency of the network, increasing the power drain of all devices. Constructing additional compressors will improve the efficiency of the network.

An air-conditioning machine that fits into a wall. Emits either cool or warm air in the same direction, depending on the current temperature of the room. Unless unlinked from the network settings, it will aim to achieve the temperature specified in the AC control unit. Needs to be connected to the AC control unit via air ducts.

A wall-mounted air-conditioning machine. Emits either cool or warm air in the same direction, depending on the current temperature of the room. Unless unlinked from the network settings, it will aim to achieve the temperature specified in the AC control unit. Needs to be connected to the AC control unit via air ducts. Can only be constructed on a wall.
An air duct used by the AC network to transport air between the AC units and compressors.

A large, industrial-type air-cooling machine that fits into a wall. Cool air comes out one side, while hot exhaust comes out the other. Can be used to cool down rooms during summer, or to create a walk-in freezer. It’s capable of cooling the room to much lower temperature than the standard cooler.

A large, industrial-type heater machine that fits into a wall. It converts electricity into heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature. It is capable of heating up the room to a much higher temperature than the standard heaters.

A large air conditioning compressor that needs to be connected to an AC control unit via air ducts. Can only be placed outside. When powered, it allows the AC network to operate six additional AC devices without losing efficiency.
A large, ceiling-mounted fan. Can be used to quickly cool down rooms during the summer. Not efficient enough to refrigerate food, it will aim to reach a room temperature of 17C.
A ceiling-mounted fan. Can be used to slowly cool down rooms during the summer. Not efficient enough to refrigerate food, it will aim to reach a room temperature of 17C.
A free standing air-cooling machine. Cool air comes out of a fan and spreads over surrounding area. Can be used to cool down rooms during summer. Not efficient enough to refrigerate food.
A subterranean air duct used by the AC network to transport air between the AC units and compressors.

A heating machine that fits into a wall. It converts electricity into heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature. Can be used to heat up rooms during winter.

A wall-mounted air-cooling machine that fits into a wall. Cool air comes out one side, while hot exhaust comes out the other. Can be used to cool down rooms during summer, or to create a walk-in freezer. Can only be constructed on a wall.
A wall-mounted device that converts electricity into heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature. Can only be constructed on a wall.
A simple, wall-mounted vent for equalizing the temperature between two rooms without allowing people to walk between them. Can only be constructed on a wall.
Bandolier with ammunition. Decreases cooldown of all ranged weapons by 25%.
A simple roomy backpack. Greatly increases the carry weight of the wearer.
A battle banner. Gives the wearer a fighting spirit and confidence. Increases resistance to pain.
A belt with explosive shells. Will detonate upon user's death.
A bag with everything needed to provide medical treatment. Increases tend quality and surgery success chance.
A simple automatic gun made to be mounted on a shoulder.
A small pack of ammunition and components that control a shoulder mounted mini-turret.
Small quiver for arrows. Increases firing rate of all neolithic and medieval weapons.

A projectile-repulsion device. It will attempt to stop incoming projectiles or shrapnel, but does nothing against melee attacks or heat. It doesn't prevent the wearer from firing out, and shuts down instantly if hit by EMP.
A belt containing a dose of resurrector serum. Injects the serum immediately after death.
A simple tool belt with everything needed to nail a couple of planks or fix electrical appliances.

Small, domestic rabbit that has developed an ability to enhance the neural heat dissipation of anyone they bond with. Unfortunately, as a side effect, it the bonded master of the angora rabbit dies, the rabbit will also die of sadness and loss.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> As long as an Angora Rabbit is bonded to a colonist, this colonist will receive a psychic connection that helps with neural heat dissipation.

A large, long legged bird species, cranes are a symbol of royalty and nobility and often populate royal gardens. It is believed that people of imperial rank will appreciate having plenty of cranes around them.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If you have at least 5 cranes under your control, any pawn with a nobility rank of any kind receives a +5 mood boost.
A fertilized crane egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby crane. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized crane egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized megachicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a megachicken. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized megachicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized peacock egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a peacock. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized peacock egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized pheasant egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a peacock. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized pheasant egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized quail egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a quail. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized quail egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized swan egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby swan. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized swan egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

The unusual royal farm bird, the megachicken is raised by spoiled royalty as a pet project. This bird simply never stops growing, and if taken care of, it can reach gargantuan sizes. Royalty is known to compare their megacocks, and usually the one with the biggest specimen wins. Those that lose often argue that it’s not about the size, it’s about what you do with it.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Megachicken have five growth stages, at 0, 3, 8, 12 and 18 years of age. Their abilities get increasingly powerful as they age.

A great ape invented and patented on Sophiamunda, orangutans are intelligent and can hold a normal conversation with humans. They are often used as home teachers in the Empire, educating young nobles in history, arts and correct etiquette.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> When holding sophisticated conversations with humans, they boost their social recreation. Furthermore, each conversation with an orangutan gives a little bit of XP to a random skill.

Beautiful, majestic royal birds genetically altered to be able to bestow psychic energy onto people they nuzzle.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Peacocks will periodically nuzzle your colonists. This increases Psychic Sensitivity of the nuzzled person by 25% for 12 hours. This stacks up to 8 times, resulting in +200% Psychic sensitivity if nuzzled by 8 peacocks.

Small, noble poultry that was genetically engineered to be an excellent small game animal. When pheasants run away from danger, they emit a psychic invisibility blast, making their allies hidden from sight.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Whenever a pheasant is in danger (it starts fleeing), it emits a psychic shockwave in radius of 6 cells around itself. It only does it once every 24 hours. This shockwave gives all pawns under player control in that radius Psychic Invisibility for a short period of time, making them untargetable by enemies.

Royal poultry bred specifically for human consumption, quail meat is one of a kind, with a taste so succulent it turns any meal into a lavish meal.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Their extremely tasty meat instantly turns any simple or fine meal itâ€™s used in into a lavish meal. It will also turn grills and bakes from Vanilla Cooking expanded into their lavish variations.

Raw meat from a quail, extremely tasty. Instantly turns any simple or fine meal itâ€™s used in into a lavish meal. It will also turn grills and bakes from Vanilla Cooking expanded into their lavish variations.

A large, genetically engineered sabretooth tiger that evolved in captivity. Nobility often buys them for their spoiled children, as in reality, royal tiger is not dangerous at all, and prefers fleeing at the first sight of danger.

One of the largest birds capable of flying, swans are often kept by royalty due to the beautiful nests of flowers they build for themselves. These nests have an extremely high beauty, but a swan will never build more than one.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Swans create their own sleeping spots that have extremely high beauty. These nests are auto-asigned to them as sleeping spots on creation.
A beautiful nest of a swan.
A fertilized hydra egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby hydra. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized hydra egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized toxiguana egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby toxiguana. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized toxiguana egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.

The Hydra is a fearsome creature, mutated by the toxic environment to become a three-headed snake. Living primarily in water, two of its heads remain submerged while only one absorbs oxygen. Quick and vicious in its attacks, the Hydra's bite is lethal, delivering a potent venom with each strike. Its ability to adapt to even the harshest of environments makes it a formidable predator, and one that should be approached with caution. The Hydra's three heads are a symbol of its adaptability and resilience, making it a force to be reckoned with in the polluted biomes.

The Megatardi is a massive and curious creature, mutated from its tiny tardigrade origins to become a behemoth of the wasteland. Though slow and blind, it requires very little sustenance to survive and can consume even the most toxic wastepacks. With its six legs, the Megatardi is an impressive sight, and its gentle nature makes it a popular pack animal among those brave enough to venture into the polluted world. Though it is easily tamed, the Megatardi is not without its dangers. Upon its death, it releases a cloud of toxic gas, making it a potential hazard for anyone nearby. Despite this, the Megatardi remains a valuable asset for those willing to take on the challenges of the polluted wasteland.

The Pestigator is a massive and fearsome predator that reigns supreme in the polluted swamps of the rimworld. With its heavily armored form and powerful jaws, it is capable of inflicting massive damage on any opponent. Though slow on land, it is nimble and nearly invisible in water, making it a formidable adversary in its native habitat. The Pestigator is not to be underestimated, as its powerful bite can rip apart its prey with ease. Its insatiable hunger and aggression make it impossible to tame, making it a constant threat to any unfortunate enough to encounter it.

The Toxafox is a highly intelligent fox that has evolved to survive in the harshest of environments. With a keen sense of smell, it is capable of digging up waste packs buried deep underground, making it a persistent pest. Its presence in an area inadvertently generates more pollution, making it a major environmental hazard. Despite its small size, the Toxafox should not be underestimated. Its cunning and adaptability make it a formidable opponent in a world already wracked by toxicity and decay.

The Toxbear is a monstrous and deadly creature born from a polluted environment. Standing at an immense size, this mutated bear is a force to be reckoned with. With no natural predators, it roams and hunts at will, leaving destruction in its wake. Its aggressive behavior is fueled by cancerous growth around its head, making it nearly impervious to damage.\n\nAs the growth puts pressure on its brain, the Toxbear becomes increasingly violent, eventually turning completely rabid. Not long after that, toxbears are known to try and scratch their own brains out, resulting in their violent demise.
A poor, dark-greenish brown coloured leather that flakes quite easily. Very flammable.
Raw meat from a toxic animal.\n\nChewy, gelatinous and disgusting.

The Toxiguana is a small, two-legged lizard that is well adapted to highly polluted environments. Standing about the size of a dog, this creature is not to be underestimated. It has a formidable defense mechanism, capable of spitting corrosive acid at any predator that threatens it. When it dies, a chemical reaction causes its body to rapidly decompose, releasing a toxic cloud of gas that can be dangerous to any living thing that comes into contact with it. The Toxiguana is a remarkable example of the resilience of life in even the harshest of environments.

The toxlion is a sad reflection of what once was a proud and powerful predator. Its toxic environment has left it weak and emaciated, forcing it to survive by feeding on waste. Despite its diminished state, the Toxlion is still a threat. Its bites and claws have a high chance of spreading the toxflu, and it hunts in large packs to make up for its lack of strength and speed.\n\nToxflu is a highly contagious and rapidly striking disease, characterized by fever, cough, fatigue, and difficulty breathing. It spreads quickly through contact with infected bodily fluids. Those who contract Toxflu can experience severe respiratory distress and, in severe cases, death. It goes away nearly as quickly as it appears, however anyone who interacted socially with a person suffering from toxflu has a chance of catching it.

The Toxscorpion is a massive and fearsome creature, growing as large as a cow. Its thick carapace and powerful claws make it nearly invulnerable, capable of cutting through even metal. With no definite weak spot, the Toxscorpion is a formidable opponent. Mutated from exposure to radiation and toxins, its stinger injects a fast-acting poison that alters the DNA of its victims. This leads to toxic buildup and cancerous growths that must be swiftly removed before they spread, making the Toxscorpion one of the most dangerous creatures in the wasteland.

The Wasteboar is a massive and powerful creature, mutated by the polluted environment it inhabits. With four eyes and two sets of formidable tusks, it is well equipped to defend itself against predators. Despite its intimidating appearance, the Wasteboar is not aggressive. Instead, it spends its time ramming its head into trees in an effort to break them down. While this behavior may seem harmless, it can have devastating consequences for the environment, making the Wasteboar an unwanted and destructive presence. The destruction of resources and the unsightly impact on the surroundings make this creature a problem that should not be taken lightly.

The Wastedeer is a unique creature adapted to thrive in the most polluted of environments. With two heads, it is capable of detecting and avoiding danger from all directions. Its mutated form has allowed it to survive in even the harshest of conditions, making it a symbol of resilience in a world ravaged by toxins. Despite its unusual appearance, the wastedeer is a graceful and nimble creature, easily navigating the treacherous terrain of the wasteland. Though it may not pose a direct threat, its presence serves as a reminder of the dangers of a world overrun by pollution.

A large, mutated, herding herbivore descended from buffalo and adapted for high-toxicity environment. While enraged wastefallo are deadly, they can be tamed and even serve as pack animals.

The mutated secondary head makes them much more deadly, as with 2 sets of eyes and two gigantic jaws they are more than capable of defending themselves from any predators willing to hunt them.

Due to the genetic instability, wastefallo rarely live long enough to be viable farm animals. Their meat is also known to be highly toxic.

The alligator is a large reptile that inhabits freshwater wetlands. They are apex predators and consume fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals.  Like other crocodilians, healthy alligators may continue to grow throughout their lives.
The anaconda is the heaviest and one of the longest known snake species, reaching lengths of 5 meters. They are a primarily nocturnal species that tends to spend most of its life in or around water.
A small canine native to tundra and boreal forests. It's capable of hunting much bigger prey, and is often found in packs, similar to a wolf.

Badgers are small nocturnal mammals with flat, wedge-shaped bodies, broad feet with long claws and coarse hair. Badgers have strong limbs and sharp claws that help them dig burrows and find food underground. They are omnivorous, with a diet consisting largely of earthworms, insects, grubs and small animals such as hedgehogs and rabbits.
A small scent hound developed primarily to hunt hares. It's intelligence and loyalty make it a valuable companion on the hunt.

A large semiaquatic rodent with a flat, paddle-shaped tail and webbed hind feet. Beavers are renowned for their innate construction skills, building their homes, or "lodges", out of sticks, twigs, rocks, and mud in lakes, streams, and tidal river deltas. Beavers are well known for building dams across streams and constructing their lodges in the artificial ponds which form.
Beaver dams or beaver impoundments are dams built by beavers to provide ponds as protection against predators such as coyotes, wolves, and bears, and to provide easy access to food during winter.

A medium sized omnivorous mammal adapted for cold climates. Bears' thick blubber and fur keeps them warm in winter.\n\nWhile their usual diet consists of fish, berries, honey, and scavenged meat, the black bear is capable of using its massive strength and deadly claws to kill live prey. They are startlingly quick for such lumbering creatures.
Due to some insane experiment, these cute, fluffy rodents create a powerful fire-starting explosion when killed.

A large land mammal adapted to arid environments. Domesticated since ancient times, its wool and leather are both exceptionally good at insulating against the desert heat, and its milk is quite nourishing. It can be used as a pack animal, but two humps make it extremely uncomfortable to ride.\n\nBy storing water in its large humps and reclaiming moisture from its breath as it exhales, it can go weeks without drinking.
A slender, fine-boned, medium sized shrubland cat.
A domesticated hybrid of a domestic cat and tropical leopard.

A pedigreed version of a regular british cat, with a distinctively chunky body, dense coat and a very broad face. While being relatively powerful-looking due to the large chest and massive paws, these cats are absolutely harmless.
A largest domesticated cat, it has a distinctive physical appearance and valuable hunting skills. It's a large and sociable cat, hence it has been given a nickname "the gentle giant."
Also known as a sausage cat, this new cat breed has distinctively short legs. Sweet natured, playful and people-oriented, these cats are perfect companions for people with lazy lifestyle.
A cat adapted for very cold climates due to the top coat of glossy, water-shedding hair.
A long haired cat originating from desert wildcats, Persian cats are regal, intelligent and agile, which makes them excellent for hunting small rodents and birds.
A cat with distinctively triangular head, blue eyes and long and muscular body, renowned for it's social nature.
A shrubland cat, sharing the same family as the Abyssinian cat. It has a bushy tail and almond shaped eyes.
A cat known for it's lack of fur, developed due to selective breeding. Being warm to the touch, they lose body temperature easily which makes it seek out heat sources.

These cats are lean and lanky, allowing them to reach incredible speeds. They have tan-colored fur with solid black, round spots, very long legs, a long narrow tail for balance, and a deep chest that contains large lungs.
A smallest dog, just a little bit bigger than a rat. Back in ancient times considered to be reincarnations of different deities and are to this day considered sacred.
Historically used for cattle herding, Corgis quickly became popular on imperial worlds and are recognized as a symbol of royalty.
A praire wolf native to desert and shrubland areas. It's capable of hunting much bigger prey, and is often found in packs, similar to a wolf.

A large semi-aquatic reptile, most often found on the river banks. It's predatory insticts combined with an extremely strong jaw allow them to tackle much larger prey. Older crocodiles can grow to monstrous sizes.

This heavily armored desert-dwelling reptile is known for its slow moving speed and extremely hard, flat domed shell, making it tough to kill. Desert tortoises, despite their small size, are great combat animals and are relatively easy to tame.
A fertilized alligator egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into an alligator. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized alligator egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized anaconda egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into an anaconda. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized anaconda egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized crocodile egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a crocodile. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized crocodile egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized desert tortoise egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby desert tortoise. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized desert tortoise egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized gila monster egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby gila monster. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized desert gila monster egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized penguin egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby penguin. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized penguin egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized platypus egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby platypus. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized platypus egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized rattlesnake egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a rattlesnake. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized rattlesnake egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized wild goose egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby wild gosling. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized wild goose egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

Originally used for bull-baiting, which is an act of pitting one species of animal against bulls, these bulldogs are fierce opponents and due to their small body-size, they are fast, agile and lethal.
A large sized working dog. Easy to train and good at following instructions, they are often seen accompanying glitterworld police forces and rimworld farmers.

A large, slow moving type of lizard found mostly in the desert, known for it's highly venomous bite which inflicts a severe toxic build-up in it's prey. Outlanders to this day believe in tales which state that this lizard is a spawn from hell, and that it's foul breath is lethal.
A giraffe is one of the tallest mammals in the galaxy, using it's long neck to reach leaves and branches unavailable for smaller herbivores.

These ground-dwelling herbivorous apes are the largest living primates, and they are the next closest living relatives to humans after the chimpanzees and bonobos. Like all the great apes, the arms of the gorilla are longer than its legs. Gorillas are usually surprisingly shy, though they may show aggression by beating their chests and charging if their social group is threatened.
One of the largest species of dogs, Great Danes are dignified, elegant and strong.

Hedgehogs are diminutive mammals with cone-shaped faces, short legs and bodies that are covered with porcupine-like quills. When in danger, the hedgehog rolls into a ball so that the quills cover the entire area of their exposed body and protects the animal from predators.
A massive semi-aquatic hoofed mammal with thick skin and a very powerful jaw.\n\nExtremely difficult to tame, these monstrous beings often attack on a failed attempt.
A large dog-like carnivore most often found in the shrublands and deserts. A common trope in tribal legends, the laughter produced by these animals can sow fear during the long desert nights.

The Indian elephant is one of the largest unmodified land animals. In general, Indian elephants are smaller, have smaller ears, but relatively broader skulls and larger trunks than African elephants, and have the highest body point on the head.

A large felid species and the only existing member of the Panthera native to the Americas in old Earth. This spotted cat closely resembles the leopard, but is usually larger and sturdier. The jaguar enjoys swimming and is largely a solitary, opportunistic, stalk-and-ambush predator at the top of the food chain.

Kangaroo is a large, human-sized marsupial with strong legs and powerful arms. It's favoured by tribal hunters due to its tender meat and heat resistant leather. Kangaroos make for powerful adversaries in melee combat, and can be trained easily to become animal super-soldiers.

Koalas are easily recognizable for their stout body and large, round heads with fluffy ears. They typically inhabit arid and temperate woodlands, but can rarely be seen in tropical areas. Favoured by royal families, koalas are great household animals due to their wonderful nuzzling effect.
A thick leather of a hippopotamus. Not only it's extremely durable, but it also provides considerable heat insulation.
A strong, luxurious leather of the leader of the animal kingdom. Often sought after by urbworld philanthropists.
Tanned and dried leather from a Pinniped. It is of average strength but insulates well against heat.
Platypus fur was considered a valuable commodity in the days of old Terra, a fact that almost drove the Platypus to extinction. Luckily, the animal was protected and thrived again.

Tigers were often the target of poachers in old Earth due to their luxurious pelts, a fact that almost drove them to extinction. With the near lawlessness of the Rim, tiger leather is seen as another exotic commodity to trade between settlements.
A thick but crumbly wildebeest fur and leather. Whilst it provides some degree of damage protection, the overall longevity of the item decreases.

A small primate, lemurs are arboreal, and usually nocturnal. They have a pointed snout, large eyes, and a long tail. Although they are social creatures, they have traditionally been regarded as being less intelligent than other primates, since they don't exhibit behaviours of tool use in the wild.

A king of the animal kingdom, a lion is a muscular, deep-chested cat with a short head and a prominent mane. Being the biggest of the cat species, lions are excellent combat animals despite their wildness.

Mandrills are one of the largest of all monkeys. They are shy and reclusive primates that live only in rain forests. Mandrills are extremely colorful, perhaps more so than any other mammal. They are easily identifiable by the blue and red skin on their faces and their brightly hued rumps.

A giant, genetically-engineered scorpion. Once created as a perfect assassination tool, it is not often seen returning to it's natural habitat - desert. A very potent poison in it's stinger helps it in tacking even much bigger prey. A eusocial creature, it cannot reproduce individually.

A giant, genetically-engineered predator with massive paws to rip apart prey. Thick fur allows it to survive the coldest of climates, whilst it strength allows it to hunt even the largest of mammals for food.

The largest and heaviest member of the deer family, well-adapted to life in cold climates. Can easily be identified due to the size of the antlers and long, thin legs. Cannot carry packs, but domesticated moose can be milked. Unlike most other deer species, moose do not form herds and are solitary animals. Although generally slow-moving and sedentary, moose can become aggressive and move quickly if angered or startled.

Musk oxen are massive buffalo-like mammals that roam the tundra areas in search for roots, mosses and lichens to sustain them. Being related to muffalos, musk oxen can be used as pack animals. They are noted for their thick coat and for the strong odor emitted during the seasonal rut by males, from which their name derives.
The inner wool of the muskox, originally known in Inuktitut as Qiviuq. Stronger and warmer than sheep's wool, and softer than cashmere wool.
Long, slim, semi-aquatic mammal dieting mostly on fish and small plants. They are extremaly good swimmers and can hold breath under water for a prolonged amount of time.
An aquatic, flightless bird, only found in arctic regions. Often seen in massive flocks, penguins are fierce when cornered, and can quickly overrun any hunter incompetent enough to get close to them.

Platypus is the sole surviving representative of its animal family. They are beaver-tailed, duck billed mammals, known for the fact that they lay eggs. It is an omnivore animal, capable of killing small rodents with it's venomous ankle spurs.
Large, pampered dogs, historically used for bird hunting, now mostly residing in imperial palaces and royal gardens.
A large rodent with a coat of sharp quills that protect it against predators.

A small dog with physically distinctive features of a wrinkly, short-muzzled face, and curled tail. They are known for being extremely social and friendly, however due to years of genetic alterations they have a very short lifespan. Whatever life they have, they will always cheer up people around them.

A highly venomous snake, recieving it's name from a rattle at the end of their tail. The sound generated sounds as a warning to any predators, however in case it doesn't work, rattlesnake's venom-fangs inflict extremely painful toxin.
Raw fish. A resourceful penguin has fished this.

The red panda is a small mammal, slightly larger than a domestic cat, with a bear-like body and thick russet fur. The belly and limbs are black, and there are white markings on the side of the head and above its small eyes. Red pandas are very skillful and acrobatic animals that predominantly stay in trees. It is a solitary animal, mainly active from dusk to dawn, though they can be quite affectionate if properly tamed.
A large domestic dog and one of the oldest herding breeds. Being one of the most hard working dogs, it is a perfect companions for farmers and cattle herders.

A medium sized semi-aquatic mammal, which only comes ashore to eat, mate and escape from predators. Being an aggresive hunter, seals are capable of tackling much bigger prey and are known for cannibalism if the food is scarce.
An oriental toy dog, Shih-Tzu have extremely long and soft hair. While they are kind of useless, petting them provides people with great comfort.
A large, herbivorous mammal, similar in shape to a pig, with a short nose trunk, tapirs inhabit jungle and forest regions.

The tiger is the largest species among the big cats. It is most recognisable for its dark vertical stripes on reddish-orange fur with a lighter underside. It is an apex predator, territorial and generally solitary. In the wild, tigers mostly feed on large and medium-sized mammals.
A large marine animal only found in the arctic regions of the planet, a walrus has prominent tusks which are a valuable luxury good across the planet.
