A walrus's tusk. It is quite valuable, but somewhat unwieldy as a melee weapon.
A hunting dog, welsh terriers are perfect for small rodents, foxes and badgers. Due to their intelligence they are perfect at way-finding, always able to return home after running into the woods.

Also called Gnu, this wild cow earned it's name with it's menacing horns, pointed beard and large head. These animals are known from their migrations and the size of their herds.\n\nDue to its wildness, it produces milk at much slower rate than a standard cow.

A non-domesticated grey goose. Compared to their domesticated brethren, which have been selected over thousands of years for larger size, wild geese don't lay as many eggs, and they are renowned for their aggressive temperament. Peace was never an option.
A member of the horse family native to arid and desert areas. Whilst it doesn't share the same bodysize as a horse, due to years of evolution it's one of the fastest hooved animals.
Camel's wool. It provides good insulation from heat.

A book detailing different recipes and concepts related to new ingredients and culinary techniques.\n\nOnce read, it will provide a new favourite ingredient or meal to the reader, and then it will be destroyed (why is it destroyed? I see you haven't spent much time around a kitchen...)
A terminal that allows contact with the bank, access to stock market, price overview and contracts.

An uncomfortable piece of fabric which covers the wearer's mouth, preventing them from speaking. Psychologically suppresses the wearer, but effectively prevents them from interacting socially with others.

An uncomfortable jacket with crossed sleeves that restrain the movement of the wearer. It psychologically suppresses them, reducing the chance of a slave rebellion. It also prevents the wearer from doing any harm to themselves and others by removing their ability to move their arms.

A blueprint containing technological secrets. Studying it will provide your colony with full understanding of a given research project.\n\nBlueprints containing too advanced knowledge cannot be read until more research projects are completed.
A paper map containing information about a point of interest. Studying it will unlock a location on the world map.
A perishable newspaper. Reading it will provide colonists with joy.\n\nOut of date newspaper cannot be read and needs to be cleaned up.
A work station with all the necessary equipment to write books. This station is equipped with a typewriter which speeds up the work significantly, but does require connection to power.
An unfinished book of some sorts.
A work station with all the necessary equipment to write books.

Also known as “liquid sunshine” ambrandy is a twice-fermented, distilled liquor of the highest calibre. Glitterworld aristocrats are known to send entire private militias to assist in shipping singular bottles.

Beyond being flavorful enough to bring one to tears, studies have found this liquor when deeply aged causes increased stimulation in the section of the human brain known for artistic creativity and expression while also dampening the section of the brain in control of emotions.

Even the bottle itself is a beauty to behold, when hit with even the tiniest amount of light golden flecks are seen dancing in the liquid within, like embers rising from a campfire. While Ambrandy can be mixed into cocktails, those few who have had the privilege to drink it say it is best on its own.
A gilded distillery of very delicate craftsmanship meant for aging ambrosia must into a drinkable form.
Sliced and pressed ambrosia, can be left alone to ripen into must to be further distilled into an extremely fine liquor.
Fully ripened ambrosia liquified into essentially a thick juice and left alone to grow bacteria for fermenting. Ready to be distilled into a drinkable form.

A somewhat ornate countertop stocked to the brim with different vessels and devices for making a variety of drinks. An ice box, a series of taps, hoses, and carbonators means anybody tending it can brew much quicker than at a rudimentary brewery.

A barely alcoholic beverage meant to be refreshing and fruity. Try as anybody might it is pointless trying to get inebriated by cider alone, thus it is popular among those with prior addiction. Anecdotally, many praise it for supposed antioxidant properties.

A small, rolled paper tube stuffed loosely with tobacco leaves. Offers a brief euphoria as well as mental stimulation, and is very addictive. The pungent odor is generally disliked at first, but becomes much more pleasurable as the smoker becomes more addicted. Can be crafted without equipment, at a crafting spot.

A fat, dried tobacco leaf stuffed to the brim with more tobacco leaves and a gold foil wrap around it. Cigars have a historically posh presentation and are usually only affordable by those who mingle with glitterworld elite regularly. Cigars are often flavored with citrus or vanilla to make their flavor and odor less acrid.

The result of whiskey being aged once more in an unused or lightly used secondary barrel. Flavor profiles are stronger and the afterburn in one's throat notably reduced. The increased aging time for fine whiskey generally portrays a sense of mercantilism and class among its consumers.
A sour and fizzy drink finished off with a hint of bitterness. The combination of sugar and caffeine means a burst of energy upfront, but with a crash later. Notable aftertaste of radiation.
A fine complex cocktail, mixed precisely from multiple ingredients.

A particularly mild and colorless spirit, aromatically speaking this bottle has a pine-tree like scent as well as subtle fruity notes. This liquor has a reputation for being light-hearted and fun to drink and for causing what is known as  “gin drunkenness”, during which one feels increased empathy and understanding.
A clear but cloudy mixture of juiced berries and miscellaneous grain, ready to be fermented.
A fully metal industrial distilling device, meant to purify and ferment gin into a drinkable form. The metallic design means aging is possible in a wide range of temperatures and conditions.

A gelatinized cube of Go-Juice, able to be digested rather than injected. While it can be used in any meal, it is often melted into soups or used as a thickening agent in cocktails. Provides a very diluted version of the drug without risk of addictions.

A veritable miracle of mixology. This cocktails every factor from temperature of glass, choice of stirring instrument and type of garnish has been precisely calculated for the maximum flavor and body possible.
A warm, bitter beverage usually complemented with milk and sugar. A favorite among students and office workers across the galaxy due to high caffeine content and pleasing aroma.

An icy, chilled coffee. Chilling coffee is preferred by many in warmer climates, anecdotally it also smooths out bitter flavors and brings out noticeable, albeit subtle nuttiness. Just like its warm counterpart, it is ubiquitous among students and professionals alike.

Juiced fruit, tart and tangy, can be made from a singular fruit or a mixed variety - Also known as a juice medley. Many drinks can be brewed or mixed using juices as base. While no more nutritious than non juiced fruit it is much easier to store and transport.
A masterpiece of bartending incarnate, this cocktail was mixed from a wide variety of ingredients and chilled to perfection before meticulously shaken and served.

A very finely blended powder made from luciferium. Usually put around rims of alcoholic drinks in bars frequented by the wealthy and powerful. Provides a very diluted version of the drug without risk of addictions.

A low-lying bush of tropical origin, dotted by small reddish berries when fully grown. The pits of these berries are known succinctly as “coffee beans” and can be roasted to bring out a robust and bitter flavor, as well as natural caffeine. This particular genus has been selectively bred for quick maturing compared to its ancient counterpart.

A very leafy shrub that is particularly easy to cultivate. Since early mankind it was discovered the leaves of these plants can be boiled into a variety of earthy and soothing tea. This particular genus has been selectively bred for quick maturing compared to its ancient counterpart.
A short, broad-leafed plant that thrives in warm climates. Tobacco leaves can be dried quite easily to then be rolled and smoked, providing a mild stimulating effect.
The pits of coffee plant berries, known succinctly as “coffee beans”. They can be roasted to bring out a robust and bitter flavor, as well as natural caffeine.
The leaves of the tea plant. They can be boiled into a variety of earthy and soothing beverages.
The leaves of the tobacco plant. Tobacco leaves can be dried quite easily to then be rolled and smoked, providing a mild stimulating effect.
A simple cocktail quickly mixed from a duo of ingredients.
A very sweet and semi-citrusy beverage best enjoyed chilled. Whilst not exactly nutritious the sugar rush is welcomed and the effervescence is quite refreshing.
An all-in-one soda manufacturing unit. Syrup is mixed with water thoroughly, carbonated and then deeply chilled before being dispensed into sterilized bottles.

A thick and disgustingly sweet mixture made by boiling citric acid and natural sugars together - Usually derived straight from cane sugar or as corn syrup. Must be cut with water and left to carbonate briefly in a sealed environment before consumption.

A pleasing, aromatic beverage prepared by steeping a bag of tea leaves in boiling water. Tea can range anywhere from citrusy to bitter to spicy flavor profiles. Tea has been one of humanity's favorite beverages since ancient times and is believed to possess many natural healing properties.

A fermented liquor made from agave sap, a favorite of wild, rowdy banditos and muffalo wranglers alike. Includes a high-protein snack at the bottom of the bottle believed to bring good luck. Imbibers are known to excel at extended combat.
A small still and fermenter combo unit, perfect for storing behind the counter of a warm saloon, but doesnâ€™t fare very well in rain.
A thin mixture of juiced agave sap or prickly cactus pear and insect meat starter, ready to be cut and fermented in a barrel.
Unfermented cider with a starter in an air-tight bottle. If left alone to develop the proper bacteria the end result will be a weak alcoholic beverage.

A clear and mostly flavorless alcohol made from fermented potatoes. While vodka is drinkable alone it is mostly known for being a perfect mixing agent and for causing foolishly high levels of boldness in those who choose to drink it.
A cheap container meant for storing water retrofitted to be air tight for short-term fermenting.
A thick, starchy mash of raw potatoes and a starter, ready to be fermented.

A crushed and powdered form of wake-up, surprisingly sweet and sometimes used as a replacement for sugar in desserts or dissolved into a morning espresso. Provides a very diluted version of the drug without risk of addictions.

A potent liquor distilled from fermented grain. The flavors of the aging barrel tend to permeate this spirit more than others. A woody, almost smokey flavor is pronounced throughout. Simple whiskeys are stereotypically associated with rough manual laborers and hard workers, a common sight in steamworlds across the galaxy.

A huge barrel meant for aging a high volume of whiskey at once. Natural woody flavors are imparted into the mixture as the barrel itself absorbs a portion of the liquid during the fermenting process. Any sunlight hitting the barrel may slow down the fermenting process, or even ruin the whole batch.
A starchy and mostly liquid mixture with pulpy chunks strewn throughout. Ready To be aged.
A particularly bitter tea cut with creamy milk, wakes one up and helps cure ailments.
For a very particular… refined coffee drinker.

A trendy, newer drink in the world of coffee rumored to originate from far off steamworlds. Coffee granules are rapidly mixed with sugar to form a thick, luxuriously sweet foam. Afterwards the foam is dispersed over deeply chilled milk before drinking. Arguably the perfect drink to sip on while studying or reading.
A very strong and semi-sweet fruity tea. Floral notes dance around one's pallet at even the most dull scent. People who finish the drink feel strangely urged to colonize.

An espresso machine brews coffee by forcing pressurized water near boiling point through a "puck" of ground coffee and a filter in order to produce a thick, concentrated coffee called espresso. This particular machine also has the possibility of brewing tea using a separate system.
A light, citrusy green tea spiked with sugar to create a buttery, almost nutty flavor.
Whiskey and coffee blended thoroughly before forming a head and being topped with a spoonful of whipped cream. Perfect for those who just got out of bed and are already wanting to go back to it.

A sugary tea left out to ferment, after fermenting the end product is a sweet and sour, sometimes even naturally fizzy drink known as kombucha. Praised for health benefits, though some remain skeptical. Kombucha acts as a home remedy against the flu, reducing its severity by 5%.
Potent espresso mixed with fresh milk to help ease bitterness before pouring and serving. Lattes have a reputation of being particularly meant for “sophisticated” individuals.
A sweet beverage mixed together with lemon juice, sugar, and water.

A particularly strange combination. Already acidic coffee garnished with a wedge of more acidic lemon, meant to be juiced into the coffee before quickly consumed while hot. An acquired taste to be sure but lauded by those who do prefer it. Specially indicated to combat intestinal parasites.
A dark tea cut with water, chilled and garnished with a wedge of bright lemon.
A tall tree native to both deserts and tropics alike. Bright, sour lemons grow profusely on this tree, some lemons may even mature early and fall to the ground before the primary harvest is ready.
A musty, warm, almost woody tea crafted by simple leaves and complex spices. Masala chai acts as a home remedy against wound infections, reducing the severity of a random infection by 5%.

Bitter espresso mixed with cream or milk, then further mixed with mint oil and garnished. Cool and velvety coffee gives way to an even cooler, soothing aftertaste, not to mention great smelling breath.
Mint leaves are infamous for their cooling effect on one's mouth, as well as ease of seeping into an array of teas or delicately garnishing a dish. Mint leaves rot quite fast.

A plant sprouting into an array of mint leaves. Mint leaves are infamous for their cooling effect on one's mouth, as well as ease of seeping into an array of teas or delicately garnishing a dish. Flourishes indoors and in plant pots, while being pleasant to look at.
Black tea leaves mixed with mint leaves and deeply steeped, then garnished with additional mint leaves. Leaves your breath surprisingly fresh.
Coffee mixed with sweet and luxurious chocolate, very fun to drink. Those of snobbish persuasion would argue mocha is more of a dessert than an actual coffee drink.

An ancient Kashmiri tea mixing tea leaves, a pinch of salt and baking soda, which results in an striking pink beverage that is famous for being not only delicious but also healthy for the body. Used for treating muscle parasites.

Cheap, mass produced generic coffee seeped in an array of spices, oddly excluding any actual pumpkin. Thousands of years ago, this type of coffee was stereotypically consumed by red-haired hipsters with man buns.

A mixture of coffee and tea. Tea and coffee drinkers respectively set their differences aside while concocting this unholy amalgamation. It is usually served cold, but whether it’s served hot or cold it’s equally confusing to the imbiber.

An acid and sour tasting fruit. The juice, zest, and pulp are often used in cooking, often on fish and other meat for better taste. Lemon is also used to flavour drinks, such as lemonade, or mixed with tea and coffee.
Raw anchovy. A small, common forage fish of the family Engraulidae. Most species are found in marine waters, but several will enter brackish water and some are restricted to fresh water.
Raw angelfish. Angelfish refers to several species of fish, both marine and freshwater, usually sporting bright colours and deep, laterally compressed bodies.
Raw anglerfish. Anglerfish are bony fish named for their characteristic mode of predation, in which, a fleshy growth from the fish's head (the esca or illicium) acts as a lure towards other fish.
Raw bass. Bass is a name shared by many species of fish. The term encompasses both freshwater and marine species, all belonging to the large order Perciformes.
Raw catfish. Catfish are a diverse group of ray-finned fish. Named for their prominent barbels, which resemble a cat's whiskers.

Raw clownfish. In the wild, clownfish form symbiotic mutualisms with sea anemones. Depending on species, clownfish are overall yellow, orange, or a reddish or blackish color, and many show white bars or patches.

Raw cod. Cod refers to several large species of fish with a voracious appetite, feeding largely on other fishes and various invertebrates. Cod has long been considered a perfect fish for human consumption, with its flaky white flesh, a high protein content and low fat content.

Raw eel. An eel is any ray-finned fish belonging to the order Anguilliformes. Eels are elongated fish, possessing no pelvic fins, and many species also lacking pectoral fins. Eels swim by generating body waves which travel the length of their bodies.
Raw goldfish. The goldfish (Carassius auratus) is a freshwater fish in the family Cyprinidae of order Cypriniformes. It is one of the most commonly kept aquarium fish.

Raw guppy. The guppy (Poecilia reticulata), also known as millionfish and rainbow fish, is one of the world's most widely distributed tropical fish, and one of the most popular freshwater aquarium fish species.
Raw haddock. The haddock (Melanogrammus aeglefinus) is a saltwater fish from the family Gadidae, the true cods. It is an important food fish and has traditionaly been consumed either fresh or smoked.

Raw halibut. Halibut is a common name principally applied to the two flatfish from the family of right-eye flounders. Less commonly, and in some regions only, other species of flatfish are also referred to as being halibuts.
Raw herring. Herring are forage fish, mostly belonging to the family Clupeidae. Herring often move in large schools around fishing banks and near the coast.
Raw koi. Koi are colored varieties of the Amur carp (Cyprinus rubrofuscus) that are kept for decorative purposes in outdoor koi ponds or water gardens.

Raw lobster. Lobsters are large marine crustaceans. Lobsters have long bodies with muscular tails. Three of their five pairs of legs have claws, including the first pair, which are usually much larger than the others.

Raw mackerel. Mackerel is a common name applied to a number of different species of pelagic fish, mostly from the family Scombridae. They are found in both temperate and tropical seas, mostly living along the coast or offshore in the oceanic environment.
Raw minnow. Minnow is the common name for a number of species of small freshwater fish, belonging to several genera of the family Cyprinidae.

Raw perch. Perch is a common name for fish of the genus Perca, freshwater gamefish belonging to the family Percidae. The perch lend their name to a large order of vertebrates: the Perciformes. Many species of freshwater gamefish more or less resemble perch, but belong to different genera.

Raw piranha. A piranha is a freshwater fish that inhabits rivers, floodplains, lakes and reservoirs. Although often described as extremely predatory and mainly feeding on fish, their dietary habits vary extensively, and they will also take plant material, leading to their classification as omnivorous.

Raw pufferfish. Pufferfish are a family of primarily marine and estuarine fish. The majority of pufferfish species are toxic and some are among the most poisonous vertebrates in the world. In certain species, the internal organs, such as the liver, and sometimes their skin, contain tetrodotoxin and are highly toxic to most animals when eaten; nevertheless, the meat of some species is considered a delicacy when prepared by specially trained chefs.

Raw salmon. Salmon is the common name for several species of ray-finned fish in the family Salmonidae. Typically, salmon are anadromous: they hatch in fresh water, migrate to the ocean, then return to fresh water to reproduce. However, populations of several species are restricted to fresh water through their lives.

Raw sprat. Sprat is the common name applied to a group of forage fish belonging to the genus Sprattus in the family Clupeidae. Like most forage fishes, sprats are highly active, small, oily fish. They travel in large schools with other fish and swim continuously throughout the day.
Raw swordfish. Swordfish, also known as broadbills, are large, highly migratory, predatory fish characterized by a long, flat, pointed bill.

Raw trout. Trout is the common name for a number of species of freshwater fish. Most species of trout live in freshwater lakes and rivers exclusively, while there are others, such as the steelhead, which can spend two or three years at sea before returning to fresh water to spawn (a habit more typical of salmon).

Raw tuna. Tuna are large saltwater fish. An active and agile predator, the tuna has a sleek, streamlined body, and is among the fastest-swimming pelagic fish. Tuna has been highly prized as a food fish. Besides their commercial value as food, the great size, speed, and power they display as apex predators has attracted the admiration of fishermen, writers, and scientists.

An industrial liquid storage tank meant for storing chemfuel delivered via chemfuel pipe network. Tanks can be designated to be siphoned for usable chemfuel as needed.  If damaged even lightly the escaping fumes will very swiftly create a gaseous fireball, the size of the resulting explosion will depend on the amount stored within.

A complex machine capable of refining deepchem delivered via deepchem pipes into usable liquid chemfuel. If no output is detected fuel will automatically be dispensed into cans. If output is connected to a tank or tap via chemfuel pipes the finished product will be transported as such for storage or dispensing.

A heavy industrial sludge storage tank meant for storing deepchem delivered via deepchem pipe network. Tanks can be designated to be siphoned as needed.  If damaged even lightly the escaping fumes will very swiftly create a gaseous fireball, the size of the resulting explosion will depend on the amount stored within.
A hopper-like storage basin meant to input chemfuel into the network, filling up connected tanks.
Steel pipes designed to transport usable chemfuel from deepchem refineries to chemfuel tanks for storage or to chemfuel-consuming structures for automatic refilling.
A hopper-like storage basin meant to hold dispensed chemfuel from tanks periodically for use later.

A volatile liquid chemical sludge, extracted from deep underground pockets. Itâ€™s too thick to be used to fuel engines and rockets, or to transmute into propellant for projectiles, but it can be refined down into standard chemfuel.
A hopper-like storage basin meant to input deepchem into the network, filling up connected tanks.
Heavy steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as deepchem from pumpjacks to refineries or deepchem tanks for storage.

A deep-crust drilling rig for extracting deepchem from deep under the surface. Use a ground penetrating scanner to detect deposits. Deepchem can then be further refined into usable chemfuel. Can be repositioned freely.
A hopper-like storage basin meant to hold dispensed deepchem from tanks periodically for use later.
Subterranean steel pipes designed to transport usable chemfuel from deepchem refineries to chemfuel tanks for storage or to chemfuel-consuming structures for automatic refilling.
Subterranean heavy steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as deepchem from pumpjacks to refineries or deepchem tanks for storage.
A thin and semi-sweet syrup extracted from agave, known for its healing and anti-inflammatory properties. Although healthier than sugar, its high fructose content may be harmful if taken constantly.
An anomalous, leafy vine bearing tiny unpalatable green fruits. When ground finely during cooking the fruits release a complex, peppery flavor, perfect for spicing dishes.
An strange blue cheese created from Bearffalo milk. Very nutritive, but very high in saturated fats.
A delicious cheese extracted from milk created by lactic bees. Yeah, best not to think about it.
Blue cheese is cheese made with cultures of the mold Penicillium, giving it blue spots or veins. This carries a "distinct" smell.
A brown coloured cheese, similar in taste to Cheddar but with roasted nut overtones. Not very nutritive, but very pleasurable to eat.

Boiled eggs vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.

Fish vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.

Cooked and preserved fruit in a hermetically sealed, durable container. Preserved food lasts indefinitely without refrigeration.

Fruit differs from produce in that it is specifically required in the production of desserts.

Canned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.

Meat vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.

Produce vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.
A canning machine is an appliance to store preserved food inside metallic cans. Each canning operation needs a certain amount of Steel.

Cheese is a dairy product, derived from milk and produced in a wide range of flavors, textures and forms by coagulation of the milk protein casein. Cheese is valued for its portability, long life, and high content of fat, protein, calcium, and phosphorus. It is more compact and has a longer shelf life than milk.

A cheese press is a device for pressing whey from curds when making cheese. Pressing influences the cheese's hardness and texture and will also determine the shape of the block or wheel of cheese. This press also works as a ripening chamber, so the longer the cheese is left ripening, the higher its quality will be.
A very bland, almost tasteless cheese. The only good thing about it is that it ripens much faster than other cheeses.
A mixture of cocoa, water and sugar, forming a bitter-sweet pourable syrup, perfect for coating desserts. While still not good for you, chocolate syrup is not quite as bad as straight sugar.
A working table to cut, slice and prepare condiments used in gourmet recipes.
A complex soup assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients.
A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity soup.
A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this soup nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
A simple soup slow cooked from a few ingredients.

Meat tenderized then filleted, then rolled in a thick batter before being submerged in hot oil and fried. The end result is a crunchy, unhealthy yet tasty soul food - Not quite as filling as a meal, but very pleasing to eat. Deep frying meats in this matter reduces shelf life as well unless frozen.

Fish tenderized then filleted, then rolled in a thick batter before being submerged in hot oil and fried. The end result is a crunchy, unhealthy yet tasty soul food - Not quite as filling as a meal, but very pleasing to eat. Deep frying meats in this matter reduces shelf life as well unless frozen

Vegetables julienned then patted dry before being dunked into hot oil until golden brown. As crispy and pleasing as they are, these deep fried treats are not as filling as a normal meal, nor as healthy. Deep frying vegetables in this matter reduces shelf life as well unless frozen.

A deep fryer is a kitchen appliance used for deep frying. Deep frying is a method of cooking by submerging food into oil at high heat, typically between temperatures of 350 Â°F and 375 Â°F (175 Â°C to 190 Â°C).

An extremely high tech glitterworld concoction, unsafe to drink directly due to bitter taste and a mild case of intraventricular hemorrhaging. However, when cooked properly into food, these effects inexplicably seem nullified. Any pawns with this present upon death will immediately be revived as if by miracle.

An electric pot, also called a thermo pot, is a small appliance used for boiling water and maintaining it at a constant temperature. It is typically used to simmer soup over an extended period of time. Ingredients are submerged in water and left to cook over an extended period, allowing the flavors of the ingredients to gradually deepen and blend over time.\n\nSoup on an unpowered electric pot will spoil extremely rapidly. Beware of solar flares!
A complex bake assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients and flour.
A complex dessert assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients.
A complex grilled meat assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.

Spelt flour, ideal for use in baking. Spelt flour takes a very long time to go bad, if stored in a dry environment. It was produced by spelt that has been genetically engineered to gently break into flour with a mere touch.
A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity bake.
A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity dessert.

A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity grilled meat. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.
Gow cheese is greatly appreciated for its smooth, melting texture, and its very soft taste.
A grill is a device that cooks food by applying heat from below. This is a wood fired grill that needs wood as fuel to work.
Why? Why, why, why, WHY? Cheese. With hair. WHY???

A potent, spicy sauce made from blending and simmering chilis and other spices. While particularly strong hot sauces hurt at first, they are known to numb pain and work up a warm sweat shortly after consumption.
A strong smelling cheese reminiscent of Roquefort. Insect cheese is white, tangy, crumbly and slightly moist.
Insect jelly cut with sweetener then sealed as a preserve. Very rich and sweet, while being surprisingly healthy.
A thick sauce made from stewing tomatoes, both salty and tangy in tandem with a sweet aftertaste. Perfect for meaty meals or fried foods.
A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this bake nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this dessert nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this grilled meat nourishes the body, mind, and soul. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.
This lumpy cheese seems to have concentrated protein chunks, tasting like chicken nuggets. Colonists seem to like it.
A thick emulsion of egg, oil, and other herbs. Mayonnaise, while terrible for you, adds a pleasing tang and is very calorically dense.
A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity meal.
A mechanoid cheese created by pressing mechanoid milk. This isn't edible, but if consumed it will act as an incredibly powerful drug, similar to Luciferium, but without addictiveness.
The pitch black rind of this soft cheese gives it a striking contrast with its pale paste. Very nutritive.
Once you get over the thought that this cheese was created from giant rat milk, it isn't so bad at all!
A variety of aromatic and pungent spices, adds several sharp notes on the tongue and puts a bit of a pep in your step.

Brown sugar is a sucrose sugar product with a distinctive brown color due to the presence of sugarcane molasses, and it adds a much desired sweetness. Sugar is easily converted into energy by the body, which may result in a nice burst of energy.

A complex grilled meat assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.

A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity grilled meat. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.

A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this grilled meat nourishes the body, mind, and soul. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.
A simple grilled meat cooked from a few ingredients. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.
Rock salt, like common salt, enhances the flavor profile of other ingredients it is cooked with.
A simple bake quickly cooked from a few ingredients and flour.
A simple dessert quickly made from a few fruits and other ingredients.
A simple grilled meat cooked from a few ingredients. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.
A thickened, fatty substance made by churning milk into very thick cream, combining with smokeleaf oil and allowing it to set. Very unhealthy and hits quite a bit harder than a joint.

A thick, tall, tropical grass with jointed stems. The Uba variety is much hardier but less productive than common sugarcane. While not exactly the most nutritious thing to eat on its own, this genetically modified sugarcane is easily milled into brown sugar and then used for a variety of culinary creations - Particularly desserts.
Thrumffalo Gouda is considered a refined delicacy. It takes a very long time to ripen, but once it is ready it is one of the most nourishing cheeses available on the Rim.
An uncooked fine soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
An uncooked gourmet soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
An uncooked lavish soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
An uncooked simple soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
A foul smelling cheese constantly dripping an oily film. It has a sour, chemical taste, which can either be considered an acquired taste or disgusting.

Spelt is a species of wheat that has been cultivated since approximately 5000 BC. A good source of dietary fiber, protein (which it contains more of than common wheat), and vitamins and minerals. Spelt takes almost an entire growing season to come to fruition. While fragile and time-consuming, this cereal has been genetically engineered to gently break into flour with a mere touch.
An electrically-powered state-of-the-art culinary station, designed for precision cooking and artful meal presentation.

An opulent symphony of flavor and form, where the finest ingredients and masterful technique unite in a transcendent culinary masterpiece. Provides exquisite mood when eaten, and ensures that colonists eating it together will form strong opinions of each other.
A complex stew assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients.
A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this stew nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
A simple stew slow cooked from a few ingredients.

A wood-fired stove with a top side perfectly indented for a large pot. The lack of need for electricity means this cooking apparatus is perfect for the rugged outdoorsman or medieval peasant alike. Stews are heartier and easier to cook than proper soups. Stews often meld into a singular flavor over long periods of time, perfect for gamey or â€œless enticingâ€ meat products.
An uncooked fine stew. This needs to be cooked in a stew pot to be edible.
An uncooked lavish stew. This needs to be cooked in a stew pot to be edible.
An uncooked simple stew. This needs to be cooked in a stew pot to be edible.

A simple chirashizushi bowl made from a few ingredients. In Chirashizushi rice is served in a bowl and topped with a variety of raw fish and vegetable garnishes. It is commonly eaten because it is filling, fast and easy to make.

Sushi is one of the healthiest foods about. So sooner or later someone was going to deep fry it and make it the opposite. The end result is a crunchy, unhealthy yet tasty soul food - Not quite as filling as a meal, but very pleasing to eat. Deep frying sushi reduces shelf life as well unless frozen.

A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this sushi nourishes the body, mind, and soul. Futomaki is a large cylindrical piece, usually with nori on the outside. A typical futomaki is five to six centimetres in diameter. They are often made with two, three, or more fillings that are chosen for their complementary tastes and colors.

A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity sushi. Gunkanmaki is a special type of nigirizushi: an oval, hand-formed clump of sushi rice that has a strip of nori wrapped around its perimeter to form a vessel that is filled with some soft, loose or fine-chopped ingredient that requires the confinement of nori such as roe.

A nondistinct paste mimicking the texture of lobster, crab, or other shellfish. Only, this one... is made with human meat. A delicacy for cannibalistic Rim tribes, and a profoundly disgusting and taboo culinary experience for anyone else.

Complex nigiri sushi assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. Nigirizushi consists of an oblong mound of sushi rice that the chef presses between the palms of the hands to form an oval-shaped ball, and a topping (the neta) draped over the ball.
Simple norimaki sushi made from a few ingredients. Norimaki are a type of sushi wrapped with seaweed.

A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity sushi. In oshizushi, a block-shaped piece is formed using a wooden mold. The chef lines the bottom of the mold with the toppings, covers them with sushi rice, and then presses the lid of the mold down to create a compact, rectilinear block. The block is removed from the mold and then cut into bite-sized pieces.
An edible bean that can be used as a vegetable in a multitude of recipes, and most famously known as the main ingredient in delicious soy sauce.

A plant of the horseradish family. A paste made from its ground rhizomes is used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other foods. It is similar in taste to hot mustard rather than chili peppers in that it stimulates the nose more than the tongue.

A species of legume widely grown for its edible bean, which has numerous uses. Soybean plants grow quite fast, but they are very sensitive to temperature, with optimum growing conditions in mean temperatures of 20 to 30 °C (68 to 86 °F). Temperatures outside that range stunt growth significantly.

A soy fermenter is a cilindrical, metallic drum where soybeans are pressed, mixed with aspergillus bacteria and grain, and aged for long time periods. The result is soy sauce of increasing quality, which can be used as a food condiment.

A fermented paste of soybeans, roasted grain, brine, and Aspergillus bacteria. Soy sauce can be added directly to food, and is used as a dip or salt flavor in cooking, as well as providing a rich umami taste.
A working table to cut, slice and prepare all kinds of sushi.

A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this sushi nourishes the body, mind, and soul. Temaki is a large cone-shaped piece of nori on the outside and the ingredients spilling out the wide end. A typical temaki is about 10 centimetres long, and is eaten with fingers because it is too awkward to pick it up with chopsticks.

Complex uramaki sushi assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. Uramaki is a medium-sized cylindrical piece with two or more fillings, and it differs from other maki because the rice is on the outside and the nori seaweed inside.

A plant of the horseradish family. A paste made from its ground rhizomes is used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other foods. It is similar in taste to hot mustard rather than chili peppers in that it stimulates the nose more than the tongue. Wasabi is widely considered to be one of the most difficult plants to grow, since it needs a temperate, extremely humid environment. It will receive a growth penalty if less than 20 tiles of water are found in a 6 tile radius.

A paste made from ground wasabi rhizomes and used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other foods. It is similar in taste to hot mustard rather than chili peppers in that it stimulates the nose more than the tongue. It loses its flavour and deteriorates extremely fast.
A garden flower with a violetâ€“blue teint, with strongly recurved petals. Bluebells are very beautiful, and last long.
A flower with large petals. Gardenias are very beautiful.
A large cultivated flower with inimitable blue petals. Gentians are very beautiful.
A long lasting flower with purple petals. Petunias are very beautiful.
A chunk of a spacecraft. Can be deconstructed to yield useful resources.
A chunk of a shuttle. Can be deconstructed to yield useful resources.
A smooth, lustrous pearl formed within anima blossoms, faintly humming with psychic resonance. Anima pearls can be consumed to increase psychic sensitivity for some time.
A deep, water filled sinkhole carved into the stone. The surface appears calm. Due to its depth, it can't be filled.
A deep, water filled sinkhole carved into the stone. The surface appears calm. Due to its depth, it can't be filled.
A colorful, calcified colony of tiny marine organisms, forming intricate reef structures teeming with life.

These mushrooms produce volatile chemicals as a defensive measure. They can be harvested for chemfuel. However, this must be done with extreme caution, as the mushrooms can explode if they're destroyed.\n\nGlitterworld researchers have used genetic material from boomshrooms to bioengineer a number of chemfuel-producing animals.

A fungus that gives off hallucinogenic spores. Standing next to them is enough to produce mild hallucinations. Psilocaps can be harvested and ingested to produce a vivid sensory experience that increases the user's mood.
A tall spindly slow-growing mushroom with gills that droop like a willow tree.
A sinister geyser that exhales eerie, ash-like dust, capable of reawakening the dead under its influence.
A heavy, compacted layer of snow that resists movement and muffles sound. Can be mined to remove it.
A sun-bleached fragment of wood, smoothed by waves and time, carried ashore from some distant and unknown origin.
An amber cereal plant that takes almost an entire growing season to come to fruition. This wheat has been subjected to drought conditions for too long and has wilted away.
A spiny, evergreen shrub covered in bright yellow flowers. Its thick, tangled branches provide shelter while resisting wind and harsh conditions.
A low, flowering shrub with dense clusters of tiny blooms. It carpets the landscape with muted colors and weathers wind and exposure with ease.
A massive, foreboding hole that connects the surface with a dark network of underground caves. It is possible to climb down into the caverns below.
A chunk of jadeite. It can be deconstructed to acquire some jade.
A beautiful herbaceous perennial wild plant.
A salt-tolerant tree adapted to life in brackish wetlands. Its tangled roots anchor it in shifting mud and provide shelter for aquatic life.
A stump left behind after a mangrove tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
The remnants of a mangrove tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
Advanced ancient electronics, compacted over time. Can be mined for useful advanced components.
A silicate mineral in the pyroxene family that is one of the two forms of jade.
A chunk of obsidian. It can be deconstructed to acquire some obsidian.

Clumps of vines that burst through the soil to bloom into beautiful flowers. Believed to be part of great collective roots that spread deep under the terrain which occasionally breach the surface to pollinate. The attractive qualities of the flowers encourage humans not to remove them.
Thorny, fast-growing vines infused with faint anima energy. Their barbs are humming softly to the touch.
A short shrub. Does talk, but only when you're not around.
A delicate, softly glowing flower that blooms near anima trees, resonating faintly with psychic energy. Can be harvested to acquire anima pearls.

A withered anima tree whose bark has hardened into a gray, lifeless shell under relentless pollution. Its psychic presence is muted and distorted, yet a stubborn echo of anima still clings to the husk, refusing to fully fade.
Pale, twisting roots shot through with a soft inner glow, drawn from anima-touched soil. Can be harvested for wood.
A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
A tall palm thriving along warm coasts, bearing clusters of sweet, energy-rich fruit. Its deep roots and flexible trunk help it withstand salt spray and coastal winds.
An anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
A shade-loving plant with broad fronds that spreads across damp forest floors and sheltered ground.
A hardy tree adapted to grow on volcanic ground. It is among the first plants to colonize cooled lava, helping life return to the black stone.
A stump left behind after a firewood tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
The remnants of a firewood tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
A twisted, weathered pine shaped by wind and poor soil. Its gnarled branches and sparse needles give it a rugged, enduring appearance.
A grand anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
A hardy, low-growing bush with aromatic needles and dense berries. Its branches twist and spread, enduring harsh winds and exposed conditions.
An evergreen broadleaf tree with dense foliage and aromatic leaves, well adapted to mild, humid climates.
A stump left behind after a laurel tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
The remnants of a laurel tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
A small, vividly colored mushroom with a faintly iridescent cap. Can be harvested for raw fungus.
A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
A hardy coastal tree with spiraled leaves and exposed roots, adapted to wind, salt, and shifting sand.
A stump left behind after a screw pine has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
The remnants of a screw pine destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.

A huge rare tree with warm, skin-smooth bark and long iridescent leaves. Infused with bioluminescent micro-organisms, ancient anima trees develop a unique form of psychic symbiosis with surrounding lifeforms.

If a person (psycaster or not) meditates near an ancient anima tree, it will grow anima grass around its base. Once enough grass is grown, it becomes possible to carry out a psychic linking ritual with the tree and upgrade a person's psychic powers. Only tribal peoples know the secret of this ritual. Tribal psycasters are also able to draw psyfocus from ancient anima trees while meditating to them.

Ancient anima trees' psychic properties are weakened if artificial structures are placed nearby. They refuse to be caged or studied, and must remain part of nature.

Most tribes believe that ancient anima trees are not simply trees, but are rather the physical extremities of a single world spirit.
A temperate-biome tree known for its thin, white, paper-like bark.
A common medium-sized tree, easily identifiable by its distinct three-pointed leaf.
A hardwood tree. Oaks take a long time to grow, but their wood is so strong that 'oak' is used as a metaphor for strength across many cultures.
A softwood tree that grows very fast. Unfortunately, its wood is weak and so it yields less useful material than hardwood trees.
A small anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
A rusted barrel leaking discarded nuclear fuel.
Raw dates. Nice to eat, even when raw. They are sweet, nutrient-rich fruit harvested from date palms. They keep well in warm climates and provide a reliable source of energy.
A foul geyser that intermittently belches clouds of corpse-born miasma, choking the air with the stench of decay.
A sudden collapse in the ground forming a deep, unstable pit, often exposing bare rock beneath the surface.
A smoldering fissure that intermittently exhales thick, acrid smoke, reducing visibility and choking the lungs.
A silicate mineral in the pyroxene family that is one of the two forms of jade.
A naturally-occurring break in the ground from which sulfuric gases escape. The gas produced on these fissures stinks of rotten eggs.
A volatile fissure that periodically spews noxious green gas, poisoning the air and sickening anything that breathes it.

A doorway with a gate easy enough to open so that it can be pushed open by animals. It allows animals to pass, but doesn’t insulate the room. Animal gates are often used as barn entrances in warmer climates.
Divides rooms. Armored doors must be manually opened, which slows people down. Armored doors are nearly entirely blast resistant and barely take damage from breaching methods.
An impassable, heavy wall with multiple layers of armor. Nearly completely resistant to explosives and any breaching tools.

A brick fence made tall brick pillars and a short fence in between them, used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.
A slow moving door that separates two rooms. Cell doors cannot be attacked by prisoners.
An impassable wall consisting of prison bars. Cannot be attacked by prisoners.

A metal fence made of posts joined by chain link sheets used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.
A beautiful, impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
An impassable wall made out of fake hedge. Very flammable.

An invisible fence, for players wanting to create their own pens without fences, or to make it so that rivers or trees act as fence barriers. Does not have any path cost - thus not slowing down pawns wanting to traverse through it.
A wall of horizontally placed wooden logs, for this nice, homely feeling. Capable of holding up a roof.

A fence made of posts joined by crossbars used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.

A fence made of posts joined by vertical wooden boards called pickets, used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.\n \nPicket fences are considerably more beautiful than alternatives, and evoke a nice, homely feeling.
Divides rooms. Simple doors must be manually opened, which slows people down. The amount of slowdown does not depend on what the door is made of.
A human-sized light projecting device, depicting an artwork made using several programming languages. Needs to be plugged in to display the artwork.
An ancient, human-sized mysterious machine. Creates a detailed imagery in the minds of surrounding pawns. Works through walls.
A small carving sculpted from a waist-tall wall piece. Can act as cover.
Plant decorative flowers on this ornamental plant pot to improve the mood of people nearby.
A human-sized light projecting device, depicting an artwork made using several programming languages. Needs to be plugged in to display the artwork.
An impassable, massive monument with an ornate pedestal and a large art piece.
A tall piece of material shaped into an obelisk with an art piece carved into the sides.
An impassable, decorated pillar capable of supporting a roof.
A small plinth with an artistic imagery carved into the sides. Can be used for storing miscellaneous items. Items stored in this will not deteriorate, even if outside.
A small-size pieces of pottery with artisting engravings. Can be crafted on a crafting spot.
An impassable, long piece of wall with an artistic relief carved into it.
A huge floor covering textile that dominates a room, decorated with artistic embroidery.
A large size floor covering textile, decorated with artistic embroidery.
A small size floor covering textile, decorated with artistic embroidery.
A huge piece of material on a decorative pedestal, sculpted into an artistic form.
A person-sized piece of material on a decorative pedestal, sculpted into an artistic form.
A small-size piece of material on a decorative pedestal, sculpted into an artistic form.
An impassable wall with a beautiful wallpaper. Capable of holding up a roof.
An air-cooling machine that fits into a wall. Exhaust cool air without forcing warm air onto another room. Can be used to cool down rooms during summer, or to create a walk-in freezer.
An ergonomically designed double-wide bed to help support the spine, distribute body weight and overall enchance sleep quality. Fits two.
An ergonomically designed bed to help support the spine, distribute body weight and overall enchance sleep quality.
A bed fit for a king, flush with gold-inlaid posters and a high head rest. Extremely comfortable and beautiful, it is a true luxury. Fits two.
A somewhat comfy net and pillow rested on a sturdy frame.
A large slab of smoothed stone, very uncomfortable and only marginally better than sleeping on the floor.
A large bin to help keeping the area around it clean. Slowly removes trash and filth in a 20 Cell radius.
A small bin to help keeping the area around it somewhat clean. Slowly removes trash and filth in a 10 Cell radius.
A tall cabinet that holds a small cathode ray display and control panel for playing a variety of pre-installed games.
A popular game among rimworlds involving throwing darts at a circular target, It's relaxing, and trains shooting skills at a higher rate than horseshoes or hoopstone.
A desktop computer based off an old design, very inefficient and prone to crashing. Gives a minor boost to learning after use.
A high tech computer with utilising an LCD monitor and better performance parts that are less prone to crashing. Gives a boost to learning after use.
A large playable piano for practising on, quite enjoyable and it is a great way to develop artistic talent.
A fabric filled punching bag to let out anger on. Can be quite calming and helps to train melee ability.
A soft-topped bounded table with a wheel for playing roulette, a good way to train your social skills and luck.
A power saving lamp that more effectively converts electricity to light, useful for lighting rooms. Will short circuit in rain.
A tall electrical lamp that lights an area, able to be placed outside without catching fire, making it ideal for road lighting and only turns on at night.
A piece of furniture used for organising documents and records in paper form. It will help you perform research taks faster.
An old device from a bygone era, it is able to pick up radio waves and play them back. Even on the rimworlds, there is often an old transmitter or lone radio station broadcasting something.
A high tech radio reciever that is able to pick up very faint signals and play them back at crystal clear quality.
A simple long platform on which multiple people may sit at the same time, not very comfortable but much better than nothing. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
A simple cushion for sitting on, popular amongst the tribes. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
A flexible ergonomic chair designed to fit the curves of the human body, improving comfort.
A large, soft, luxurious seat. Fitted inside a golden frame for only the wealthiest elites. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
A comfy, padded seat with a high back plate rested inside a gold frame. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
A comfortable and attractive chair not specifically used for dining. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
A small shelf for storing miscellaneous items. Items stored in this will not deteriorate, even if outside.
A heavy duty rack for storing weaponry. Weapons stored in this will not deteriorate, even if outside.

Cooks meals but produces less heat but more light, burns out slower than the standard campfire. As with\n      all heat sources, it must be placed indoors so it has a closed space to heat. Refuelable.

A specially-treated firepit that produces a dim light over a large area. People who prefer darklight work best with this light source. Cooks meals and produces heat, but burns wood in the process. As with all heat sources, it must be placed indoors so it has a closed space to heat. Refuelable.
A smooth, waist high surface for eating off of, commonly found in kitchens and bars.

A small bedside table that has been adorned with a light, making nearby beds more comfortable. Must be placed directly adjacent to the head of the bed. Placing more than one end table near the same bed has no effect.
A luxurious dresser, adorned with solid gold and elegant in design, making nearby beds more comfortable.Placing more than one end table near the same bed has no effect.

A small bedside table that has been adorned with luxury gold fittings, making nearby beds more comfortable. Must be placed directly adjacent to the head of the bed. Placing more than one end table near the same bed has no effect.
A tall wardrobe for storing clothing and other items. Gives a small sleep effectiveness bonus to all nearby beds. Placing more than one wardrobe near the same bed has no effect.
An ornamented burial receptacle for the honored dead animals.
A somewhat comfy net and pillow rested on a sturdy frame that fits two people. Due to the simple construction, quality is not a factor in this bed.
A simple yet beautiful stone slab used for sleeping that fits two people. It offers minimal comfort but is durable and fireproof.

A small table which makes nearby armchairs and couches a little bit more comfortable. Must be placed directly adjacent to the couch or an armchair. Placing more than one coffee table near the same couch has no effect.
An electrical desk lamp that lights a small area. People need light to move and work at full speed.
A low-profile light source embedded into the floor. While not as bright as a standing lamp, it provides subtle, even lighting without obstructing movement. Does not short circuit in rain.
Plant decorative flowers here to improve the mood of people nearby. Designed to be combined with other plant pots to form various shapes.
A reclining cushioned lounger designed for relaxation. Offers superior comfort but is not ideal for deep, restful sleep. Allows for sunbathing recreation.
A compact, cushioned seat designed to be combined with others to create larger couches or corner seating arrangements. Ideal for large rec rooms, lounges, or social areas.
A beautifully crafted standing lamp adorned with gold trim and elegant design.
A grand, gold-trimmed wardrobe designed for nobility. Gives a small comfort bonus to all nearby beds. Placing more than one royal wardrobe near the same bed has no effect.
Soup sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely.

A large, automated machine that combines the functions of a sower and harvester, moving back and forth across a designated outdoor zone to harvest mature plants and sow new crops. It deposits harvested goods into attached factory hoppers or drops them on the ground if hoppers are not present, and its operation can be set to automatically cycle once the majority of plants in the zone are fully grown.

An industrial-grade loom designed for the automatic production of textiles and apparel, it efficiently weaves materials like cloth and leather into various finished goods. It can only output goods of normal quality.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

A colossal industrial furnace built to contain the immense heat and pressure needed to bond base materials into exotic alloys like plasteel.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

An industrial-grade press capable of automatically manufacturing various artillery shells from raw materials.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

An automated industrial machine for assembling complex items like components and advanced components.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

Automatically refines biological matter like wood or plant matter into chemfuel.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. An automated industrial distilling system for the mass production of beer and spirits from raw crops, requiring no manual labor.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

An automated deep-crust drilling rig for extracting minerals from deep under the surface. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption.

An industrial fish breeding and harvesting facility designed to automatically extract fish from bodies of water with fish populations.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. Needs to be constructed in shallow water.

An automated industrial facility designed for the precision manufacturing of various weapons, armor, and complex equipment. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

A series of automated saws and mincers capable of processing corpses into processed mince.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

Extracts usable metal from slag chunks, broken-down weapons, apparel, stone chunks, and mechanoid corpses using strong electromagnets and heat.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

A sensitive micro-processor array designed to enhance the speed and efficiency of industrial factory buildings. It links with nearby factory machines to extend their maximum overclocking limit by an additional 100 percent, allowing for drastically increased production speeds. One factory can use up to three factory boosters.
A simple conveyor belt capable of moving an item from one place to the other. Due to safety reasons, items on the conveyor belt cannot be interacted with.

An industrial oven for the automated mass-production of simple, shelf-stable, and packaged meals.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
Holds resources for use by factory machines.

A cooling system designed to draw excess heat away from industrial factory buildings. It links with nearby factory machines to reduce their total heat output by 25%, mitigating the risk of overheating and fire. One factory can use up to four factory heatsinks.
A mass-produced meal assembled by machine from processed ingredients.

An advanced pharmaceutical processor for the mass-production of basic medical supplies and drug batches from raw materials. It efficiently grinds, synthesizes, and packages items like medicine, psychite drugs, and smokeleaf joints.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.

An advanced chemical facility designed for the automated synthesis of neutroamine from organic feedstocks. It operates by converting rendered animal fat and chemfuel into neutroamine.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
A block of heavily processed minced meat, safe to eat but with an unappetizing taste.
A simple entrance tunnel for the conveyor belt, allowing it to travel a short distance underground. Needs to be linked to a nearby underground conveyor exit to work.
A simple exit tunnel for the conveyor belt, allowing it to travel a short distance underground. Needs to be linked to a nearby underground conveyor entrance to work.
A feeding trough. Food stored in this will not deteriorate, even if outside.
Grows plants quickly in an artificial, sealed ecosystem. Requires a high amount of power to work; plants will die if power is cut.\n\nContains a built-in sun lamp to provide light for the plants.

Grows plants quickly in an artificial, sealed ecosystem which can be configured to effectively grow even picky plants. Requires a high amount of power to work; plants will die if power is cut. Contains a built-in sun lamp to provide light for the plants.\n\nThis version is modular and can be assembled in any shape necessary.

An artificial nutrient bath for growing plants. The nutrients pumps must work continuously; the plants will die if power is cut.\n\nThis version is modular and can be assembled in any shape necessary.
Grows plants in a box of soil. Does not require power to work.
Grows plants in a box of soil. Does not require power to work.\n\nCan be constructed in any desired shape.
A humanoid figure made out of hay, dressed in old clothes to deter animals in a large radius. Small animals will be too afraid to approach.
A water sprinkler, providing an area around it with water. Enhances the growth of surrounding plants.
A medical that uses partial cryptosleep to slow down the degredation of wounds and help the body heal, although with the same side effects of cryptosleep, albeit at a lesser severity.
A crude table cluttered with instruments that supposedly make surgery easier, the cleanliness of the table is questionable though. Slightly improves medical outcomes over normal beds.

An intravenous drip stand for quickly delivering lost fluids to a patient, helping the immune system and blood loss recovery when placed directly adjacent to a hospital bed or surgical table. Only works for hospital beds or surgical tables - normal beds will not benefit. Attaching more than one stand to the same bed will have no effect.
A waist high sterilised surface for performing sensitive research on. Gives a small boost to nearby research benches as well as helping to keep the area clean. Stacks up to 20 times.

A large centre for monitoring the vitals of several patients at once, increasing medical tend quality, surgery success chance and immunity gain speed when placed in the vicinity of a hospital bed or surgical table. Only works for hospital beds or surgical tables - normal beds will not benefit.
An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof. Fitted with special cleanliness-enhancing properties to create a sterile environment.

Stores electricity for later use. Due to technological advancements, this battery unit is significantly more efficient. Charged advanced batteries are waterproof and don't explode when exposed to rain.
This spacertech solar generator is capable of extracting significantly more energy from sunlight. Does not work in the dark or under artificial light.

A spacertech building that produces electricity from a river. Must be placed with its wheel in moving water. If watermills are placed too close together, the turbulence they generate will interfere and reduce power generation.
A spacertech, advanced wind-powered electrical generator. Requires a large clear space in front and behind for optimal air flow.
Produces a large amount of power by consuming chemfuel. Must be periodically refueled by hand.
Produces a large amount of power by consuming wood. Must be periodically loaded with wood fuel by hand.

Stores large amounts of electricity for later use. Due to technological advancements, this battery unit is significantly more efficient. Charged large advanced batteries are waterproof and don't explode when exposed to rain.
Stores large amounts of electricity for later use. Charged large batteries explode when exposed to rain or fire.

Generates a large amount of free energy, forever, without using fuel. This technology is developed by archotechs, and is beyond even most glitterworld societies. While nobody knows exactly how it works, scholars believe it somehow extracts energy directly from fluctuations in the quantum foam.
Produces a small amount of power by consuming chemfuel. Must be periodically refueled by hand. This generator is light enough to be carried around.
Produces a small amount of power by consuming wood. Must be periodically loaded with wood fuel by hand. This generator is light enough to be carried around.
Stores small amount of electricity for later use. Charged batteries explode when exposed to rain or fire.

Generates a very small amount of free energy, forever, without using fuel. This technology is developed by archotechs, and is beyond even most glitterworld societies. While nobody knows exactly how it works, scholars believe it somehow extracts energy directly from fluctuations in the quantum foam.
Produces electricity from moving masses of water, particularly tides. Must be placed on water.
This advanced, spacertech generator produces electricity from geothermal steam geysers. Must be placed on a steam geyser.

This device taps into psychic energy leaving the bodies of surrounding humanoids, changing it directly into usable energy. This technology is developed by archotechs, and is beyond even most glitterworld societies.
A natural source of chemfuel, more common in arid biomes. A chemfuel pump can be installed here to extract the chemfuel. The pond will run out eventually.
An industrial style pump capable of extracting chemfuel from underground pockets. After the chemfuel is fully extracted, chemfuel pump can be safely deconstructed.
Refines biological matter like wood or plant matter into chemfuel. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network.
An air-cooling machine that releases cooled air around itself. Can be used to create a walk-in freezer. Cannot be adjusted and will automatically lower temperature slightly below zero.
A huge stone crematorium which vaporizes corpses with extremely high temperatures. Operates on helixien gas.

A deep-crust drilling excavator for extracting minerals from deep under the surface. Use a ground-penetrating scanner to detect the resources. This drill will yield stone chunks if no resources are present. Can be repositioned freely.

A gas-powered tall floodlight that lights a massive area. People need light to move and work at full speed. It doesnâ€™t shine bright enough to grow crops. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
A device that converts helixien gas into radiated heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature.
A gas-powered standing lamp that lights an area. People need light to move and work at full speed. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
A pipe that transmits helixien gas.
A pipe that transmits helixien gas. This pipe is hidden from view and is protected from gas explosions. Much more expensive to build than regular pipes
A fueling port for launching one transport pod. Pod launchers can launch as a group - but the launchers must be placed adjacent to each other. Requires connection to a helixien gas system.
Extracts usable metal from slag chunks and other mixed metal items. Operates on helixien gas.
A gas-powered station for smithing non-mechanical weapons and tools.
A gas-powered stove with an attached countertop for preparing meals.
An industrial lamp which lights an area brightly enough to grow crops, but consumes a medium amount of helixien gas. Automatically turns itself off at night.
A large steel tank for gas storage. Will explode if damaged.

A wall-mounted vent capable of releasing toxic compounds of helixien gas into the targeted area. It drains helixien gas supply rapidly, and dissipates quickly. It needs a gas valve installed in the same network to limit the flow of gas. Helixien gas is very volatile and will travel with wind.

A gas-powered wall light that emits a dim light over a large area. People who prefer to work in darklight are happy with this light source. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
A gas-powered wall light that lights an area. People need light to move and work at full speed. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
Produces power by consuming helixien gas. Must be connected to a gas pipe network.
A natural source of high-pressure gas. Helixien gas pumps can be built on top to extract the gas.
An extractor machine mounted on helixien gas geyser. Self-powered, this machine pumps out valuable gas and needs to be connected to other gas-based utilities using the pipe network.
Produces a large amount of power by consuming helixien gas. Must be connected to a gas pipe network.
An advanced technology allows this device to harness the power of lightning and place it as a ready-to-use charge in the batteries.

A large nuclear reactor repurposed from a ship power core. Consumes uranium to produce large amounts of power. Can be overloaded, resulting in even larger power output at the cost of local radiation.

Nuclear reactor will quickly overheat and should be cooled at all times.

Cannot be placed outdoors.
A workbench equipped with advanced tools to produce components.
Increases work speed when placed near a drug lab. One workbench links up to two drug cabinets.

A wood-fueled station for smelting. Extracts usable metal from slag chunks and other mixed metal items.
