Increases work speed when placed near a smithy. One workbench links up to two smithy cabinets.
A motorized table with a rotating saw blade for butchering dead creatures quicker into precise slices of raw meat. Consumes a lot of power.
An advanced work bench equipped with containers, heaters, and measurement devices for producing various drugs with more efficiency. Consumes a lot of power.
A workbench equipped with advanced tools and enough space to produce technological marvels from simpler materials in shorter amounts of time. Consumes power.
An electric-powered work center for smithing non-mechanical weapons and tools. Consumes a lot of power.
An advanced work table with electrical saws and holders for cutting stone chunks into usable blocks in shorter duration of time on an assembly line. Consumes a lot of power.
A large size manufacturing stove as well as multiple ovens to prepare meals and cook significantly faster.
A large size manufacturing workbench with several tools for designers and a sewing machine for rapid tailoring of clothes. Can work without electricity at 33% of normal speed.
Increases work speed when placed near a tailoring table. One workbench links up to two tailor cabinets.
An air duct used by the AC network to transport air between the AC units and compressors. These do not transport anything.
Steel pipes. These do not transport anything.
A simple, paintable cloth carpet border. To be placed in the corner.
A simple, paintable cloth carpet border.

A simple, paintable wooden border. To be placed in the corner.
A passable chest-height wall. Cannot support roofs.

A rare, huge flower which can be found in the deepest jungles. Its quite striking colour and nice smell makes it popular in gardening, if it can be extracted from the deep jungle, of course. This one is fake.
Uses alcyonite solar cells to extract electricity from the sun. Inactive.
Uses alcyonite solar cells to extract electricity from the sun. Inactive.
An experimental device that emmanates strange waves making everyone love the truly alien weather. Inactive.

A fake mechanoid power generation unit. It won't output any power.

An ancient ship part, torn off of a craft of unknown origin.
These jelly cocoons keep swarmlings safe from fire, extreme temperatures, and other threats while they undergo a metamorphosis process. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.
A hive of giant insects of the Kemian geneline. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A massive insectoid-made hole connecting to some unseen cave network deep under the surface. The walls are covered in insectoid goop and are too slippery to climb down. Insectoid chittering can be heard from the tunnels beneath. Seems to be empty inside.
A medium-sized insectoid-made hole connecting to some unseen cave network deep under the surface. The walls are covered in insectoid goop and are too slippery to climb down. Seems to be empty inside.
A hive of giant insects of the Nuchadus geneline. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A hive of giant insects of the Xanides geneline. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.
An enclosed, space-efficient growing unit designed for use on gravships. Upon closer inspection, it's just a decal!
A small-sized box containing large fake uranium shells.
A hopper-like storage structure meant to input astrofuel into the network, filling up connected tanks.
Produces power by consuming astrofuel. Much more efficient than chemfuel generators.
Steel pipes. These do not transport anything.

A specialized structure that refines chemfuel into high-grade astrofuel. It must be connected to the astrofuel pipe network and linked to at least one astrofuel tank to operate. Once connected, it automatically converts inserted chemfuel into astrofuel for use in engines and systems.
A hopper-like storage device meant to hold dispensed astrofuel for use later.

A gravtech unit that enhances the precision and stability of astrofuel refinement. When placed next to an astrofuel synthesizer, it enables perfect 1:1 conversion from chemfuel to astrofuel, though the process takes twice as long.
A specialized device that balances and synchronizes energy flow between nearby capacitors. This one is non-functional.
A space-efficient, gravship-powered drug lab designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
A space-efficient, gravship-powered fabrication bench designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
A space-efficient, gravship-powered machining table designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.

A space-efficient, gravship-powered tailor bench designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
An interface used to monitor the maintenance status of all gravship components. It draws power directly from the grav engine. Non-functional.
An advanced computational unit that optimizes data collection during gravship travel. Non-functional.
A monumental astrofuel storage tank that supplies fuel for thrusters on a gravship. Sealed and empty

A massive, reinforced astrofuel thruster built with advanced alloys and ultra-dense vacstone plating. It massively increases the range and maximum size of the gravship. Thrusters require an unobstructed area free of large buildings and gravship substructure.

Requires vacstone blocks, which can be obtained from orbital asteroids.

A gravship can support up to four giant thrusters.
A massive gravtech reactor that channels and amplifies gravitational wave energy into immense electrical output. This one is non-functional.

A specialized thermal sink that draws all residual heat from the grav engine, instantly clearing its cooldown. The absorbed heat leaves the gravheat absorber inoperable for an extended period while it safely dissipates the stored energy. Non-functional.
A complex vertical launch and stabilization platform integrated into the gravship’s core structure. This one is non-functional.
An advanced recorder integrated into the gravship’s systems. The only thing this one would do in an accident is be blown to pieces.
Stores high amounts of electricity for later use. Or it would, if it wasn't so fake!
An interface used to analyze gravdata gathered from gravship launches and travel. Inert.
A low-grade subpersona specialized in gravship combat. This one lacks the brain, so it can post on Reddit.
Produces a large amount of power by consuming astrofuel. Much more efficient than chemfuel generators.

A large thermal regulator that absorbs heat from gravship travel and slowly converts part of it into usable power while preventing system overheating and decreasing the grav engine cooldown duration. Non-functional.
A massive astrofuel thruster built by the mechanoid core to propel their orbital platforms. This one is non-functional.
A large oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
A vital device that processes harvested oxyalgae into breathable oxygen. Converts biomass into gas and pumps it into the oxygen network to supply pressurized rooms and equipment.
A centralized system that optimizes and automates routine upkeep across the gravship. This one is non-functional.
A compact fuel reservoir integrated into mechanoid platforms. Empty and sealed.
The cold, artificial heart of a mechanoid orbital platform. This one seems inert.
A mechanized launch bay that periodically fires drop pods packed with mechanoids. This one is non-functional.
A machine-built structure that sustains mechanoid platforms by breaking down cosmic debris and wreckage into usable resources. This one is non-functional.
A compact defense unit that projects a localized shield around itself. This one is non-functional.
A fast-growing aquatic plant cultivated in hydroponics and used to generate oxygen when converted in algae oxygenator. This one seems to be a plastic plants.

A device that draws in breathable oxygen from the surrounding atmosphere and stores it in the oxygen network. It functions only on planetary surfaces with an atmosphere and will not operate in vacuum or low-oxygen environments.
Steel pipes. These do not transport anything.

A self-contained atmospheric unit that extracts trace oxygen from surrounding gases. It produces oxygen at a slow, steady rate, but requires no input materials. Needs to be connected to an oxygen tank via oxygen pipes to work.

A compact thermal regulator that absorbs heat from gravship travel and slowly converts part of it into usable power while preventing system overheating and decreasing the grav engine cooldown duration. Non-functional.
An oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
Fake solar panelling built out of cardboard. This will probably win a high school science fair or something. We don't have those where I live, so I have only seen them in movies.
A control station dedicated to operating a single gravship artillery piece. Out of order.
A cheap container meant for storing water retrofitted to be air tight for short-term fermenting. This one is empty and non-functional.
An unsettling sculpture made from bioferrite. Inactive and merely decorative.
A warped structure made of jagged oily metallic-like pieces. Inactive and merely decorative.

This is an effecter prop for "Vomit". Intrinsically laggy, use in moderation.

An air conditioning compressor that needs to be connected to an AC control unit via air ducts. Can only be placed outside. When powered, it allows the AC network to operate two additional AC devices without losing efficiency. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A main air conditioning control unit. Connects to compressors to allow AC units to generate cool or hot air. Needs to be connected to compressors by air ducts. AC control unit dictates the desired temperature for all AC units in the same network. Installing too many AC units may decrease the efficiency of the network, increasing the power drain of all devices. Constructing additional compressors will improve the efficiency of the network. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

An air-conditioning machine that fits into a wall. Emits either cool or warm air in the same direction, depending on the current temperature of the room. Unless unlinked from the network settings, it will aim to achieve the temperature specified in the AC control unit. Needs to be connected to the AC control unit via air ducts. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A wall-mounted air-conditioning machine. Emits either cool or warm air in the same direction, depending on the current temperature of the room. Unless unlinked from the network settings, it will aim to achieve the temperature specified in the AC control unit. Needs to be connected to the AC control unit via air ducts. Can only be constructed on a wall. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A large, industrial-type air-cooling machine that fits into a wall. Cool air comes out one side, while hot exhaust comes out the other. Can be used to cool down rooms during summer, or to create a walk-in freezer. It’s capable of cooling the room to much lower temperature than the standard cooler. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A large, industrial-type heater machine that fits into a wall. It converts electricity into heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature. A large air conditioning compressor that needs to be connected to an AC control unit via air ducts. Can only be placed outside. When powered, it allows the AC network to operate six additional AC devices without losing efficiency. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A free standing air-cooling machine. Cool air comes out of a fan and spreads over surrounding area. Can be used to cool down rooms during summer. Not efficient enough to refrigerate food. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.
Switches air flow on/off. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A heating machine that fits into a wall. It converts electricity into heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature. Can be used to heat up rooms during winter. Non-functional: all its internal mechanisms have been removed.

A wall-mounted variant of the firefoam popper. It is empty.

A general-purpose fighter plane, the Warbird is an incredibly fast and effective attack craft. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.

Several dormant and inactive wasp drones.

Wooden stakes placed in a row to build defensive fortifications. Very cheap and quick to build, provides basic cover and deals damage to people trying to traverse them. Breaks after some time.

A specialized defensive turret that intercepts explosive projectiles and incoming drop pods within its protective radius, but cannot engage ground targets. Interception chance scales with the target's velocity. Requires steel to refurbish its barrels after heavy use.

An armored, portable automatic turret. Requires steel to refurbish its barrel after heavy use. Extra armor plating allows the turret to provide some cover, makes it more resistant to enemy fire and prevents explosion in case of severe damage. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.

A manned artillery that launches all kinds of shells. Capable of inflicting devastating damage to both enemy forces and structures, this military machinery excels at laying down fire support for soldiers in the field.\n\nCapable of targeting other areas of the world within a medium radius.

A heavy automatic turret enhanced with 2 barrels. Its large-caliber shells do heavy damage over significant ranges, but its barrels must be refurbished after use. It cannot fire at close-up targets, and may explode when damaged.

An automated mortar that launches high-explosive shells. While it can inflict devastating damage, the mortar's inherent inaccuracy makes it more useful for attacking large fortifications than groups of enemies in the field. Due to a delicate firing mechanism, it's only calibrated to fire high-explosive shells.
An ancient military siege engine in the form of a crossbow mounted on a wooden construct. Bolts fired from this machine explode into splinters on impact.

A stationary charge cannon mounted on a metal barrier. Capable of sustaining fire until the barrel needs changing.

A cutting-edge, pulse-charged, rail-assisted weapon system that fires a short burst of high-velocity projectiles, energized with unstable plasma as they leave the rail. The resulting shots pierce armor and bodies alike, but the turret requires new plasteel barrel after use. It cannot fire at close range, and may explode when damaged.

A heavy railgun cannon usually mounted on spacecraft, utilising an array of advanced sensors which offer top-class accuracy. Requires components to be replaced in order to operate. May explode when damaged.
An automatic turret with a charge pulser gun on top, utilising advanced sensors which offer improved accuracy. Requires plasteel to refurbish its barrel after heavy use. May explode when damaged.

A compact automatic turret equipped with retractable armor plating and a submersion mechanism. It is capable of submerging into the ground, becoming impossible to target. Requires steel to refurbish its barrel after heavy use. May explode when damaged. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.
A cloth dummy holding a wooden rifle. Attracts enemy attention.
An electro-magnetic pulse cannon usually mounted on a spacecraft. Requires uranium rods to be replaced in order to operate. Can only target mechanoids and may explode if damaged.
A portable flamethrower turret. May explode when damaged. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.

A submersible military-grade double barreled automatic turret which acts as a normal floor tile when unpowered. It only takes half as much damage when submerged. Requires steel to refurbish its barrels after heavy use. May explode when damaged. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.
A stationary heavy machine gun supported by some sandbags to grant cover.

A military-grade double barreled automatic turret. Requires steel to refurbish its barrels after heavy use. May explode when damaged. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.

A heavy automatic turret. Its multi-barrel mechanism unleashes sustained fire at high speed, shredding lightly armored targets at mid-range. It may explode when damaged. Requires steel to refurbish its barrels after heavy use.

A turret designed to fire rubber bullets at intruders or unruly colonists. It causes pain and blunt trauma with low chance of fatal injury, making it ideal for non-lethal suppression. May explode when damaged. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.
An apparatus combining an extremely luminous lamp and a mirrored parabolic reflector, useful for lighting up and dazzling targets.

A self-powered turret with a small gatling gun mounted on top. Light enough to be carried and deployed wherever. May explode when damaged. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.

A compact automatic turret fitted with a semi-automatic shotgun. Requires steel to refurbish its barrel after heavy use. May explode when damaged. Its dumb AI brain can't be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.

A cutting-edge utility turret designed to counter the mechanoid advances. Four capacitors are charged to release a bolt of electricity. Upon impact, the power surge stuns affected mechanoid, before seeking to jump to another. Will only target mechanoids.
A self-loading large-bore double cannon designed to be attached to a turret.
A self-loading minigun designed to be attached to a turret.

A powered high-tech bed that automatically readjusts itself to perfectly fit the contours of the sleeper, with built in white noise generation. Sleepers often report feeling refreshed and more alert the morning after.

A powered high-tech double bed that automatically readjusts itself to perfectly fit the contours of both sleepers, with built in white noise generation. Sleepers often report feeling refreshed and more alert the morning after.

A powerful advanced lamp for lighting up rooms effectively whilst utilising an integrated temperature regulation module to keep rooms warmer to a maximum temperature of 15C. Will not short circuit in rain.
A reinforced impassable wall, created by layering materials over eachother. Capable of holding up a roof.
An advanced chair that automatically changes in response to the contours of the sitter, giving optimal back support at all times and vastly improving comfort. The ultimate luxury.
A powered high-tech shelf that utilises mechanites to slowly heal any damage on any clothing, armour or weapons placed on it.
A self sustained lamp for outdoor use, weaker than a normal industrial lamp but does not require any power, storing energy during the day to power itself 24/7. Will not short circuit in rain.

A large sun lamp which lights a large area brightly enough to grow crops as well as providing heat for the area, whilst consuming only a moderate amount of power. Never turns off at night and will not short circuit in rain.
An illuminated dresser, adorned with light strips. Gives a small comfort bonus to all nearby beds, whilst lighting up the room. Placing more than one dresser near the same bed has no effect.

A small illuminated bedside table adorned with light strips which makes nearby beds a little bit more comfortable, whilst lighting the room. Must be placed directly adjacent to the head of the bed. Placing more than one end table near the same bed has no effect.

A table for eating off of with interactive display for watching shows, playing games or taking notes on whilst eating. Fulfills recreation need when a pawn eats at this table and the table is powered.

A table for eating off of with interactive display for watching shows, playing games or taking notes on whilst eating. Fulfills recreation need when a pawn eats at this table and the table is powered.
A huge, curved, ultra-tech television. Viewers feel as if they are part of the action themselves. Very entertaining. Capable of picking up signal from the core worlds.

A compact, high-efficiency air purifier that filters out dust, allergens, smoke, and other unwanted particulates while circulating clean, scented air. Improves room cleanliness, slightly boosts mood, and enhances the overall environment.
A sleek, spacer-era armchair. Built with memory foam, smart-fabric upholstery, and ergonomic design, it provides superior comfort and aesthetics.
A sleek, double-wide spacer-era couch. Memory foam, ergonomic design and smart-fabric upholstery provide maximum comfort.
A sleek, modern wardrobe with an integrated soft-glow lamp. Gives a small comfort and rest effectiveness bonus to all nearby beds. Placing more than one wardrobe near the same bed has no effect.

A high-fidelity audio system designed to enhance the viewing experience of nearby televisions. When placed near a television, it links with the screen to deliver immersive sound, increasing the recreation gain of any colonist watching.

A sleek, high-tech flat-screen television. Its slim profile saves floor space while delivering the same rich color and clarity as traditional flat-screen units. It doesn’t have a huge viewing space, but can be mounted on walls.

Something went terribly wrong. This wretched creature is struggling to keep breathing. Whilst it doesn’t pose any threat and will slowly bleed to death, attacking it can trigger its self-defense instincts.

A payload of alpha animal genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via an alpha genome excavator from:

A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
A very efficient battery that has somehow managed to survive for so long without maintenance, purpose-built to power genome storage rooms.
Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
Whatever these crates contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.

A super-charged air-conditioning unit designed for its intended purpose of preventing genomes from breaking down entropically. Unfortunately, that temperature is also beyond the comfort range of almost all organic life but it can no longer maintain the desired output of approaching absolute zero.
This device has collapsed in on itself, rendering it useless.
The exterior shell has completely fallen apart, yet it seems that it exploded instead of imploded.
This device has collapsed in on itself, rendering it useless.
Most of the useful and otherwise functional parts are missing, indicating intentional sabotage.
Lacking proper maintenance for so long, this machine is rusted shut.
A very old recombinator that somehow possesses an awful stench and excessive mould.
A Genomorpher that has fallen into complete disrepair, some salvage still remains.
Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
The machinery and electronics are irreparable but could be broken down for scrap.

The Animus Hare is a small leporid with latent psionic abilities. Quite intelligent for an animal, but not intelligent enough to properly use psionics. It will however quickly bond with human beings and reach with his mind to make happy everyone that treats it well.\n\nUseful abilities: they will often use a psionic nuzzle on colonists, keeping everyone happier. They also breed very fast.

The archotech version of the Genomorpher, this machine combines the best genetic material of all the animal branches into a single Archocentipede Growthcell. The way it functions is beyond understanding and is impossible to replicate even with the best glitterworld technology.

In order for this machine to function, it requires multiple connecting DNA Storage Banks which each have the complete genetic data from the different animal branches. Each storage bank will be completely drained in order to ensure that the resulting Growthcell is perfect in every single way.

The resulting Archocentipede Growthcell requires its own special Archowomb, as it is impossible to replicate the necessary nurturing environment.

Genomes needed:

A portable, archotech-made casing designed to hold an embryo until it is transferred to a relevant Electrowomb, similar to fertilized eggs that require incubation. This particular one is completely stable and contains the embryo of an Archocentipede, which can only grow in an Archowomb.\n\nCare should be taken before beginning the process, as the resulting energy signature will force any and all hostiles to attack the colony before the monstrosity can be born.

The Archocentipede is the end result of the Archotech Project, aiming to provide a "friendly" machine superintelligence with a capable body. Even though this centipede is forcefully loyal to the colonists, and can be somewhat controlled by them, most other inhabitants of the planet will consider its existence to be an abomination, since dabbling in archotech usually signals the end of comprehensible human life.

Useful abilities: What do you mean useful? You have WON the game. The Archocentipede is basically unkillable by normal means, just use it to lay waste to everything on the planet. You don't have enemies now, you have pets. Ooooor... the archotech may kill all your colonists. One of the two.

Based on concept art for the Nydus Worm by Federico Dallocchio from Blizzard Entertainment at the Starcraft wiki, https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Nydus_worm/Development.

An advanced genome extraction device that is capable of self-arming and self-guiding at the touch of a button. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome. Each device holds up to three charges before it becomes useless.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is guaranteed to be painless and instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body... The device is much better at compressing material and as such can be used on even the largest of organisms.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:

A relatively massive machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb specifically for the Archocentipede. Once the Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released.\n\nCare should be taken before beginning the process, as the resulting energy signature will force any and all hostiles to attack the colony before the monstrosity can be born. The procedure will last 15 days and once finished, the creature will be under the player’s control with peerless combat prowess.

A payload of avian genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary avian hybrids lay eggs. Combat avian hybrids get increased movement speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:

An unusual concoction of traits that was favoured by a particular client who was obsessed on making things explode. Earned the nickname Boom-Boom as they would often make requests along the lines of “two more booms, wasn’t paying attention”.

An ursine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Lacking intelligence compared to a bear while also missing out on some strength and confidence in hunting. However, they can be commanded to detonate a limited amount of fuel sacks for increased combat versatility in exchange for hurting themselves intentionally.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. The "Detonate chemfuel" ability can be used to cause a powerful "Bomb" damage type explosion surrounding the hybrid. Bear in mind (pun intended) that this will damage the hybrid too!

This tardigrade-like creature is basically a huge bed bug infused with bear genes. It consumes a large amount of food, but its paralysing attacks combined with its resilience can be very useful in a combat situation.\n\nUseful abilities: Bear bugs produce a powerful neurotoxin that can paralyse their prey, leaving them completely unable to defend themselves.
Fertilized bear bug egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Actually, it's a beetle egg, it is disgusting either way... If all goes well, it should hatch into a bear bug.
Unfertilized bear bug egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Actually, it's a beetle egg, it is disgusting either way...

Massive, fluffy murder babies is just one of the many “cute” nicknames made for this animal, their only downside being that they’re unable to purr. Because they’re so large, they often end up knocking over the people they’re trying to show affection to before laying on top of them for warmth.

An ursine hybrid with recessive feline genes. Retaining their intelligence and being faster and more fierce in combat, they are adorable killing machines that love to nuzzle. They prefer easier prey and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, you monster.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. The pelt can be sold or used in the art workbench to create rugs.

A surprisingly clean animal dubbed the “owl-bear” by those that find them amusing as they attempt to fly by rapidly flapping their stubby vestigial wings. That is until they get their eyes pecked out and flattened by their weight.

An ursine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Slightly dumber but easier to train, they lay eggs every couple of days and have the strength to protect their clutch. Slower than a bear but their ability to hover over terrain means they perform better in harsh terrain.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Although too heavy to fly, it can use its wings to nullify terrain movement costs.
Fertilized bearchicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized bearchicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
An improvement over the bionic Power Claw, these claws have been vat-grown with a mixture of human and bear DNA, and replace the subject's hand with versatile, non-retractable claws.

A wonderful animal for those cold winter journeys, very popular as travellers snuggle into their fur at night to keep warm. Fights have even broken out over who gets to sleep with the oversized rug.

An ursine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Very filthy but a decently intelligent pack animal that produces good-quality wool and milk. Performs very well in cold environments and is more than capable of defending itself.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks.
Milk from a bearffalo. Quite high in fat content, and very nutritive.

While they’re surprisingly tireless when they have a home to rest at, these manned mares are quite reluctant to hurry along when made to carry loads in a caravan. Their body’s efficiency means they’ll get a task done when it needs to, and not a moment sooner. What makes it worse is that they’re smart enough to know that they’re cute - and powerful - enough to get away with it.

An equine hybrid with recessive ursine genes. The inherent hibernating nature of bears has combined with the high energy of horses to create this particular hybrid that requires little in the way of rest and a much more efficient and versatile digestive system. They are quite a bit slower as a consequence, along with their higher body mass.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
A prosthetic animal leg strengthened with bear muscles, and ending in sharp bear claws that add a powerful melee attack.
An implant that uses bear genes to improve function of the liver. Specially useful for those alcoholic chickens.

A terrifying combination of the raw power of a bear and the intellect of a human, resulting in a creature that loves to abuse its prey. Not quite as strong due to the strange hand-paw shapes, but very energetic and eager to get their victims to catch these hands.

An ursine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very high intellect but comparatively fragile compared to similar hybrids. Their bodies contain oversized and overstimulated adrenal glands that are found just under the surface of the skin, which can be regularly extracted for a substance analogous to Go-Juice.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.

Not many people get to see a naked bear, and fewer still want a repeat viewing. This oversized mole-rat inspires plenty of fear with its massive teeth, but gets a lot of love and affection from those wanting a large, hairless pet that gnaws on the furniture.

An ursine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Very slow both mentally and physically along with dietary restrictions, but very fierce in combat and immune to pain. Also they have the added benefit of having a short reproductive cycle, low metabolism and being completely fearless in hunting prey.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. No pain sensitivity. This hybrid won't be knocked down in combat due to pain.
Grafts of bear muscle tissue on the subject's torso increase overall strength and stamina. These grafts however tend to stimulate the production of an abundance of body hair.

The dinosaur nerds out there should love this hybrid. Got one hell of a nasty bite and even the claws will both knock you down and make you unable to get up. Often bred for hunting by the bored one percenters who want to eat massive drumsticks, caveman style.

A reptile hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Highly intelligent and an exceptional frontline trooper, with a venomous, stunning attack, fast movement speed and high health pool.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.

An absolute unit of an insectoid hybrid, this massive chubster is incredibly smart and hard to kill. All the excess blubber has been replaced with either chitin or muscle fibre, so don’t go making comments about their bulk.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Very thick armour plating and a calm temperament make this hybrid an excellent combat animal. Requires little in the way of retraining and can dish out respectable damage as well.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.

Great care must be taken before considering raising this chonky boy, as they have a metabolism that matches their high ferocity and movement speed. Pretty useful in keeping egg-laying animal populations in check.

An ursine hybrid with recessive canine genes. A very fierce and intelligent combat hybrid with an incredible appetite. As they are almost constantly hungry, they will dig for berries when they’re hungry provided it’s growing season.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks.

An incredibly quick - mentally and physically - hunter with an overactive castoreum gland, effectively allowing them to actively convert meat and trees into vegetables. Technically vegans should be fine with it since, much like bees, it needs to be harvested and the beaverwolf will absolutely let you know if it doesn’t like you touching it.

A rodent hybrid with recessive canine genes. A very quick but cautious predator, this hybrid is both intelligent and frequently looking for its next meal. Able to freely roam and doesn’t require a pen, they are both carnivorous and dendrovorous while also offering daily harvests of castoreum which functions as a vegetable in dishes.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.

A payload of bee genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used only as a primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All bee hybrids hover over terrain.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table from a combination of 10 bees.

A Bear / Bee hybrid. This huge bee hybrid is fiercely agressive and territorial. It will act as a very powerful attack animal with venomous attacks if properly trained.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Boomalope / Bee hybrid. This bee hybrid is covered in chemfuel pustules and it is highly unstable.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The boombee injects chemfuel with its stinger, instead of venom, which is as painful as it sounds. It will also explode in a cloud of bees when it dies, which is incredibly stupid from a scientific standpoint, but it is cool, so meh. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Cat / Bee hybrid. The agile catbee is an affectionate pet that will also sting your enemies to death.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The catbee has a powerful venomous sting that makes its prey blind for a while. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Chicken / Bee hybrid. As it is often the case with avian hybrids, they are not very strong on their own, but when used in high enough numbers they can be a real nuisance.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The chickenbee is an excellent egg layer. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Lizard / Bee hybrid. This bee hybrid is covered in scales, providing it with a thick natural armor.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. Lizardbees have a very potent venom. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Mouse / Bee hybrid. These diminutive bee hybrids reproduce really fast.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The mousebee reproduces with blinding speed. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Muffalo / Bee hybrid. This docile bee hybrid is a versatile pack beast, since it ignores terrain movement costs. Apart from its honeycombs, it also produces additional small amounts of milk and wool.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Megaspider / Bee hybrid. A powerful, armoured bee hybrid that cannot reproduce on its own. It has a dangerous ranged attack.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting, and a ranged sting attack. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Thrumbo / Bee hybrid. This gigantic bee hybrid is very intelligent, and it can easily be the backbone of a colony's defense if trained to do it.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A Wolf / Bee hybrid. A fearsome flying predator. One of the fastest creatures on the Rim, due to its ability to ignore rugged terrain. Perfect for chasing down intruders.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The wolfbee has a powerful venomous sting that paralyzes its prey. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.

A chitinous, floating, hydrogen-filled monstruosity. The beetlefleet wanders the land bouncing off objects in its path, seeking prey to consume.\n\nUseful abilities: Beetlefleet produce insect jelly, explode on death violently, and reproduce asexually.
A metallic capsule filled with some bioluminescent compound. This one seems to still be producing some faint light.

This strange stellated octahedron seems to house an assembly of complex computer chips. It appears to be self-powered, and a faint beeping noise emanates from its core. The radio waves it is constantly emitting are being decoded, but the process seems to have destabilized its power source, and it will blow up soon!

Originally intended as a way to make boomalopes more combat viable, their powerful muscles have instead been focused on compressing its produced chemfuel as a way to store greater amounts of chemfuel without care of how dangerous it is.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Consumes more than twice as much compared to a boomalope but produces deepchem instead. They’re also capable of defending themselves should any predator be foolish enough to go after them.

A biological weapon in compact form, these living, breathing, shitting bombs are ideally assembled into a massive horde and sent squawking at the enemy. Allegedly, this particular hybrid has been brought up in certain court cases as the original creator thought it would be funny to create “lethal egg timers”.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive avian genes. Produces edible eggs very slowly, but can lay a clutch of explosive eggs twice a day. They’re both reasonably intelligent and quick on their feet.
Fertilized Boomachicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Boomachicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

The boomalisk is a boomalope / dunealisk hybrid that produces a weird spider silk know as boomthread. This silk is as strong as synthread, but it is also an excellent thermal insulator. Boomalisks also catch their prey with exploding webs, which... sounds dangerous.\n\nUseful abilities: Produces chemfuel, boomthread and attacks with explosive webs.

A payload of boom genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All boom hybrids explode on death. Farm hybrids produce chemfuel, while combat hybrids can use exploding abilities.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
An implant that uses chemfuel to pump blood through the animal's body. This implant is intended for creatures whose heart is failing catastrophically, since it is less efficient than a natural heart.
These skin nodules can be integrated in a human's torso, causing a huge explosion if the subjects dies violently.
An implant that uses boomalope genes to improve function of the stomach.

Outright refuses to let anyone else milk them for their chemfuel as they view it as a violation of their humanoid rights. They make for a good conversation partner even if their stance can be a little volatile.

A boomalope hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Instead of supplying a regular amount of chemfuel, this hybrid will syphon themselves to top up a limited number of machinery powered by chemfuel, such as generators and pod launchers.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Boomen periodically refuel some machines powered by chemfuel in a 10 tile radius (chemfuel generators and pod launchers).

For lovers of xenomorphs everywhere, this bomb-ass bombadier is ready to launch sacs of acidic chemfuel at your enemies for fun and profit. Don’t expect a second pair of jaws though, that mouth is for spitting only.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Quite temperamental for a hybrid, but they prove to be worth it as they store caustic chemfuel in their sacs which can then be lobbed at a distance. Practically useless in melee, so keep them safe and provide fire support. Can be ordered to detonate auxiliary acid sacs as a defensive measure, but that will also self-harm.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.

Combining the frequent nuzzling of a cat and the deepchem production of a primary boomalope hybrid is surely in no way going to end badly. Better not to ignore them should they complain loudly about wanting dinner.

A boomalope hybrid with recessive feline genes. They make for an interesting companion for those who desire chemfuel production but also want to be able to cuddle said creatures. They can also be butchered to retrieve an internal gland filled with deepchem.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.

People love efficiency and having one thing to cover all their needs. Well this isn’t it, but it gets damn close. The chemfuel sacs have mixed with milk-producing glands, which make for a milk that smells like pure gasoline and could almost power a vehicle.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Has a large appetite due to it producing multiple products, namely deepchem, thin wool and an unusual milk that shares properties with Chemshine.

Inherently less effective at refining chemfuel when allowed to rest at its leisure while also being a good pack animal and riding companion. During distant travels, the long periods of engagement for their muscles actively puts their glands into overdrive. Quite effective should there be a vehicle that uses chemfuel, but don’t ask how that would work out. Maybe it’s the desire to compete against machinery or knowing that if the vehicle breaks down it's gonna have to do all that bloody walking again.

An equine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. While they are comparatively slower, their inherent need to exercise means that their capability in producing chemfuel is severely hampered if their muscles aren’t engaged for long periods of time. When travelling as part of a caravan, their chemfuel production speed is effectively quadrupled.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.

The Boomnotherium is an hybrid between a boomalope and a large prehistoric relative of modern-day elephants. In latin, boomnotherium roughly translates as "terrible beast that will explode in your ass".\n\nUseful abilities: Boomnotheriums produce huge amounts of chemfuel, and they are "tanky", able to withstand a lot of abuse before exploding.

What happens when you mix a spitting cobra, a rattlesnake and a creature designed to lactate refined fuel? This abomination, that’s what. Drive it into a corner and it’ll hurt itself to release deadly neurotoxins. Try and keep your distance and it’ll spit toxic blood right at you. Clearly someone was a sore loser in playing pretend games as a child, but had either the brains or the money to prove themselves right.

A reptile hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Decently intelligent with a nasty bite and even nastier ranged attack, both causing toxic buildup. Quite a high metabolism which supports its various combat abilities, best suited for home defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.

With adorable horns and long snoots, these squeaky lads are ideal in giving your enemy a stunning surprise. Their chemfuel mixture includes other gases that create a blinding and disorienting flashbang when remotely detonated. They’ll still make a regular boom if killed though.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Quite fast and intelligent, these hybrids work as excellent swarm attackers. Their rapid reproduction cycle and stunning explosion make for an ideal companion to ranged masters.
This silk is as strong as synthread, but it is also an excellent thermal insulator

For those who wished they could equip their canines with explosive vests, the Boomwolf stores pressurised - almost solidified - chemfuel which can be remotely discharged as a non-explosive gas. Thanks to thermodynamics, it is extremely cold upon release and can just as easily hurt the hybrid as well as everything nearby.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive canine genes. Very intelligent, fast and dangerous, they make for an interesting combat animal. One of the few canine-type hybrids that work better solo than as a pack.

Self-assembling machinery that fuses with the specimen’s nerve centers during the growth process. Effectively allows hybrids to be drafted but increases the chance of failure due to introduction of invasive foreign material.

Compressed medicine that allows hybrids to develop functional sexual organs. Effectively allows hybrids to reproduce with the exact same species.\n\nNote: Insectoid hybrids are always incapable of reproducing.
Capsules containing genetic stabilizers that help maintain the dominant-recessive genome relationship. Effectively reduces the chance of the hybrid having the opposite intended result.

Capsules containing genetic stabilizers that help maintain the dominant-recessive genome relationship. Greatly reduces the chance of the hybrid having the opposite intended result, at the expense of more processing time.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
Capsules containing genetic restorative medicine that helps prevent genomes from breaking down prematurely. Effectively helps mitigate the possibility of the hybrid ending in complete failure.

Capsules containing genetic restorative medicine that helps prevent genomes from breaking down prematurely. Greatly helps mitigate the possibility of the hybrid ending in complete failure, at the expense of more processing time.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.

Compressed medicine that slows the growth of the specimen, increasing both stability and safety. Effectively increases growth time by 50% in exchange for a vastly increased chance of obtaining the intended result.
Capsules containing growth hormones that help speed up the hybrid’s growth process, halving the time it takes to complete.
This huge leporid ear gives amazing enhanced hearing. Also, super kawaii!
An eye implant that uses genetic splicing with canid genes to give an old (or damaged) eye decent vision. This implant is not meant to replace healthy eyes.

A payload of canine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary canine hybrids have the adrenaline rush ability. All canine hybrids have fast attack speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
Castoreum is a yellowish exudate from the castor sacs of beavers. It is historically used as a food additive and natural flavour, often as a substitute vanilla flavour.

Have you ever wanted to have a cat that could start fires with hairballs? No? Too bad, this one does and if you’re unlucky it’ll be outside when it’s hunting. Which won’t be often, because those fuel sacs make for a surprisingly efficient energy reserve. Exploding kittens is no longer just a game, it’s a hazard warning.

A feline hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Intelligent, low maintenance with a weak ranged flame attack, they make for an unusual companion. 

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight.

The perfect companion in compact form, this tiny terror is willing to throw down with its surprising bulk. And good lord, what an affectionate beefcake.

A feline hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Can both take and dish out punishment disproportionate to its size while being clever enough to actively hunt for prey not much larger than itself. Incredibly easy to train and will intermittently dig up burrowed rats.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Stunning attacks like bear hybrids.

This smart little shit knows where to kick so it hurts, and that’s often the eyes. The unusual genetic makeup has made their claws as long as a human thumb, so best to pet it when it wants to and avoid touching the belly.

A feline hybrid with recessive avian genes. A little slow for its size, it makes for a vicious attack animal if it can be permitted to get in melee range. Be careful as it can’t take much punishment.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Go for the eyes: Catchicken attacks have a 25% chance to affect the victim's eyes.
This hybrid humanoid / feline eye provides improved night vision, at the expense of a certain short-sightedness outside and during the day. Ideal for nyctophile colonists.

Someone couldn’t bear being separated from their trusty feline companions when away on trips. So they decided to make one that everyone would want to take with them on long journeys. Also they look incredibly adorable with their little baggies.

A feline hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. While not great as a pack animal, they make for a fantastic caravaning companion as they will actively forage for prey during trips. This creates a stacking effect of boosting the amount of food foraged for every other catffalo in the trip. They’re quite hardy too but should be kept away from combat.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Forages for small prey while caravaning, increasing the amount of foraged food by 10% (and it stacks per catffalo on the caravan!).

Tell me, do you want a cat large enough to ride at speeds that would be illegal on most urbworld city streets? Of course you do, and if you say otherwise you’re a coward. This ferocious feline is ready to tear up the track and hunt prey comically smaller than itself. They’re also incredibly smart and have a tendency to stalk their owners when their backs are turned. Don’t worry though, it only wants to pin you down and drown you in fur and saliva.

An equine hybrid with recessive feline genes. Quite durable and combat capable for a pack animal, they can be drafted to execute at-will pouncing to close distance with the enemy. They are only slightly less effective as horses in riding but have better stamina, in exchange they are pure carnivores so their diet can be rather restrictive.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, making them perfect for the front lines, and a short nuzzle period. Always starts as draftable, even if not using a controller booster to create it. Pounce ability: when drafted, the player can use the pounce ability to make the hybrid jump around. The ability can be triggered every 2 hours.

You seriously have to wonder who comes up with ideas to play with things beyond their understanding. Have you ever had an octopus attach itself to your face and try to climb down your throat? Well now you can find out, with an extra helping of teeth, claws and furballs.

A feline hybrid with recessive Eldritch genes. Comparable to a regular big cat, such as a panther, however its bite not only causes blindness but also inflicts bouts of insanity from the lingering touch of the tentacles. Oddly enough it also enjoys fondling its master with those same tentacles, notably without its teeth out. Oh, did I not mention the fact it has teeth along those tentacles?

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Its bites cause suffocation and sanity loss.

Just the sweetest little kitty cat you’ll ever encounter, they were bred as tiny caretakers to look after people in hospital wards. Despite being a health violation, they were extremely popular with the infirms as they not only fed patients but also engaged in conversation and gave friendly headbutts. It is also alleged that their purring helped broken bones heal quicker, but no conclusive evidence has been shown.

A feline hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Quite slow and fragile, comparatively speaking, with a low damage output and lower hunger rate. However, they make for very good bedside companions for the ill and will regularly feed those that cannot feed themselves.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Catmen are compassionate animals that will help with feeding medical patients around the colony.

Everyone can agree that cats and rabbits have incredibly soft fur, so can you imagine how cute and fluffy they would be if you mixed their traits together? They also love to be pet multiple times a day, so everyone can get in on this pet so fluffy it's to die for.

A feline hybrid with recessive rodent genes. While a somewhat independent carnivore, this hybrid is ideal inside a colony as it not only provides a  non-stacking mood boost to those nearby from its cuteness, but it also nuzzles every few hours. They’re also very fast breeders.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Gives non-stacking +3 mood to everyone in a 8 tiles radius.

A tail implant that uses genetic splicing with feline genes to substitute a broken or missing tail. This implant is not meant to replace a healthy tail. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.

For some reason people really want cats to be able to carry stuff, depicted often in consumer media. Well this palico should have you sorted, however despite numerous efforts it has proved impossible to train them in using brooms.

A feline hybrid with recessive canine genes. An incredibly fast hauling companion that can also defend itself, these creatures borne forth from pop culture are incredibly smart and incredibly clean. They can’t carry much, but having a whole army won’t cause your base to fill with filth, just the normal degenerates.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Produces (and spreads) absolutely no filth.
A reinforced bionic heart that acts like a miniature 4-stroke internal combustion engine powered by chemfuel to pump blood through the body.
A reinforced bionic stomach that uses a combination of gastric acid and chemfuel to efficiently digest food.

For something so small, this animal has a hell of an appetite. Lacking in many departments, but the nutrition has to go somewhere and that’s for those looking for a non-vegan friendly option of getting high. The person who thought it would be a good idea to inhale the burning feathers of this plump creature is one stoners everywhere praise while they blaze.

An avian hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Has a considerably larger appetite and inherent wildness, but reproduces quickly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Can be regularly plucked for chickenbear feathers. When these feathers are turned into powder at a Drug lab, they can be rolled into a joint to be smoked.
Fertilized Chickenbear egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Chickenbear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
Chickenbear feathers can be gathered and processed into a fine powder called featherdust, which produces a mild euphoric sensation when burned and inhaled.
An implant that uses chicken brain tissue to patch damaged neurological pathways. This implant is intended for brain-dead creatures.

Respectably intelligent as well as lethal, these egg-laying, plump beasties love nuzzling their owners and biting their enemies. Sometimes those are different people.

An avian hybrid with recessive feline genes. Quite clean with a low gestation period, they make for an average farm animal and an average combatant with good speed. They still require a pen, so they make for good home defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.
Fertilized Chickencat egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Chickencat egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

You wouldn’t think something so small could eat so much, but that’s the price you pay for the sake of convenience. That convenience is sporadic, low in nutrition and very thin, an effect commonly described as “nerfing”.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Loves to wander so it must be securely penned, but they make for a surprising pack beast. Capable of producing the holy trinity of milk, eggs and wool, however they can be quite lacking in substance.
Fertilized Chickenffalo egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Chickenffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
Milk from a chickenffalo. Looks like watered down milk. Probably not much better than water.

An agile avian that most people expect to be more yellow in colour and go “wark” every now and then. A delightfully speedy mount that can pull off some impressive turns, this hybrid has been bred even further with other species to make for more colourful and genetically stable bird mounts. Some speculate that this particular creature is the original ancestor of the Ave, while the Royal Ave came from those that were bred more for plumage than performance.

An equine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Comparatively smaller than a purebred, but incredibly fast both in a singular location and as part of a caravan. A rather unusual hybrid in that it reproduces very slowly in a safe, similar environment, however this changes on caravan trips. Constant new sights and experiences puts their reproductive cycle into overdrive as it tries to spread its species over a broad horizon, however these eggs are genetically sterile so that they instead provide a healthy protein supplement for those who can only gather fruits and vegetables.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
Fertilized chickenhorse egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized chickenhorse egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
An implant that uses chicken genes to improve function of the kidney. Birds have large kidneys. Yep, you learned that today.

Chickens are basically an evolution of dinosaurs, so someone decided to turn them back. Unfortunately they didn’t exactly bother to check that they should still be avian rather than reptilian, so it resulted in this nasty little thing that reproduces at an insane rate and empties your fridge. But hey, they’ll spit on your enemies and their right to shit everywhere, especially where they eat.

A reptile hybrid with recessive avian genes. This particular farm animal is smart enough that it doesn’t need a pen and breeds faster than other reptiles. A very large appetite for a creature so small, which supports its ability to spit out nasty toxins on their enemies and create messes on the floor. Very easy to train and has a good memory.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
Fertilized chickenlizard egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized chickenlizard egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
The chickenlodon is a very weird cross between one of the most fearsome predators ever existing... and a chicken. A saber-toothed chicken, if you will.
Fertilized chickenlodon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickenlodon.
Unfertilized chickenlodon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

The ideal companion for those who love the smell of fried eggs and napalm in the morning. Regretfully they’re far too birdbrained to receive any direction, and most owners have been noted saying “I’ve made a huge mistake”.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Very dangerous if allowed to breed uncontrollably, so consider liberal fireproofing and selling the excess. Produces chemfuel daily as well as an egg every 2 days.
Fertilized Chickenlope egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Chickenlope egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

It is often said that birds are little more than evolved dinosaurs. Well, this small theropod hybrid is both a dinosaur AND a bird. Take that, science!\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Chickennathus are great egg layers.
Fertilized chickennathus egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickennathus.
Unfertilized chickennathus egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

One of the few unusual animal hybrids that come with their own warning labels despite being almost entirely harmless. All it took was one absent-minded vegan to cause a single couple of “Breeders” to overpopulate and devastate the ecology of an entire continent. Fairy tales surrounding the fluffy terror have done nothing to help it be taken seriously.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive rodent genes. An incredibly low metabolism coupled with an incredibly high reproduction rate, these animals are very sensitive to temperature and are unusually keen on escaping to the wilds.

Fertilized Chickenrabbit egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked. The colonists swear it was coloured like this when the animal laid it.
Unfertilized Chickenrabbit egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The colonists swear it was coloured like this when the animal laid it.

For the unusual connoisseur who both enjoys a healthy breakfast and the feel of silk pyjamas. A very niche market, but considering the amount of money paid for the development, people like that are too rich to be considered mad. They’re simply eccentric with refined tastes, or “tossers” as the disenfranchised call them.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive avian genes. Quite large and difficult to train for an animal that lays eggs, this particular hybrid should always be kept warm due to the various functions taking up valuable space. Capable of producing not only eggs but also valuable silk, and is protected by thin, flexible scales to resist cuts.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
Unfertilized Chickenspider egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Since insectoid hybrids are always infertile, these eggs can never be fertilized.

The Tiny Terror with a disconcertingly large mouth and sharp teeth. These comic flightless web-footed little bastards may finally unwittingly help man to fathom the uncharted depths of just how dangerous they can make a living creature the size of a basketball.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive canine genes. Doesn’t produce much in the way of eggs but makes up for it by having a shorter gestation period and a very nasty bite. They’re also incredibly smart and agile, making them ideal for swarm tactics.
Fertilized Chickenwolf egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Chickenwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A bionic heart implant using a reinforced shell of insectoid chitin for protection. This is a heart designed to take a beating. Pun intended, but it's the last one, I promise.
This was once a smart table. But now it's dumb.

This hybrid humanoid / insectoid eye provides improved vision in a wide range of different wavelengths, including those usually outside the human range, completely modifying the way the subject perceives their surroundings.
A very volatile sludge with a tar-like appearance. Can be used defensively or converted to regular chemfuel in a refinery.
A bionic implant adding a sturdy crocodile spine to the creature's skeletal system.

A rather muscular hybrid with an impressively long snout, this ravenous reptile combines adaptability with mobility. Unusually, its terrifyingly long maw filled with curved teeth is not used to tear flesh to eat but to imbue toxins and the required muscles to spray those toxins out. This means they have to eat a lot more due to their subpar digestive system and their favourite activities include lounging in the sun and moving with disturbing quickness when the dinner bell is sounded. Also it… kinda looks like a weird shoe if you look at it, but, uh, don’t put your foot in there.

An equine hybrid with recessive reptile genes. A rather slow hybrid for its category, its main advantage is that it is very durable and it has a ranged toxic breath attack. Quite durable and comfortable in a wide range of temperatures, they present a hidden danger for those foolish enough to attack an otherwise easy target.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
This wood-like material has been extracted from the tentacles of an eldritch creature. Let's make a cupboard out of them!
A set of improved teeth designed to rapidly chew and ingest any kind of food. Leaves kind of a mess, though...
Grotesque but powerful hand blades that have been modified to be used in mining operations, increasing both speed and yield.
This seems to be an amalgamation of feathers, scales and wool, perfectly interweaved. Gives adequate defense against blunt attacks, and is quite warm.

A payload of dinosaur genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via a dinosaur genome excavator from:

A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:

Dinobeetles are huge beasts that produce a nutrient rich, amber-like substance. They probably would use it to build complex structures similar to bees, but they don't because... well, because they are a scientific abomination doomed from the start... Because of its unique biological properties, it can nourish almost any creature and never rots.
Thick leather partially covered in scales and dino-fuzz. Harvested from a dinosaur hybrid, this thick skin insulates and protects very well.

Triceramuffalos produce a secretion from various parts of their upper digestive tract. The resulting "milk" is fed to their offspring via regurgitation, and is similar to what mammals produce; it contains similar levels of fat and protein, along with carotenoids and antibodies. Now, why would anyone want to drink this? 'Cause it's the Rim, baby!

This huge sauropod hybrid has notable insectoid traits. It is hard to see how such a creature could have any predators, since it would probably leave its herbivorous diet and just eat them whole.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Diplobeetles produce a very nutritive jelly.

A vital piece of archotechnology that is part of the process in creating an Archocentipede.

This machine is able to extract genetic material from live hybrids and stores it for later use, preventing deterioration. Depending on the base Genoframe, the amount of viable genetic material extracted will vary.

Each storage bank can only store the genetic material from a single animal branch, such as ursine or feline. Inserting a hybrid into the database yields half as much valuable data compared to a paragon. DNA storage banks need to be in line of sight of the archocentipede former to connect to it properly.

Detailed yields for paragons (half for hybrids):

Awful quality: yields 1%
Poor quality: yields 3%
Normal quality: yields 6%
Good quality: yields 12%
Excellent quality: yields 25%
Masterwork quality: yields 50%
Legendary quality: yields 100%

This armoured rodent is an hybrid of a raccoon and a Doedicurus. The Doedicoon breeds fast, and its armor can be useful in the frontlines.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher. Armoured rodent

Man’s best friend who is also himself? Have you ever thought that the folds on a dog’s skin can act almost as pockets? What if those folds held a knife and the dog could wield it? Well look no further, as this pup will answer those questions with violence.

A canine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. A smart hybrid capable of holding conversations with their technical superiors and will sometimes obey commands to slaughter other tamed colony animals.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Dogmen will periodically obey commands to slaughter other colony (tamed) animals.
This huge egg is filled with unstable chemicals. It is going to explode!
Fertilized meadow lizard egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby meadow lizard. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Fertilized nighthrumbo egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby nighthrumbo. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.

The Elasmobearium is a hybrid of a bear and the ancient ancestor of the rhinoceros.
