The Elasmobearium is a pretty aggressive omnivore, using its horn to impale its victims and then mauling them with their vicious paws.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Aside from obviously being a very powerful creature, they produce a wool of excellent quality.

This otherworldly bear has repulsive, purple coloured tentacles protruding from its maw. Its pelt has a strange wooden-like appearance. Its ravenous appetite can only be sated by consuming large amounts of meat, which the creature dissolves with the green acidic goo dropping from its many gaping maws.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All bear hybrids have stun attacks. Since the creature constantly regenerates, its tentacles can be trimmed every few days, and used as a substitute for wood.

This polymorphic entity is made of light brown ichor and looks like amorphous goo. It seems to be extremely resilient and very difficult to dispatch. Its pelt seems to be powerfully acidic in substance and can dissolve human flesh with even a slight touch.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Explodes on death with a very powerful psionic blast. This explosion can also be triggered on command, though it will damage the creature too!

An unholy amalgamation of fungus, crustacean, insect and... chicken. Can this creature even be considered to be truly alive?\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This psionic chicken lays A LOT of purple eggs that erode your sanity. What's not to love?
Fertilized chicken of Mi-go egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chicken of Mi-go.
Unfertilized chicken of Mi-go egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting.

A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:

A payload of eldritch genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via an eldritch genome excavator from:

Jelly stored and used as food by oversized insects. It is smooth, rich, and brings incredible joy to those who consume it. Also, it quickly erodes their sanity and turns them into insane psychopaths, but it's so tasty! Because of its unique biological properties, it can nourish almost any creature and never rots.

This engorged, tick-like creature has a writhing mass of tentacles where its head would normally be, sensing their surroundings by a combination of touch and echolocation. They have lost their capacity to burrow underground, but they are capable of powerful psionic attacks.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Produces eldritch insect jelly, a very nutritive and enjoyable food that quickly erodes your sanity.
This milk is purple coloured, and its smell is indescribable. It whispers to you...

This blind muffalo can detect its prey by scent, and is attracted to animal -and human- blood, although it can also consume vegetal matter.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Decent pack beast that doesn't need to be penned. Produces milk and wool. Purple milk and wool...

This multi-headed snake has been blessed by Yig, the Father of Serpents. Or maybe it is just a very unfortunate snake which has been the target of your colonists' mad science... It is most likely the latter, to be honest.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks. The snake of Yig has six mouths, and attacks incredibly fast.
Fertilized snake of Yig egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting. If all goes well, it should hatch into an snake of Yig egg.
Unfertilized snake of Yig egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting.

IÃ¤! IÃ¤! Cthrumbo fhtagn! Ph'nglui mglw'nfah Cthrumbo Rimworld wgah'nagl fhtagn! A gigantic, squid-like creature that feeds on fear and nightmares. Legends say that an Eldritch Cthrumbo is really the wisest creature in the universe - it simply would melt the minds of everyone near it should it choose to speak.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Invoke insanity ability: twice a day, the Cthrumbo can release a cloud of insanity that erodes its prey's mind.

The back of this feral looking wolf is an indistinct mass of tentacles and malign eyes, constantly shifting in place and being reabsorbed into its pelt again.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. This creature doesn't have a huge health pool, but it is nearly impervious to damage.
This purple wool seems to be irrigated by pulsing, throbbing purple veins. However, they can only be detected by sight, not by touch.

This diminutive egg seems to be metallic in nature at first, yet it is soft enough to crush it between two fingers. Once thus crushed it can be swallowed normally, and the active mechanites inside have a reinvigorating effect on the consumer. This effect can be slightly addictive, though.

A relatively small machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb. Once a Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released. Provides limited growing room for specimens.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> This electrowomb is very cheap to build, but will produce a failure if the inserted creature has a body size bigger than 1.

A payload of equine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids have increased caravaning speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
Chickenbear feathers can be gathered and processed into a fine powder, which produces a mild euphoric sensation when burned and inhaled. Featherdust use can produce a dependency.

A payload of feline genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All feline hybrids have an inherent dodge chance. Combat feline hybrids get a further increase to dodge chance.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
A swarm of small, deadly flies. Almost impossible to hit using rudimentary weapons, the ideal way of dealing with fleshflies is by using fire, to which they are highly susceptible.

Something went terribly wrong. A huge and weird growth has spawned within the electrowomb, breaking it completely and spreading all around. This growth is a hatchery for small flies that can devour any living creature to the bone. It seems to be sensitive to daylight, but no one knows how far it can spread at night.

Something went terribly wrong. A huge and weird growth has spawned within the electrowomb, breaking it completely and spreading all around. This growth is a hatchery for small flies that can devour any living creature to the bone. It seems to be sensitive to daylight, but no one knows how far it can spread at night.

Something went terribly wrong. This wretched creature is struggling to keep breathing. Whilst it doesn’t pose any real threat and it seems quite friendly, it requires a tremendous amount of love and attention for it to find the strength to live another day.

Something went terribly wrong. This wretched creature is struggling to keep breathing. It’s rabid, and the moment it crawled out of the vat it started to look for the next unfortunate living being to destroy. It can and should be slain as soon as possible, as each additional kill only prolongs its life.
Deprecated. No hybrid should produce this now.

A machine where the miracle - or horror - of birth happens using automated procedures, a little genetic hand-waving and a great heaping dose of lost sleep. The inserted Genoframe is influenced by two genomes, one dominant and one recessive, and can be modified with optional boosters.

The process isn’t perfect, due to material contamination, missing chromosomes or simple bad luck, and can result in something completely unwanted being developed. For the machine, it makes no difference whether it’s trying to grow an animal or a comically large fruit. The resulting growth cell has a limited lifespan as the embryonic fluid will start breaking down if not allowed to gestate properly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> If the process to deliver ingredients to the genomorpher is interrupted, the ingredients that have already been inserted in it will deteriorate and be lost.

A specialized extraction module that is equipped to extract the genomes from a subject’s corpse. The material is converted into a soupish-mixture which is then condensed into an extremely complex genome. Completely useless by itself, but functions as a template which can be compared to another genome. The procedure removes any excess genetic information and renders them completely identical.\n\nBear in mind that this machine cannot duplicate some genomes, such as colossal.

A specialized extraction module that is equipped to extract the genomes from a subject’s corpse. The material is converted into a soupish-mixture which is then condensed into the relevant genome. Processing speed is influenced by intelligence, as it relies on knowing which button to press, when to press it and where to find it under the intimidating number of submenus.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> The genome extractor table will only work for tier 1 genomes. For tier 2 (insectoids, reptiles, equines) and tier 3 (colossals) you will need genome excavators, built on the genetics tinkering table or found on abandoned labs.

An advanced and specialized workbench capable of manufacturing medical-grade implants and mixing genetic boosters specifically tuned to Genomorphers. These include but aren’t limited to boosters that improve the safety or stability of hybrids and even micro-controllers that enable direct user-input.

A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nAwful genoframe will result in a hybrid with greatly decreased stats over a normal hybrid.

A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nExcellent genoframe will result in a hybrid with significantly improved stats over a normal hybrid.

A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nGood genoframe will result in a hybrid with slightly improved stats over a normal hybrid.

A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nLegendary genoframe will result in an incredibly powerful hybrid thatâ€™s eclipsing normal creatures.

A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nMasterwork genoframe will result in a hybrid with superior stats over a normal hybrid.

A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nNormal genoframe will result in a hybrid with baseline stats.

A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nPoor genoframe will result in a hybrid with slightly decreased stats over a normal hybrid.
An unfinished genoframe of some kind.

A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
This Lizardochs milk is green coloured, and quite unappetizing.

The horned groundffalo is a great pack beast (albeit a bit slow) capable of producing milk and wool, as well as mining for your colony. Compared to a Groundrunner it won't be as efficient on its mining work, but the other characteristics make up for this.\n\nUseful abilities: Produces wool, milk, acts as a pack beast and  mines rocks and minerals.

A portable, human-made casing designed to hold an embryo until it is transferred to a relevant Electrowomb, similar to fertilized eggs that require incubation. The embryonic fluid is unstable due to the unnatural genetic combination, so it will need to be implanted within 7 days after its creation. This growth cell has to be kept at a temperature between 0 and 50 degrees Celsius.
A biochip that modifies the brain to reduce the need for sleep.
This enormous bear pelt is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
A bearcat pelt rug. This thing seems to be quite more exotic than other pelt rugs!
This enormous bear pelt is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
A bear pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!

A payload of humanoid genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All humanoid hybrids have a somewhat decent level of intelligence and can do various tasks around the colony.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from any sentient humanoid.
A genetically modified thrumbo horn that can be implanted in a subject's forehead. Nasty.

An extremely cursed riding companion that can vocally explain in excruciating detail the troubles it goes through as a beast of burden. Most people don’t like to be reminded that animals are capable of feeling, especially if they can vocalise themselves in human speech. Don’t be surprised if no one wants to eat or wear butchered parts of this creature, as it looks like a misshapen human with cloven hands.

An equine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very intelligent and relatively low maintenance, they make for a decent rideable pack animal. Their main advantage comes in their ability to cheer up the travellers in a caravan, however it doesn’t understand social cues and can throw out some devastating social commentary.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM. Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). The hurseman engages caravan members too while caravaning, causing mood boosts (up to +15). 10% of the time however it will insult them, making them loose mood (up to -30). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.
Overlapping plates of hardened chitin. Really durable in combat, but provides little protection from weather.
Light, pliable sheets of scaly skin. Light, strong, and insulates well.
Fabric spun from silk fibers. Resists blunt and sharp damage easily, but provides almost no protection against extreme heat.

A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a measure of defense but, more importantly, makes the subject more resistant to high temperatures.
Self-assembling machinery that fuses with the specimen’s nerve centers during the growth process. Effectively allows hybrids to be drafted.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
A bionic implant which gives the creature compound insectile eyes. Compared with simple eyes, compound eyes possess a very large view angle, and can detect fast movement.
A bionic implant adding insect mandibles to the creature's face. Insect mandibles improve manipulation and eating, while also working as an offensive weapon

These hybrid humanoid / insectoid feeling appendages can be implanted in a subject's head, completely modifying the way the subject "sees" and feels the surrounding world. The experience may be slightly overwhelming due to sensorial overload.

A payload of insectoid genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All hybrids with insectoid genome get 100% toxic resistance as well as an armor increase. Combat hybrids get a further increase to armor.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
An implant that uses genetic splicing with insectoid genes to give the ability to detect pheromones, thus improving the olfactory system. Can't be installed on birds and (ironically) insects.
This is a bioelectronic kidney prosthesis that greatly improves the efficiency of the subject's kidney by using insectoid tissue, providing resistance to toxic substances.
Divides rooms. Simple doors must be manually opened, which slows people down. The amount of slowdown depends on what the door is made of.
An ancient, rusted bank of lockers. All of the lockers have been forced open with nothing valuable left behind.

A larger machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb. Once a Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released. Provides more growing room for larger specimens.
A bionic animal leg reinforced with tough lizard skin.

It is doubtful that one would meet a crueller or more sadistic creature than this hybrid. It actively experiences joy when slaughtering tame animals and executing prisoners, the latter of which even provides a powerful boost which can be used before battle. Maybe it has something to do with taking pleasure in killing those that made it this way, but that’s a story for another time.

A reptile hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. A very intelligent but combat-limited animal that performs surprisingly poorly against similar sized creatures. They will only passively engage creatures much smaller than itself and takes some twisted, psychotic pleasure in killing those who are completely helpless.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks. Lizardmen will obey order to slaughter other colony animals, as well as execute prisoners of the colony.

The lizardochs is basically a reptilian bull, an hybrid between a lizard and the ancestor of domesticated cattle. The lizardochs needs little substenance to survive, at least for a creature of its size, and can be used as a pack animal, which makes it ideal to use in caravans through warm and dry lands such as deserts.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks. Produces milk, and acts as a pack animal that needs little food.
This tangle of decayed cables were probably once housed inside the ceiling panels.
This wool is extremely thick and warm. Probably the best wool in the animal kingdom.

A floating, ticking time bomb that produces extremely reactive chemicals instead of chemfuel. Arguably more of a complex chemical distillery than an animal, it's a scientific miracle that it doesn’t explode every time it coughs. Surprisingly effective in combat in ways the enemy would really have preferred not knowing.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Thanks to its sac filled with buoyant chemicals, this hybrid never touches the ground and simply floats from location to location. Ignores terrain penalty costs and traps, while also being able to set down explosive traps of its own.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Set trap ability: once every 3 days the manalope can set up an explosive IED. Hovers over terrain, ignoring terrain costs, and doesn't trigger ANY traps.

What it lacks in ferocity, it makes up for in tenacity and formidable cuteness. What person, sane or insane, could reject the diplomatic arguments presented by a waist-high teddy bear with a face just begging to be pinched. Not that you actually would, as those sharp teeth remind one that it very likely prefers to debate with its food before eating it.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Absolutely terrible in combat potential despite its genetic material, but it is surprisingly adept at convincing prisoners to join the colony.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Menbear are adept conversationalists and will help in recruiting prisoners.

A creature originally designed for other recreational purposes before that particular laboratory of degenerates got glassed from orbit, they make for an affectionate conversation partner. You know, probably, most likely, until their particular speech pattern grinds on your nerves like a cheese grater.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive feline genes. A surprisingly large yet timid predator that can output a disturbing amount of damage. Typically fun to talk to, but 10% of the time it tries to be a little too cute.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). In the case of the mancat, these interactions will 90% of the time increase the targets' social joy by 15%, but the rest of the time they'll just make the target very annoyed... If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.

“You know how, like, chicken tastes, like, so good? So what if, like, we made a chicken that, y’know, when you killed it you could, I dunno, stuff it right in your mouth as fried chicken? Dude, this shit is the shit, where did you get this, man?” - Recording playback used in a hearing against a disgraced genetic scientist for misappropriating government funding.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive avian genes. A very valuable hybrid that convinces people that its greatest desire in life is to be eaten. When butchered, it leaves large amounts of completely healthy and nutritious meat that can be eaten as-is.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Doesn't lay eggs, but can be butchered for large amounts of a very nutritive meat that doesn't have poisoning chance and takes 6 days to rot.

Considered a straight upgrade from their progenitor, featured several times in Cannibal Monthly. Produces appreciable amounts of wool and milk, it is a living, breathing reference to the phrase “what does not kill me makes me stronger”. At least, when it comes to hauling large loads, which is all some people care about.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Capable of surviving very high temperatures in opposition to the traditional use of muffalos, this hybrid performs below average for a pack animal. However, due to adaptive muscle and skeletal structure, the more it participates as part of a caravan, the heavier the load it can withstand.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. The manffalo starts as a poor caravaning animal, but learns as time progresses. Every hour spent in a caravan will increase its cargo capacity by 1%, up to a maximum of a 100% increase from base.

They say you can learn a lot by taking something apart and putting it back together again. Not this creature, it doesn’t give a damn, it just wants to take things apart because the hardest part is someone else’s problem. Some people actually prefer these chitinous creatures over actual humans as they’re not going to use up good material in a botched attempt.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Easy to train, quite resilient but not a great combatant. This particular hybrid fills the niche of fulfilling deconstruction orders around the colony, freeing up otherwise valuable manpower.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Menscarab will take building deconstruction orders around the colony.

A bloody weird little thing that hates the cold with a passion, and will take any opportunity to clear away snow from its home. Given that they are active at nighttime, they make a great conversational companion for those otherwise asocial Night Owls you keep gathering.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Quite quick for their size and unusually a nocturnal animal, they make for a fast, social and quick-breeding hybrid. Will shovel snow if there are Clear Snow zones set up.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. The mansquirrel is a nocturnal animal that sleeps during the day. Will obey orders to clear snow around the colony.

Mixing the genes of one of the biggest carnivorous birds ever existing and those of a praying mantis is either a genious idea, or anathema. The Mantistanis stalks its prey by standing incredibly still and launching itself to attack with lightning speed.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Slight regenerative properties.
Fertilized mantistanis egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a mantistanis.
Unfertilized mantistanis egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

Possibly either the ancestor to the warg or an attempt at creating a successor, the team in charge of making this particular hybrid clearly had read one to many mythological novels. Arguably there’s no transformation at the full moon, so you just get the permanent single form, but there’s no pleasing some people.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive canine genes. A fast and dangerous hunter with almost misplaced self-confidence. Also very intelligent and possessing faint regenerative capabilities. Extremely hardy but also extremely restrictive in their diet.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.

The meadow lizard is a beast bred for speed. Even though they are naturally slower than meadow aves, they can be trained to be incredibly fast mounts.\n\nUseful abilities: if properly trained they can be ridden into battle at incredible speeds (this interaction needs the Giddy-Up, combat mounts mod, and it will make the Meadow Lizards move 100% faster when used as mounts). They are also venomous.

This cybernetic beast has been mutated far beyond its original form. Heavily armoured to withstand heavy fire, and armed with a terrifying plasma cannon.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechabear an effective weapons platform, akin to a rocket turret. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

The tail of this robotic creature has been crafted from an amalgamation of scyther blade structures and mechanite self-replicators. This allows the mechacat to use its tail as a throwable weapon, and quickly regenerate it to use it again. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechacat capable of attacking with slashing attacks from a range. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

This cybernetic organism is adept at reconnaissance and infiltration, due to its small size and speed. It is armed with a small sniper flechette gun. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mecha-chicken an effective weapons platform with very long range sniper attacks. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

This piece of technology is unusual in that its design isn’t linked to the original Mechahybridizer, having been developed later on while trying to emulate the system internals. This is due to the fact that the Mechahybridizer broadcasts a signal to a nearby orbital mechanoid hive to protect the process and then recover the potential resulting mechahybrid to be dispatched into assignments. However, this process is extremely expensive and often there would be enough recovery personnel on-site, so this device was developed to reduce costs.

In the event that the Mechahybridizer attempts to send a signal once it begins the operation, this device intercepts the signal before it can extend to a sufficient distance. Upon capture, the device self-destructs to fool the system that the signal has been received and a unit has been dispatched.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If there are no intact fuses around a mechahybridizer, a mechanoid raid will arrive via drop-pod in 6 hours if the mechahybridization process is attempted.
A bionic heart strengthened by the action of active mechanites.

More machine than horse, the mecha-horse has an agile frame covered by ablative panels. Though well-liked by riders for its speed and agility, it has a significant design flaw: it heats up considerably during extended travels, needing to periodically vent excess heated air generated on their internal machinery.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mecha-horse capable of using its "vent heated exhaust" ability to scorch enemies on the surrounding tiles. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

A large broadcasting server that is specifically tuned to maintain the tameness of mechahybrids. Without this server, the self-replicating mechanites within the mechahybrids will eventually no longer be able to register friendlies as their IFF updates and branches, causing them to go manhunter.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> It is important to note that a single antenna can only broadcast to a limited number of mechahybrids, specifically up to 5 of them regardless of their size or complexity. Any additionally mechahybrid present beyond the initial 5 will no longer receive updates and will eventually lose their "tameness". Part of the broadcast will reach nearby settlements and ruins, attracting maddened mechahybrids, so they will appear from the moment you have a mechahybrid antenna in the periodic roaming monstrosities raids.

A massive factory designed to improve upon genetically perfect animals by adding doses of mechanites in a controlled fashion and influencing the process to ensure the it is a success and there is no rejection of foreign materials. Once the operation begins on a creature designated as "paragon", the factory closes with hermetic seals and broadcasts a recovery signal to the nearest orbital mechanoid hive.

Originally designed to be part of an automated mechanoid factory placed on animal worlds, the intent was to herd animals into the system, weaponize them, and send them off for their deployment. Due to the invasive nature of the mechanites, the creature absolutely had to lack junk genetic data lest the subject perish and waste materials. Later on, the lack of such paragons resulted in it being installed in facilities with genetic modification equipment on hand and external bypasses to reduce the waste material produced from summoning a retrieval team.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If there are no intact fuses around a mechahybridizer, a mechanoid raid will arrive via drop-pod in 6 hours if the mechahybridization process is attempted.

This cybernetic boomalope has been outfitted with four mechanized chemfuel reservoirs. The creature can use these reservoirs to produce a jet of burning fuel similar to a flamethrower, not incredibly harmful, but perfect for starting huge fires.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechalope an effective weapons platform, akin to a flamethrower. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
This metallic, quicksilver-like substance is not edible. It can be processed in a refinery to obtain neutroamine, chemfuel and a bit of silver.

The ultimate violation of all ethical concerns in genetic engineering: a humanlike mechanoid creature with obvious malevolence. Created by warping humanoid genes into a dangerous Mime, and then further modifying it with self-replicating mechanites by using forgotten technology, the mecha-mime will need to be kept in a short leash, lest its IFF system goes haywire and decides to enact its very own judgement day.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster. The mecha-mime has lost the infiltration abilities of a regular mime, but in exchange it possesses a powerful spatial warping cannon that ignores enemy's armor. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

<color=#E5E54C>Warning:</color> A mecha-mime's IFF sensors will fail in only 2 days, instead of 5.

This hulking cybernetic organism has been reinforced with an adaptive plasteel mesh, enabling it to carry an incredible amount of stuff. The mecha-muffalo is the perfect pack animal, but it is quite useless in an offensive role. Defensively, however, it is armed with a smoke cannon that it can use to cover the battlefield in a dense cloud of dust.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mecha-muffalo capable of laying a screen of smoke for defensive purposes. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
This highly sought-after booster will result in an almost perfect hybrid: fertile, draftable and genetically stable.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.

This strange stellated octahedron seems to house an assembly of complex computer chips. It appears to be self-powered, and a faint beeping noise emanates from its core. It is constantly emitting encoded radio waves, and perhaps these could be suitably amplified if the technology behind it could be properly reverse engineered.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid chips can only be found in abandoned labs, and can be used to build beomechanical lab beacons, that will allow you to find a biomechanical lab complex, as well as mechahybrid antennas.

This tiny cybernetic organism can be mass-produced as a kind of "suicide weapon". It is very fast, and armed with a short ranged stun gun, so hordes of mecha-rats can be sent to the front lines to keep enemies stunned and in check. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, allowing the colonists to send the mecha-rat to the front lines to quickly stun enemies. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
This elephant auditory receptor can scan a really wide band of frequencies.

This cybernetic organism has traits from both its insectoid and mechanoid origins. The mechaspider is armed with a powerful twin-coiled laser machine gun, perfect to obliterate enemies in a rain of laser fire.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechaspider an effective weapons platform with a very high fire rate. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
A bionic implant adding a plasteel structure with active mechanites to the creature's skeletal system.
An advanced animal tail, even more powerful than a natural one. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.

This hulking cybernetic behemoth is why you don't need tanks in the Rim. Its huge plasteel carapace hides a miniaturized synchrotron that constantly generates small amounts of antimatter, which is instantly used to fill a miniature antigrain warhead. Slow, but quite effective.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechathrumbo effective mobile artillery. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

This slow, cybernetic beast is heavily armoured with a plasteel-encased biomechanical shell. It is armed with a poison spitting cannon.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechaturtle an effective weapons platform that fires venomous projectiles. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

This cybernetic beast has been augmented with a powerful synthetic structure capable of generating waves of cryogenic liquid. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechawolf an effective weapons platform that fires frost projectiles. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
This wool seems to be strengthened by plasteel micro-fibers, and covered with advanced circuitry.
An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.

A payload of megafauna genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via a megafauna genome excavator from:

A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:

A bionic implant adding a small thrumbo horn where the creature's nose used to be. This implant acts as a nose, allowing the creature to breathe and smell, and can be used by the creature in melee. Birds, insects, and creatures that already have a thrumbo horn don't have enough space on their skull to install this implant.

Far too independent to be a combat animal but certainly can both dish out and take pain. Works best in the background, minding its own business and digging up metals it finds. No one is really sure why but it probably has to do with those massive whiskers.

A rodent hybrid with recessive ursine genes. An independent predator with a very low metabolism and high toughness. Has an unusual behaviour where it’ll periodically dig for treasures, typically raw metals.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.

An oversized naked mole-rat with frighteningly large chompers, however they’re not as dangerous as they look as they are quite timid and use said teeth for chewing up tough vegetables. Objectively cute when you give them a good brushing, but have a nasty habit of overshooting hand-fed snacks and biting on hands due to poor eyesight. A saddle is recommended for riding, as there is little fat and no fur to cushion their boney structure and loose skin.

An equine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Quite small and lacking in durability, but surprisingly capable as a mount. Exceedingly filthy for an animal that eats so little, however this is tied to its rapid gestation and faint regenerative capabilities.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.

A hulking monstrosity that is genuinely caring for those around them, taking great joy and actual personal pleasure through working diligently and caring for those it considers friends and family. Evidently, it was bred specifically by megacorporations to work in dangerous mines, because human greed never changes.

A rodent hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very low hunger rate and very intelligent, these gentle giants enjoy digging with their hands. They will sometimes work mining orders given the chance but they’re very slow due to their bulk.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Molemen will periodically obey commands to mine. This will also work with the Quarry mod.
A biochip that reduces pain sensitivity, allowing the subject to survive pain shock.
These monitors will never show any information again.
A bionic implant using monitor lizard brain tissue. This highly intelligent reptile has very quick reflexes.
A bionic implant adding a moth tympanal organ to the creature's ear. Moths can hear sounds of a higher frequency than any bat can produce.
A bionic vomeronasal organ that allows the subject pheromonal communication with other animals, increasing the chance of taming and training them successfully.

An absolute unit of a muffalo hybrid with muscles bursting underneath the layers of fur and fat. An excellent caravan companion for those wanting to transport lots of goods and hot muffalobear milk makes living in eternal winter seem almost worth it.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive ursine genes. While they are slower and eat more compared to a regular muffalo, they are better at self defence and offer the highest carry capacity of all primary muffalo hybrids. They also produce good thick wool and delicious yet simple milk.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
A delicious milk with roasted overtones. Very pleasurable to eat, but not very nutritious.

Combining fierce independence with an animal suited for travelling long distances might not have been the best idea. But critics have clearly never been nuzzled by a giant cat only to be buried alive in a pile of insulating hair in the winter, so what do they know.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive feline genes. Not the best for caravan duty but their regular grooming produces bundles of thick wool and can be milked for a rather disgusting milk filled with hair. A low maintenance way of getting more hair than you bargained for.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Produces wool without needing to be sheared. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.

Don’t let its adorable appearance fool you, this beastie is ready to fly off its handle at a moment's notice. The more it’s hurt, the more pissed off it gets and the faster it’ll try to rip its enemies to shreds.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive avian genes. A bit too slow and weak for a caravan companion but it makes  for a vicious and suicidal protective companion. Produces a thick, chunky milk that tastes like a protein shake.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> The melee cooldown of its attacks scales with the percentage of lost health, up to 6 times faster. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
This lumpy milk seems to have concentrated protein chunks, tasting like chicken nuggets. Disgusting, but colonists seem to like it.

A payload of muffalo genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All muffalo hybrid animals are pack animals, with primary muffalo hybrids getting 50% increased capacity caravaning. All muffalo hybrids have an increase to minimum comfortable temperature.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:

Belligerent bulls with a penchant for the tastes of the wild wastes. A herd of these creatures can be occasionally seen trawling through regions, consuming practically all edible vegetation and leaving fertile waste in their path. Some argue that this is the ideal form of muffalo for nomad tribes, as they produce everything they need to survive winters and are large enough to feed said tribe through the harshest of seasons.

An equine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Very unusual in that it doesn’t help a caravan speed along, largely due to it excessively foraging and eating various vegetation from new locations, as well as roughage to help with digestion. The benefit from this behaviour is that this stimulation is the only way it’ll produce milk and wool, so herds of these hybrids can be taken on a long journey to provide produce vital to small and starting colonies.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.

Milking snakes for venom in order to create medicine is an extremely dangerous career. While this particular beast of burden proved to be capable in other areas, it was mainly synthesised in order to provide a safer way of sourcing an anaesthetic coagulant to be used in emergencies. Or someone was trying to get high by milking a reptilian space cow, who really knows.

A reptile hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. A strong pack animal with a massive appetite and a proportionate filth rate. Quite durable and will prove a fatal mistake for any animal that attempts to hunt them, as their fangs, horns and claws are all venomous. Can be milked every ten days for a vial of paralysing coagulant, best used for large animals that are suffering from multiple lacerations.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
An implant that uses muffalo genes to increase breathing capacity.
By implanting muffalo mammaries into a colonist... Wait, what??? Who came up with this idea????? Anyway... this implant allows your colonist to be milked... What the...

The animal we’ve all been waiting for, namely one that not only cleans up after itself but for others as well. Their fur is surprisingly soft and has antibacterial properties, which it uses along with its fingered paws to clean surfaces. Once the fur starts to become matted is when it needs to be trimmed.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Quite intelligent but they lose out on a lot of their progenitor’s hardiness against the cold. They are also very clean animals and will clean areas around the colony, and can be sheared regularly for a decent amount of wool.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans. Muffalomen are clean muffalo hybrids that will periodically clean around the colony.
By weaving muffalo muscle strands with the subjects muscular system, strength, resilience and carrying capacity are greatly increased.

A very naughty hybrid that can be told to relieve itself on command, creating puddles of chemfuel-infused urine around it. For those who just want to watch the world burn in the most vulgar way.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. A bit too slow for a caravan companion but make up for it with intelligence and an unusual chemfuel ability that lends itself for defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.

An oversized grub with udders that extrude jelly instead of milk, its hard-headedness serves it well in both getting what it wants and nutting anyone foolish enough to think they’re better than them. Allegedly there was a case where the owner was prevented from shearing the creature for so long that the overlapping plates completely prevented it from moving, who was afterwards thanked with a kick in the nether region.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Higher metabolism compared to other insectoid hybrids as well as stubborn, any predator foolish enough to think of them as an easy meal will be responded with a headbutt like a sledgehammer. Can work as a pack animal but refuses to haul comparatively heavy loads. Regularly produces precious jelly and can be sheared for chitin, which overlaps and protects the animal well from blunt attacks.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
Cool! You can now make your own Game of Thrones / Rimworld cosplay! Or it can also be repurposed as a comfy rug.

Breeding a muffalo hybrid into such a small package means they have very little capacity for either wool or milk. They’re very quick though, and your handlers better be quicker on their daily milking and shearing.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Very weak as a pack animal but they breed quickly and can be milked and sheared daily. The amount of product is small, but it’s thick wool and nutritious milk.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
A muffalo pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!

A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a measure of defense but, more importantly, makes the subject more resistant to low temperatures.
A spine implant that uses genetic splicing with muffalo genes to give an old (or damaged) spine decent functioning. This implant is not meant to replace healthy spines.

Someone clearly wanted multiple muffalos that would be capable of tearing foolish attackers to shreds. They eat a ton but those massive teeth of theirs aren’t just good at chewing up vegetation. Plus they’re incredibly cute when they wag their massive tails.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive canine genes. The combination of lethality, speed and high intelligence makes this hybrid a fierce pack animal, both figuratively and literally. Capable of pack tactics with other muffalowolves that further increase their melee dodge, hit chance and dps.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> The pack tactics ability will give any muffalowolves around bonuses to melee dps, melee dodge and melee hit chance. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.

This abomination is part chicken, part sentient cactus. If cactus nuggets weren't a thing, they are now. You can think of them as solar-powered egg layers.\n\nUseful abilities: Needlechicken are decent egg layers. They can defend themselves with tiny poison barb attacks, and they almost don't need sustenance (except for a large amount of sunlight).
A fertilized needlechicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a needlechick. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized needlechicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A biochip using animal stem cells to reinforce the natural abilities of human neurons, increasing learning and research speed.

The nighthrumbo is a fearsome predator. Armed with strong jaws and its horn, as well as ranged quill attacks, it will make quick work of any prey it decides to attack. It is also a nocturnal animal, sleeping during the day and being active at night.\n\nUseful abilities: Ranged quill attacks.

A slurry of liquefied organic pulp fills this reinforced glass vat. This solution is used by genetic experiments, and increases work speed when placed near a Vanilla Genetics Expanded workbench. One workbench can use to up to two nutrient vats.

You know you have gone too far in your scientific experiments when you are implanting a human being with an ovipositor. This will enable the colonist to lay chicken eggs twice a day. It hurts as hell, though. Also, yuck.

The Paraceratherium was the biggest land mammal to ever exist. When you hibridize it with a sturdy muffalo, the resulting creature is the most powerful pack animal ever created. Incredibly stubborn and bad-tempered, though.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Produces wool and milk, and acts as an incredibly strong -albeit slow- pack beast.

The absolute alpha of units, the chonkiest of bois, winner of consecutive Fat Bear Weeks before being disqualified due to winning too often.\n\nA perfect ursine animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon has a particularly powerful stun attack and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.

While more of a risk to keep around, they are considerably more efficient and don’t eat your crops as much. Plus their permanent sad expression just makes you want to pet them, how can you say no.\n\nA perfect boomalope animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon produces deepchem instead and explodes on death in a much more violent fashion.

Sure the little blighter is more stubborn than its progenitor, but not having to gather the eggs so often and a bigger fried egg makes all the difference.\n\nA perfect avian animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon possesses inherent wildness not present in domesticated chickens but is more likely to stay put.

Fertilized avian paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. These eggs are pure, pristine, perfect examples of chicken eggs. Immaculate eggness. If all goes well, it should hatch into an avian paragon.
Unfertilized avian paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. These eggs are pure, pristine, perfect examples of chicken eggs. Immaculate eggness.

An incredible, large fur baby that not only wants you to scratch its belly, but regularly and without fear of getting your eyes gouged out by their massive claws. Also they’re smart enough to go after prey less than half their size, but that’s more so they don’t have to spend so much time grooming themselves afterwards.

A perfect feline animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is incredibly intelligent and can be properly trained with minimal retraining, as well as being an excellent and affectionate companion.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Cat reflexes ability: twice per day, it can use an ability that gives it further melee capabilities.

The most majestic of horses is proud and magnificent, and doesn’t care for your rules, man. So keep them properly penned up, because they’ll try to escape to freedom so they can run wild and free. And then wonder where all the carrots, sugar cubes and salt licks have disappeared to.\n\nA perfect equine animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon makes for an excellent mount and pack animal for caravans.

There is a saying that all of life is a circle, and that eventually everything returns to its origins only to begin again. This particular humanoid has undergone some mental evolution while at the same time suffering from physical devolution, resembling more closely humanity’s distant evolutionary ancestors. Just don’t say monkey.

A perfect humanoid animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon can drive man’s curiosity ever forward by providing a boost to research speed with a well-placed insightful word of wisdom.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid paragons will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Research drive ability: offers investigative insights to nearby researches, allowing them to more easily reach a breakthrough by increasing research speed by 10%.

Small and venomous, with a deadly fart, not quite what one was expecting for the perfect reptilian form. I mean, you’d think you’d get a tyrannosaurus rex or something but no, it seems that the humble iguana is here to stay. If anything, the shock of something so small moving so fast has got to be worth something.\n\nA perfect reptile animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is oviparous and can manually rupture neck sacs containing venomous gas.
Fertilized reptile paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a reptile paragon.
Unfertilized reptile paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

A terrifying callback to the original Sorne geneline, this mobile hive was originally designed to engage the mechanoid enemy head-on. By carrying an entire colony of insects with hardened mandibles, their ability to practically disassemble the mechanical menace proved just as effective at turning against their human overlords.

A perfect insectoid animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is extremely intelligent and carries a dangerous brood of warrior flies. A burst of pheromones summons the drones to protect their hive and queen, shredding apart all other nearby creatures.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Insect clouds ability: twice a day it can create insect clouds around it that damage any animal crossing them, except insectoid paragons, which are immune.

A return to simpler times, when everyone could have sworn blind that muffalos used to be able to produce milk but were instead fooled by hornless paragons.\n\nA perfect muffalo animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon also produces milk and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.

Look, I know what you’re thinking. Why is the rat the paragon of all rodents? And even if you’re not thinking that, this is rhetorical anyway so there. What other rodent has caused civilization to collapse due to poorly held beliefs and lack of proper hygiene. As such, we give you the King Rat. Or Queen Rat, if you want a whole colony showing up.\n\nA perfect rodent animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon has a very fast gestation period and its attacks transmit the plague.

The gigantic, graceful creature has somehow been improved even further, proving infallibly that there’s always a taller mountain. Significantly easier to train and with a more efficient digestive system, this creature is possibly how the thrumbo originally was when first bred in a lab before it got tainted through terraforming.\n\nA perfect colossal animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon makes for an excellent mount for caravans and can forcibly rush itself, getting faster and creating dust clouds that impede ranged attacks.

An improved version of man’s best friend, easier to train, more assertive and dangerous to those that threaten a household. Sadly, far too proud to admit it really wants a belly rub.\n\nA perfect canine animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.
Paraceramuffalo milk is rich and creamy. Probably too rich in fat, but certainly tasty.

This hybrid ornithopod has two cranial crests, which it uses in mating rituals. Their whole body is covered in large sacs of volatile chemicals.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This animal was created with one goal in mind: to produce huge amounts of chemfuel. Their corpses can be directly refined into compressed chemfuel in a biorefinery.
Fertilized parasaurolope egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a parasaurolope.
Unfertilized parasaurolope egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

This tiny vial is filled with a concentrated solution of Muffalokomodo saliva, which acts as a powerful neuromuscular-blocking drug. This effectively paralyses anyone that drinks it, though it is not lethal. It will also stop bleeding rate while active, perhaps allowing a person to avoid bleeding to death if the poison is administered quick enough. Effects wear off after 6 hours.
Artificial hearing aids that block psychic emanations, providing considerable resistance to mechanoids' psychic attacks.

For a combination of two relatively small animals, the rabbitcat is fucking massive and has a robust physique. It knows no fear and will tackle anything it wants to eat. Some say this was the other attempt to create the Rabbit of Caerbannog, because who says geneticists don’t have a sick sense of humour.

A rodent hybrid with recessive feline genes. Very intelligent considering the source material, these hybrids are fast, lethal and possess a robust physique with rapid natural regenerative capabilities. Quite stubborn so they require frequent, competent training but they aren’t afraid to show their appreciation.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.

Legends say of an ancient and vicious monster, a creature so foul, so cruel that no man yet has fought with it and lived. This was a somewhat successful attempt, as its weakness lulls the enemy into a false sense of security so that it can rip their throat out. So do you feel lucky, punk?

A rodent hybrid with recessive avian genes. A tiny, weak, fast-moving pest with an insane appetite for its size. Incredibly fast gestation period and a 1% chance to instantly kill when attacking.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
This server rack once housed a lot of genetic information, but all its data has now degraded completely.

This raptor theropod has strange turtle traits. Why strange? Well, if you picture an armored dinosaur you tend to imagine something like an ankylosaurus, not a raptor with a tortoise shell on its back.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
Fertilized raptortoise egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a raptortoise.
Unfertilized raptortoise egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

An interesting pack animal that packs an incredible punch that also pisses and shits everywhere. That ain’t even the worst part, because these rodents of unusual size are also plague carriers with complete immunity to toxicity. Sometimes scientists just can’t leave well enough alone.

A rodent hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Quite clever, almost absurdly lethal and quick on its feet. While they are herbivores, they will still happily munch on corpses thanks to their large appetite. Not as short a gestation period compared to other primary rodent hybrids, but every bite transmits the plague.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
This milk has a rich, thick quality to it. It's origins, however, might be a bit dubious... Unless you trust mafia guys called Tony.

A payload of reptile genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have greatly increased minimum and maximum comfortable temperature. They also have melee and ranged venomous attacks.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
An ear implant that uses genetic splicing with rodent genes to give an old (or damaged) ear decent hearing. This implant is not meant to replace healthy ears.

A payload of rodent genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have a reduced body size, but greatly increased reproduction rates (if fertile).

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
An implant that uses genetic splicing with rodent genes to give an old (or damaged) nose decent smelling. This implant is not meant to replace healthy noses. Can't be installed on birds and insects.
Raw meat from a formless spawn of Tsathoggua. Or... or at least a boomalope abomination that *looked* similar. Hard to know with these things.
A collapsed antenna that is completely unable of transmitting anything ever again.
The broken maws of this ancient factory will never be able to close again.
This ancient tank once converted biological matter into an electrical current. It is now completely broken and useless.
This ancient structure was once used to inject nanomachines in a subject in an attempt to create the perfect soldier. It now lies rusted and unsalvageable.

It seems that this structure was capable of emitting some kind of electromagnetic radiation used to control brain function of certain animals. This was crude and primitive technology prone to failure and instability, and as such it is completely useless now.
Whatever was held inside this dank, rusted metal coffin has long since escaped. It is damaged beyond all repair.
This deteriorated platform seems to have been used to assemble some kind of machinery. It now lays bare and empty.
Whatever this barrel held has long ago evaporated.

This semi-spherical vat seems to have held some kind of viscuous liquid inside, as evidenced by the rancid-smelling residue coating its insides. It is now completely ruined and only useful as metal scrap.
An ancient genepod that was used to combine genetic material. This machine is obsolete and damaged beyond repair.
A completely obsolete model of gene recombinator, that is also deteriorated and rusted beyond and salvaging.

This ancient computer was used to storage genetic information and feed genetic dataprints to other machines, but its components have deteriorated so far that all the data it contained has degraded to nothingness.
Yet another workstation that has deteriorated beyond all recovery. A foul-smelling vial of stagnant liquid warns you to keep away from it.
This little wheeled table housed a small specialized computer and refrigerated trays to store genetic material, but it is now so damaged it can only be used as scrap.
A large centrifuge used in gene sequencing. Its corroded parts will never be of use to anyone again.
This small canister has been smashed to pieces and whatever it contained has long evaporated.
Whatever this barrel held has long ago evaporated.
Rusted shelving units coated in grime and dust.
This workbench appears to have been used to try and reverse engineer mechanoid components, but it is damaged to the point of uselessness.
A machine used to process mutagenic minerals, now completely broken and unsalvageable.
A slurry of liquefied organic pulp filled this reinforced glass vat. It now lays broken and empty.
A weird-looking machine that shredded wood into organic pulp. Broken beyond repair.
An obsolete, ancient machine that recycled wood, corpses and bones into organic pulp. Broken beyond repair.
These structural cryofuel coils have been completely ruined by time and neglect. They can no longer function.
A metal sarcophagus used to grow bio-mechanic implants in a controlled manner. Its machinery has been gutted and has later decayed into rusted scrap.
Small canisters that once held some valuable liquids.
An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
This enormous bear pelt is part hair and part scales. It is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
Really, couldn't you decorate with vinyl decals, like a normal, non-insane person? This looks horrendous. Some of the colonists seem to like it, though..
A scyther tail created by attaching a jury-rigged scyther blade to an animal's tail. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.
A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
