A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
Drinking from this will give you more than just a few rads...
A metallic capsule filled with some bioluminescent compound. This one seems to still be producing some faint light.

Possibly one of the weirdest and cutest snakes to be ever created. They have several tiny legs which are used to push its body along, while also having an invitingly soft fur. Quite a lot of people never stop freaking out from something sliding up the backs of their legs only to get softly headbutted by a purring python.

A reptile hybrid with recessive feline genes. An unusually furry snake that enjoys nuzzling while also hunting for prey much weaker than itself. Incredibly intelligent and easy to train, they combine quick reflexes and an incredibly nasty bite and spitting attack.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.

People really never understand what it is to have a bad hair day, and this feisty feline is gonna help teach them. As for those who don’t want to be covered in hairballs formed from silk strands and adhesive mucus, them’s the brakes, kid. Don’t wanna get spat on, then be friends with them and get over your arachnophobia.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive feline genes. Both quick and decent offensive power, this one can handle themselves in both melee combat and a blinding ranged attack. A little difficult to train, but very clever and affectionate with flexible scales to resist cuts.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.

Arachnophobia is no joke for many people, and the researcher that created this armoured arachnid had an inherent desire to share their fear with others. Granted they passed out, frothing at the mouth, when the creature emerged from the pod, but damn it it’s hard to argue with results in a riding companion that can not only keep up with the competition, but also disable them and eat them on the go as a tasty snack.

An equine hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. A rather slow and filthy hybrid of this category, but it works very well as a ride due to the multiple legs easing the burden of long distance travel inherent to caravaning. However, their main advantage is that they are also incredibly capable in combat, being armour plated and capable of both high damage and ranged stunning attacks by shooting globs of sticky threads.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.

Neutroamine is rather difficult to manufacture due to the sterile environment needed in creating a synthetic precursor chemical. So some insane madman decided to create a living creature that can synthesise its own neutroamine. Granted it’s not a lot, but have enough of these disturbingly powerful and quick cobras and no one will ever try to rob you, or live long enough to get away.

A reptile hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. For an animal so small, it has an incredibly powerful bite and can spit venom. Sadly they can’t be trained but will happily hunt smaller prey that happen to wander into their territory or fight back against any foolish predators. Can be milked once every several days for a vial of neutroamine, so they function well as part of a large drug farm.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.

This hybrid therapod has bear-like characteristics. There are several theories concerning the use of the Spinosaur's spinal fan-sail, but in the case of the Spinobear it is clear what use it has: kicking your ass.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All bear hybrids have stun attacks. Aside from obviously being a very powerful creature, they produce a weird mixture of wool and scales that can be sheared and used in textile operations. They can also be slaughtered for their pelts. Like all dinosaur hybrids, they are oviparous.
Fertilized spinobear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into an spinobear.
Unfertilized spinobear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

It’s small, it’s cute, it’s a ticking time bomb waiting for some idiot to think it looks delicious. It’s not going to be a big explosion, but that doesn’t matter much when it’s in your mouth.

A rodent hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Small, weak but fast, this hybrid suits those looking for a fuel production method in a small package. Produces chemfuel much more efficiently compared to a boomalope but it needs to be milked every day.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.

Compressed medicine that allows hybrids to develop functional sexual organs. Effectively allows hybrids to reproduce with the exact same species.

This booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.

Note: Insectoid hybrids are always incapable of reproducing.

A colossal, armored dinosaur-thrumbo hybrid. Probably not as smart as a thrumbo, but it makes up for it by being incredibly tough.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Greater stamina ability: twice per day, the Stegothrumbo can become almost impervious to damage.
Fertilized stegothrumbo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. This is insanely nutritive! If all goes well, it should hatch into a stegothrumbo.
Unfertilized stegothrumbo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. This is insanely nutritive!
Raw meat from a manchicken. Unbelievably tasty, and unbelievably unethical. Light, nutritive and with no poisoning chance.
Absolutely and completely worthless, but when properly processed alongside an actual genome it can be trimmed down to an identical copy.
Coarse, strong wool. Provides heat and cold protection, as well as decent defense.
Brittle, strange smelling wool. Not the best wool to craft apparel with, though it's better than nothing. Provides nice heat insulation.

Now here’s a creature you absolutely want to keep properly fed, and you’re going to need entire fields to support a single one. The ridiculous amount of chemfuel they produce means that the ceasing of their life functions is more accurately described as “thermonuclear”. So if they are going to blow up, send them as a surprise gift to friends and family who keep trying to kill you.

A colossal hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. The addition of chemfuel production has vastly increased the amount of food they need to survive, but they’re also smart enough to not need a pen. Consider keeping them far away from your growing fields if the local biome can grow vegetation fast enough to support one.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.

If you thought Thrumbos were tough and hit like a tank, may I present to you the answer to all your prayers. If you can feed the thing, this absolute unit would work extremely well as an assault force against any force foolish enough to threaten its feedstock. Plus you get to ride it on long journeys, possibly clearing entire forests on the way.

A colossal hybrid with recessive ursine genes. While proportionately it has a lower metabolism, its 50% increase in size still leaves it with a higher hunger rate than a thrumbo. They are however easier to train, hit much harder and can take even more punishment.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
A bionic brain implant using thrumbo neural tissue. Superb intelligence and reflexes.

The other half of the super predator duo, along with the Thrumwolf, exchanging some ferocity and durability in exchange for going for the trachea and spitting suffocating hairballs. That might not sound that dangerous but imagine having a fistful of hair pulled from a shower drain smothering your mouth, and you’ll realise that’s still not disgusting enough. But for those that it appreciates, it will often pin them down and snuggle them given the chance.

A colossal hybrid with recessive feline genes. A large predator with an insane appetite with a focus on ferocious attacks that restrict their prey’s breathing. Extremely agile with a soft spot for those it loves, best suited for biomes that support enough wildlife to sate its hunger.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.

This is probably the closest chickens will be able to get to recovering their past glory as super predators. They’ll often hunt any other animal for their next meal, multiple times a day, and you haven’t yet lived until you’ve ridden one on a long journey. If they could be further bred to cross mountains and oceans, they’d be almost perfect.

A colossal hybrid with recessive avian genes. The combination of genes has turned this somewhat peaceful herbivore/dendrovore into a full carnivore, which makes it easier to support their large appetite. As an intelligent predator, it can move incredibly quickly for its size and its attacks are incredibly fierce. It also lays massive eggs that can feed entire families.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
Fertilized Thrumbochicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Thrumbochicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A bionic eye reinforced with specialized photoreceptors from a powerful thrumbo.

A payload of colossal genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 3 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Extreme increase in size, health, damage and market value at the cost of slower attack speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via an archotech genome excavator from:
A bionic implant adding thrumbo jaws to the creature's mouth. These creatures devour trees, so you can imagine their jaws are up to the task.
A bionic animal leg reinforced with muscle tissue from a powerful elephant.

About as close as anyone has ever gotten in making a recreation of dragons from folklore. Capable of surviving in extreme cold or heat, the only thing really missing is a pair of wings and an innate desire to hoard piles of gold for a nest. That and the fact that it isn’t carnivorous, instead having an appetite for vegetables and wood fibres.

A colossal hybrid with recessive reptile genes. With a mouth dripping with venom and tough, flexible scales covering the body, this hybrid makes for a nasty and incredibly durable combat hybrid. If that wasn’t enough, it can also breath out a fog of noxious fumes and move deceptively quickly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.

Look, we know what you want, a thrumbo that won’t eat your crops before they’ve harvested, thereby wasting entire seasons of growth for your most valuable plants. And we’re here to tell you it doesn’t exist. But we’ve gotten close in that this one will typically harvest your plants before it eats them for worse nutritional gain. Unless it’s trees, in which case we got nothing.

A colossal hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Considerably easier to train and maintain, this particular hybrid will help around the colony by cutting and harvesting designated plants. Unusually lean for how much it eats, it won’t provide much in butchering.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Thrumbomen will obey orders to cut and harvest plants around the colony.

Practically an overgrown and intelligent pest capable of tunnelling underground, so not even a nuke could clear an infestation. And it truly would be an infestation, as it has a gestation period measured in days rather than weeks, with a single breeding pair capable of wiping out an entire agricultural planet.

A colossal hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Incredibly durable and fast breeding, this surprisingly small hybrid can work as a durable screening force. One of the weakest colossal hybrids in terms of attack power but very few can outlast its incredibly robust physique.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Burrow underground: Thrumborats can burrow underground to make them almost immune to damage for a short while.
A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a lot of defense and makes the subject more resistant to extremes of temperature.

A leftover bioweapon from a now extinct civilisation designed to completely eradicate biomes with dense vegetation. Records indicate that the local indigineous tribes were very resistant to corporate takeover and the megacorp in charge wanted to reduce profit losses. Eventually the planet starved as they were unable to stop these creatures from feasting on farmlands and food reserves.

A colossal hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. A slow moving tank with limited regenerative capabilities, they are almost unkillable if it weren’t for their crippling hunger. As durable as a capital ship, any colony that could possibly support the diet of even a single one would be considered impervious to raids.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. They are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.

A huge, boar-like animal that will eat anything on its path. Thrumdraedons are cunning and resourceful.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Keen senses ability: Twice a day, the Thrumdraeodon can activate an ability that gives it increased awareness of its surreoundings.

One of the largest creatures in existence, dwarfing even the thrumbo and the mighty Gallatross. Capable of transporting a massive amount of material, producing a luxurious and excellent wool as well as thick, nutritious milk, they are almost the perfect farm animal. That is as long as you don’t look at your feed costs and die of a heart attack, but the scientists that designed this hybrid never had to worry about such things. Food is something for the proles to worry about.

A colossal hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Completely herbivorous and with an appetite large enough to cause even large colonies to starve within a single season. This hybrid can prove worth the food costs as it produces thick, delicious milk every couple of days as well as luxurious wool every couple of seasons. Slightly more dimwitted than its progenitor and will attempt to wander off in search of food if not kept penned up.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
Milk from a thrumffalo. Very nutritive. Also with a sky-high cholesterol content, but in the Rim nobody judges you.

A very nice combination of two highly adaptive baseline creatures which arguably results in the best riding companion man could ask for. While this isn’t apparent in their speed, it definitely is so once a rider experiences the thundering beats of this horsey positively barreling through entire forests and marshland in order to earn its next sugar cube the size of a fist.

A colossal hybrid with recessive equine genes. While they have a lower muscle-to-fat ratio compared to their progenitor, the overall placement and shape of the horn vastly reduces their combat efficiency. Instead, they more than make up for this shortfall with an incredible endurance even compared to paragons. An excellent riding companion that is easier to train and can even carry an acceptable amount of luggage, the majority of which contains feed.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM. Always starts as draftable, even if not using a controller booster to create it. Charge ability: when drafted, the player can use the charge ability to make the hybrid jump towards a target and cause massive damage with its horn to a single random body part. The ability can be triggered every 2 hours.

There’s a saying about the scientific community, namely “your scientists were so preoccupied with whether they could, they didn’t stop to think if they should”. This is entirely relevant to this creature, a super predator with practically no equal, a single one of these is enough to require an entire rainforest to support their daily sustenance. If that wasn’t bad enough, they were made along with the Thrumbocat as part of a duo.

A colossal hybrid with recessive canine genes. A large predator with an insane appetite with the necessary ferocity to support itself, very few colonies can support the diet of such a hybrid. Incredibly robust, fast and a terrifying ferocity, they will hunt multiple times a day and often go after prey much bigger than themselves.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.

A workplace accident waiting to happen, the Tissue Grinder takes the corpses of animals and carefully rips and tears them to pieces in order to extract useful genetic data in the form of Genoframes. The hardware is also capable of combining two identical frames into a superior form by comparing and improving upon latent and inferior genetic material.

Workspeed is influenced by someone who actually knows what they’re looking for in order to skip some of the more routine tasks, and possibly shouldn’t be placed into the hands of someone with no concept of ethics, morality or conscience.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> Bear in mind that the cost in corpses effectively doubles for each quality level, making a legendary genoframe prohibitive. The main usefulness of these recipes is to convert two genoframes of a given quality to a higher one, but your main source of genoframes shouldn't be hand crafting them. Go out there and explore abandoned labs!

This particular machine can be explained as a simplified and miniaturized version of a Genomorpher, using only a single genome which influences an inserted Genoframe. Instead of trying to create an entire animal, it instead creates very specific limbs or organs. Due to the vastly simplified process and minimal variables, no complications arise during the process and the resulting product is similarly influenced by the quality of the Genoframe.\n\nThe Implant Augmentator can be used to create implants for animals - not just hybrids - with the quality of Genoframe dictating the deficits or bonuses those implants provide. Not all implants are created equal, some being inferior replacements and others providing new capabilities provided the animal in question can support them. Human implants are also a possibility.
Drinking from this will give you more than just a few rads...

An improvised explosive trap built out of... best if you don't think about it. Cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm. Less potent than regular high-explosive shell traps.

This enormous ceratopsid can be used as an incredibly powerful pack beast. It is also quite capable of defending itself, with its sheer mass and huge horns.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Produces wool, eggs, milk, and acts as a very powerful, though slow, pack beast.
Fertilized triceraffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a triceraffalo.
Unfertilized triceraffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

A cheeky little shit that likes to go around and play with switches, to satisfy their curiosity much like how an ignorant child would. Perhaps a little too smart for their own good. Their behaviour is similar to failed service animals that learn how to operate household items without restraint.

An avian hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Surprisingly hardy, quick and very intelligent, these creatures make for a particularly filthy and hungry egg-layer. Their little wings are quite dextrous and can be used to flick switches.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Turkeymen periodically flick switches around the colony (any building that can be toggled on / off by a colonist).
Fertilized turkeyman egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized turkeyman egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

A payload of ursine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary ursine hybrids have stunning attacks, and high body size.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:

This raptor theropod hybrid escaped the shame of having feathers by instead having hair. So, uhm, still ridiculous.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
Fertilized velociwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a velociwolf.
Unfertilized velociwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
This hybrid implant substitutes the subject's jaw with a set of venomous fangs. Useful in combat, but not very comfortable.
This strange milk has a sour, chemical taste.

Some insane madman thought it would be an excellent idea to strap a flamethrower to a dog and send it out into the world to see what happens. Don’t be surprised to find out this unusual hybrid started a massive forest fire trying to hunt a rabbit.

A canine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. A bit more dim-witted compared to their baseline, due to a large amount of brain processing power dedicated to the complex internal organs that permit it to spit out burning chemfuel. Excellent flankers thanks to their increased speed and ranged ability.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.

A massively muscled boi with strong claws, sharper teeth and a mean attitude. Adorable when it’s trying to jump you to lick your face, less so when those same paws are trying to restrain you so it can bite your head off.

A canine hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Stronger, more aggressive and with a bigger appetite, this hybrid’s particular lethality comes from its tendency to attack the enemy’s head to try and decapitate them.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. There is a 25% chance that wolfbear attacks will target its victim's head, possibly decapitating enemies. Fun!

Probably an excellent example of a precursor to the alphabeaver, this beastie has an insane appetite and constantly growing teeth that it wears down by chewing on trees. Either consider creating a tree grove just for them to eat or moving to a jungle because they’re gonna be procreating a whole bunch.

A canine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Fast gestation period, highly intelligent, incredibly fast and lethal. The price for these benefits is that it has a constant hunger for trees and shares ancestry with another rapid dendrovore.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.

Dogs and cats are domesticated predators who are just the absolute cutest. So someone wanted to see how dangerous they could make something by combining these two proto predators. The results could be imagined but we’ll let them speak for themselves.

A canine hybrid with recessive feline genes. A combination of two predators makes for an incredible loyal killing machine. Quick, lethal, low maintenance and a desire for companionship. If that wasn’t already enough, it can even randomly attack twice by lunging with both claws and teeth.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Double slash: 25% of the time its attacks will strike twice.

Not many people are familiar with just how vicious a chicken can be, with them even going so far to pick at their own wounds or actively eating each other if hungry. Combining that with the sleek hunting form of a wolf and making them vegetarian creates a deadly combo. You’d go mad too if you smelled something delicious but couldn’t stomach eating it.

A canine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Very quick on their feet and very filthy, care must be taken as they are far less suited to cold temperatures. The inherent viciousness of chickens has been combined with a proper combat form, vastly boosting their speed after killing its prey.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. Blood frenzy ability: after killing prey, the wolfchicken receives 200% speed for an hour.
Fertilized Wolfchicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
Unfertilized Wolfchicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

While they make for a completely daft canine hybrid, they’re still smart enough not to wander off. Anyone familiar with dogs that shed enough to make another animal will feel right at home with this hybrid. Even the milk it produces comes already laden with hair for your displeasure!

A canine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. A great producer of thick wool and, regrettably, hairy milk. This allows them to survive in even more extreme colds, but they frequently make a mess, lack most of their intellect and need to eat a lot.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
This milk seems to be filled with strands of grey wool. Disgusting, and quite hard to drink...

Combining two of the most renowned endurance hunters in human history has resulted in man’s best friend whom you can also ride into battle with. Needless to say, the creation of this capricious canine was celebrated by many wealthy families who otherwise caused unfortunate accidents when they tried to ride their household dog when they clearly became too old or heavy for them to carry. What was not appreciated was their tendency to overestimate their - admittedly formidable - predatory capabilities.

An equine hybrid with recessive canine genes. A fast and ferocious dedicated carnivore sharing the hyperactive nature of sled dogs. Requires slightly more frequent retraining compared to similar hybrids but is overall better due to not having any extra features.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
An implant that uses genetic splicing with wolf genes to give an old (or damaged) jaw decent functioning. This implant is not meant to replace healthy jaws.
The subjects legs can be modified by weaving hybrid human/wolf muscle fibers within the base tissue, giving them a significant strength and speed boost.
This wolf pelt can be used as a trade good. It can also be crafted into a decorative wolf rug.
A wolf pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!

The answer to all your worries about escaping slaves. These omnivorous vicious beasties are effectively exotic hunting dogs, and the most exotic and forbidden prey of all is humans. That is, provided they’re practically unable to defend themselves. Everyone knows you can kill as many people as you like, just don’t hurt the poor puppy trying to tear your throat out.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive canine genes. Incredibly fast and with multiple hardened jaw plates, these ferocious hunting dogs make for an excellent, intelligent combat hybrid. Unable to take much punishment but the armour plating should help against those lacking in offensive weaponry.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.

A terrifyingly alien creature, the wolf shrimp is keenly intelligent like its wolf forefather, slithering in blinding speed towards its prey.\n\nUseful abilities: Extremely fast regenerative capabilities.

A great doggo that sheds snakeskin instead of fur, so don’t be too alarmed when scratching at an itchy spot makes skin flake off. It’ll shed itself, even when out hunting prey, so be prepared to occasionally go on a long trek to pick up the valuable skin.

A reptile hybrid with recessive canine genes. Fast, intelligent and with a nasty venomous bite, these hybrids make for an excellent hunting hound. They lack any notion of fear so they may end up going after much larger, more dangerous prey and achieving a pyrrhic victory.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.

Just like Wooly Mammoths and Wooly Rhinos, the Wooly Wolf is a creature perfectly adapted to cold climates. Wooly wolves are an excellent source of high quality wool, since they produce it much faster than a mammoth.\n\nUseful abilities: Fast wool production
Hard overlapping scales of insect chitin laid over a thick layer of semi-rigid leather. Quite durable in combat, but provides little protection from weather.
Fertilized chickenbee egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickenbee.

This is a honeycomb produced by cattle bees. Cattle bees have muffalo genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in a thick white pelt, and produce milk, wool and muffalo genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get milk, wool and muffalo genetic material.

This is a honeycomb produced by clucking bees. Clucking bees have chicken genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in feathers, and produce eggs and avian genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get bee eggs, genewax and avian genetic material.

This is a honeycomb produced by exploding bees. Exploding bees have boomalope genes spliced in their genome. This bees produce genetic honey, chemfuel, and boom genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, chemfuel, and booma genetic material.

This honey has been extracted from any genetic honeycomb, and has a very strange greenish tone that makes it less appetizing. Decays incredibly fast, but it can be consumed as a potent physically enhancing drug that doesn't cause any side effects. This honey can be consumed raw or cooked with other ingredients, and its properties transfer to the resulting meal.

Genewax is extracted from combs of the Genetic family (any Vanilla Genetics Expanded hybrid bees) and is much less resistant than regular beeswax. Its only practical application is that it can be used to create genoframes, instead of corpses.

This is a honeycomb produced by horned bees. Horned bees have thrumbo genes spliced in their genome. They take a very long time to generate honeycombs, but they produce highly sought after colossal genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax, and colossal genetic material.

This is a honeycomb produced by hound bees. Hound bees have wolf genes spliced in their genome. They produce canine genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and canine genetic material.

This is a honeycomb produced by hybernating bees. Hybernating bees have grizzly bear genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in a soft fur. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and bear genetic material.

This is a honeycomb produced by megaspidery bees. Megaspidery bees have insectoid genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in chitinous plates and produce insect jelly and insectoid genetic materials. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get insect jelly, genewax and insectoid genetic material.

This is a honeycomb produced by mousy bees. Mousy bees have rodent genes spliced in their genome. They produce rodent genetic materials and many find their constant high-pitched squeaking sounds to be quite distracting. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and rodent genetic material.

This is a honeycomb produced by organic bees. Organic bees are genetically modified bees that extract cellulose fibers from nearby plants and convert it into a slurry of genetic material which can be refined into template genomes. The main use of these bees, however, is to hybridize them with other bees to create genetic bees of different subspecies. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get honey, beeswax and template genomes.

This is a honeycomb produced by purring bees. Purring bees have cat genes spliced in their genome. They produce feline genetic materials and a lot of videos on the internet. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and feline genetic material.

TScaly bees have reptile genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in tough looking scales and produce reptilian genetic materials. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and reptile genetic material.
A fake cheese, from a fake milk. Best not to think where this came from.
Ph'nglui mglw'nafh Cheese R'lyeh wgah'nagl fhtagn. The only brand of cheese that would please the Great Old Ones themselves.
A green coloured cheese that is actually quite unappetizing.
A delicious cheese that takes a VERY long time to ripen. Paraceramuffalo Brie is certainly worth the wait, being one of the most nutritive cheeses in the Rim.
A table equipped with various tools suited for restyling hair and beards.
Large beads made of any variety of material used to signify devotion to one's beliefs.
A tall, regal ceremonial headdress meant to adorn the head of leaders of Christian faith.
A small, plain hat meant to rest gently on oneâ€™s head. Those who wear it often claim it radiates a certain angelic overtone.
A distinguished, brimmed hat adorned with a badge of bravery, meant to inspire cooperation in one's subordinates at any cost.
A simple hood with a forward facing mounted animal skull on the front. This piece is commonly seen worn by those of tribal persuasion and those close to nature.
A flat-topped conical hat decorated with a black tassel, often worn by desert nobility.
A cult-like hood meant to be worn for anonymity and collectivism during rituals or worn as a sign of great humility.
A warm, floppy hat which covers one's ears, often worn by those belonging to alpinian tribes.
A jovial hat that mimics the appearance of an equestrian, party animals far and wide know it to be an epitome of comical relief.
A standard rank-and-file military cap meant to be worn during non-combat procedures, though brave officers are known to wear them during combat as well.
A set of rags meant to cover oneself, offers almost no protection or warmth, but meant to convey detachment from life's pleasures and peace with oneself.
A small, extremely plain hat meant to cover only the crown of one's head. Often worn continuously by the pious and devout alike.
A halo-shaped headpiece meant to accentuate the wearer as being that of profane standing. Often worn by those who find salvation in suffering.
A very wide-brimmed and rustic hat meant to be worn by sheriffs and lawbringers of far off steamworlds.
A low-lying bush that produces chocolate, genetically engineered to sweeten its own product, and be plantable in almost any vessel.

Raw arapaima. On old Earth, it was among the world's largest freshwater fish, reaching as much as 3 m in length. It was an important food fish.\n\nThis is an adept catch that is caught in addition to other fish.

Raw arapaima. On old Earth, it was among the world's largest freshwater fish, reaching as much as 3 m in length. It was an important food fish.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.

Raw Atlantic butterfish. The Atlantic butterfish is distinguished by its very thin, deep body, resembling a flounder on edge. It earned its buttery name from its high oil content.\n\nThis is an adept catch that is caught in addition to other fish.

Raw Atlantic butterfish. The Atlantic butterfish is distinguished by its very thin, deep body, resembling a flounder on edge. It earned its buttery name from its high oil content.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.

Raw crayfish. Crayfish are freshwater crustaceans resembling small lobsters (to which they are related). Some species are found in brooks and streams, where fresh water is running, while others thrive in swamps, ditches, and paddy fields.\n\nThis is an adept catch that is caught in addition to other fish.

Raw flying fish. While they cannot fly in the same way a bird does, flying fish can make powerful, self-propelled leaps out of the water where their long wing-like fins enable gliding for considerable distances above the water's surface.\n\nThis is an adept catch that is caught in addition to other fish.

Raw freshwater stingray. Freshwater stingrays are one of the only rays inhabiting freshwater rather than saltwater. Raw shortfin mako shark. A large, predatory shark that lives in the open ocean and is often considered the fastest shark. It is known for its incredible leaping ability and can be observed jumping to extreme heights (out of the water) when hunting.\n\nThis is an adept catch that is caught in addition to other fish.

An ancient air conditioning machine. Its internals were smashed long ago and what wasn't looted has rusted away in the time since.
The remains of an ancient armoured troop carrier which was destroyed by some kind of heavy weapon. All the useful components were looted or deteriorated long ago.
An ancient ATM. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
An ancient, broken barrel that looks like it once contained waste of some sort. The only thing it contains now is a smear of dried sludge.
An old, rotted mattress on a rusty frame. Though uncomfortable, it can still be used, but it's too dilapidated to restore.
A shell from a smashed centipede. It has been deteriorating for many years and all the useful components have disintegrated.
A large container from some ancient freight carrying vehicle. It has long since been looted and deteriorted into uselessness.
An ancient, broken crate. Any valuables it once contained have degraded to uselessness.
An ancient computer interface. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
An ancient dropship engine. It probably came off a military troop carrier. Its delicate internals are rusted to uselessness, and the valuable parts were looted long ago.
A collection of ancient equipment blocks of unknown function. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
An ancient fence made of reinforced concrete posts joined by concrete panels.
An ancient electrical generator. Its usable parts were smashed or looted long ago, and the rest has rusted into uselessness.
A giant wheel which came off some large vehicle many ages ago. The rubber has disintegrated away and the metal is twisted and rusted.
An ancient hydrant. Heavily rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
An ancient jet engine, probably detached from a large airplane. Its delicate internals are rusted to uselessness, and the valuable parts were looted long ago.
An ancient kitchen sink made of some non-metallic fiber-sheet material. It is badly deteriorated and there's no way to salvage anything from it.\n\nHow it got here must be a long story.
An ancient self-powered lamp. Its internal power supply is badly depleted, but it still emits a weak glow.
A large crate which was abandoned ages ago. It's too rusted to store anything.
An large, ancient engine block from some giant vehicle or power source. It's rusted through, and all the useful components are gone. No repair could salvage this.
An ancient, rusted bank of lockers. All of the lockers have been forced open with nothing valuable left behind.
A long, empty crate which was abandoned ages ago. It's too rusted to store anything.
A large ancient machine case filled with unknown machinery. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
A shell from a smashed mechanoid. It has been deteriorating for many years and the useful components have disintegrated.
The broken shell of a mech drop beacon. Its useful components are missing and the shell has deteriorated after many years.
A barrel from a long-range cannon for artillery or anti-ship use. When it was new, it must have been frighteningly powerful. Now, it's far beyond repair and every useful component has been looted.
A mounting platform that once held some sort of cannon. It has been broken for many years and every useful component was looted long ago.
An ancient device for heating food. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
An ancient, military-looking crate. It was broken long ago and any valuables it once contained were looted.
The shattered remains of an ancient sub-scale warsprinter. Whatever parts weren't ruined when it was destroyed were looted long ago.
A table once used for surgical operations. It has long since deteriorated into uselessness.
An ancient oven. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
A section from an ancient pipeline. It once carried some valuable fluid over long distances. Now it's been looted and deteriorated to uselessness.
A bunch of rusted old pipes. It is hard to say how these ended up here.
An ancient pod car once capable of automated flight. Its valuable parts were looted long ago, and the rest is badly deteriorated.
An ancient postbox, smashed and deteriorated to uselessness. It seems to have been moved here long ago for some unknown reason.
A piece of ancient razor wire. It has been deteriorating for many years and no longer poses any danger.
An ancient refrigerator. It was stripped of interesting parts long ago.
An ancient, broken car. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
An ancient, rusted car frame. The body shell has long since rusted away to nothing.
The remains of an ancient dropship. The valuable parts were looted long ago and the rest has deteriorated to uselessness.
An ancient engine block that is rusted through. No repair could salvage this.
An ancient, broken troop carrier. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
An ancient, broken truck. Everything useful either rusted away or was looted long ago.
An ancient, broken security turret. The valuable parts have all been looted or smashed.
An ancient, broken ship navigation beacon. Every useful component was looted long ago.
An ancient, rusted shopping cart. How exactly it got here is a mystery, but the story is long and probably quite sad.
A small, empty crate which was abandoned ages ago. It's too rusted to store anything.
Ancient chunks of space junk that fell from orbit. Perhaps a relic of an ancient space battle.
An ancient, broken crate. Any valuables it once contained were taken long ago.
An ancient device that looks like it once stored some kind of fluid. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
An ancient stove. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
An ancient rack unit packed with unidentifiable devices. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
The remains of an ancient tank which was destroyed by some kind of heavy weapon. All the useful components were looted or deteriorated long ago.
An defensive fortification for stopping vehicles. It's been deteriorating for many years.
An ancient toilet. Smashed long ago, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
An ancient vending machine. It was smashed ages ago and generations of looters have taken every conceivably useful part. How it ended up here is a mystery.
The ruined remains of an ancient mid-scale warspider. It has been picked over many times, and there is nothing valuable left to loot.
The assault claw of an ancient mid-scale warwalker. This weapon specialized in tearing through heavy plasteel armor at close range. Its valuable components were looted long ago.
A massive foot from an ancient warwalker. It seems to have been torn free from its owner by some kind of heavy weapon. All the useful components were looted or deteriorated long ago.
The shattered leg of a mid-scale warwalker that fell here long ago. It has been picked clean by looters, and no valuable components remain.
Most of the armored shell of a warwalker. It appears to have been torn off by some colossal weapon system. All the valuable parts were looted, and the rest is badly deteriorated.
The torso of a mid-scale warwalker which fell here in ancient days. The valuable parts were looted long ago.
An ancient device used for washing textiles. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
An ancient wheel from some kind of vehicle. The rubber has deteriorated away and the metal is twisted and rusted.
An incredibly large and bright-burning bonfire made out of a carefully organized pile of logs on a stone base. Used heavily during flame-based rituals and can only be placed outdoors.

A large, swollen hive that seems to be seeping high amounts of bubbling insectoid jelly but not incubating any insectoids themselves.\n\nThe nest will need maintenance by a colonist with the insectoid herder role. If maintenance reaches 0%, the nest will be destroyed.

A primitive, though quite ingenious barricade made of wooden stick and netting meant to entrap incoming fish and leave them with no way out. Fish traps are eventually destroyed by the currents and struggling fish, and will need replacing.\n\nThis is a large trap that will only catch large sized fish.

A structure assembled from scrapped mechanoid parts. Emits an IFF programming signal capable of confusing ancient mechanoid receptors. Should two exist per colonist enough signal noise will soft reset ancient mechanoid programming, causing an immediate retreat.

A primitive, though quite ingenious barricade made of wooden stick and netting meant to entrap incoming fish and leave them with no way out. Fish traps are eventually destroyed by the currents and struggling fish, and will need replacing.\n\nThis is a medium trap that will only catch medium sized fish.
An ominous-looking obelisk of pure chiseled stone. It seems to radiate an aura of hopelessness and despair.\n\nThe Corruption need of any colonist in an obelisk's radius of effect won't decrease.
A stone bas relief of serketist imagery for decorating a floor.
A stone bas relief of serketist imagery for decorating a floor.

A primitive, though quite ingenious barricade made of wooden stick and netting meant to entrap incoming fish and leave them with no way out. Fish traps are eventually destroyed by the currents and struggling fish, and will need replacing.\n\nThis is a small trap that will only catch small sized fish.
A small metal box filled with different hooks, bobbers and baits meant to entice fish big and small to latch on before being reeled in via line and rod.
A stone bas relief of shintao imagery for decorating a floor.
A stone bas relief of shintao imagery for decorating a floor.
A large humanoid figure made out of dry brush, thatch, and wood scraps. Prisoners have to be physically restrained to the figurine then burned alive as part of a trial or ritual.

A bedside device that feeds a person lying in bed whenever they are hungry, provided that nutrient paste dripper is connected to a nutrient paste vat. It must be placed directly adjacent to and facing the head of the bed. Placing more than one nutrient paste dripper near the same bed has no effect.
A small pipe output that allows colonists and citizens to pick up nutrient paste.

A machine that synthesizes edible nutrient paste from organic feedstocks placed in adjacent hoppers. It consumes less ingredients and time than any other meal production method - but nobody likes eating nutrient paste. Accepts raw food, but not rough plant matter like hay. It does not actually dispense the nutrient paste, but it allows it to be pumped into vats and dispensers in the network.
Light steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as nutrient paste from grinders and storage vats to dispensers.
A hopper-like storage basin meant to hold dispensed nutrient paste meal from the vats. Can be used by colonists and prisoners. Nutrient paste meal dispensed and ready for pick-up can deteriorate.
A large tank that can store up to 75 nutrient paste meals in it. Makes a big mess when destroyed.
Subterranean steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as nutrient paste from grinders and storage vats to dispensers.

An ultratech staff threaded with psychic focusing materials. While it is a solid melee weapon, its main purpose is to enhance the wielder's psychic sensitivity and neural heat dissipation, allowing more use of psychic powers.\n\nThis weapon has an onboard persona that can bond to only a single person. The wielder and intelligent weapon can synchronize their reflexes and attack with frightening speed, accuracy, and creativity. Once bonded to a wielder, the weapon's persona will refuse to be wielded by anyone else.
A large-leafed desert plant with edible flowers and stalks.

A tree bearing hard apples, with a light green skin and crisp, juicy flesh. The flavour is tart and acidic. It remains firm when baked, making it a very popular cooking apple used in pies, where it can be sweetened.

A tall tropical tree adorned with a cluster of yellow, elongated berries. These trees have been genetically modified to resist the fungal infections that nearly caused their extinction in old Terra. The Gros Michel variety has a distinct taste, different from that of Cavendish variety bananas. Bananas are easy to harvest, since a whole "hand" can be cut at the same time.

These are small, pink berries known as bearberries. While raw bearberries possess an acidic and bland taste, after cooking a natural sweetness comes through and the results can be much more palatable.

A low-lying bush speckled with pink berries that thrives in poor, even sandy soil. While raw bearberries possess an acidic and bland taste, after cooking a natural sweetness comes through and the results can be much more palatable. This plant can be grown in pots.

An herb-like vegetable widely cultivated for humans and livestock alike. Though these look like ordinary beets from the outside, on the inside they have distinct red-and-white-striped flesh. These are highest on the sweetness scale. While beets do require rich soil they are quite hearty, and will continue to grow very slowly through winter. They need to be planted during growing season, though.

A large round vegetable that has a hard white centre surrounded by green leaves, naturally high in fiber and B-vitamins. This particular strain seems highly resistant to airborne toxins and radiation.

Also known as the cherry blossom, Sakura trees produce small fruits known as sakuranbo, small cherries that do not have much flesh beyond the seed within. Due to their bitter taste, sakuranbo are not traditionally eaten raw, but these trees have been genetically modified to produce sweeter tasting fruits. This particular genus is quite beautiful, speckled with pink blossoms throughout, blooming fully each winter.

A bulbous, tender, tasty purplish-black fruit of the nightshade family. Aubergines or eggplants are great in a variety of dishes, and the Black Beauty variety is delicious grilled, baked and stir-fried. Flourishes in warm weather.
Cultivated forage grass. Grows anywhere there is a little light and minimally fertile ground.
A strong and pungent variety of onions, perfect for flavourful salads or for general cooking applications when strong flavours are desired. Cannot suffer the effects of blight.

More often referred to as the “prickly pear” cactus, this plant is known to be quite bountiful even in hot, dry deserts. The pads of this tall, spiny cactus bloom pink, melon-like edible fruits, used historically for both snacking as well as medicine by desert natives.

A small citrus tree with fruit resembling miniature oranges, usually eaten plain or in fruit salads. Mandarins are smaller and oblate, rather than spherical, like the common oranges (which are a mandarin hybrid). The taste is considered less sour, as well as sweeter and stronger. Mandarin trees are well equipped for growing in humid environments and in wet soil.

The apricot is a fruit that resembles the peach but much smaller, pale yellow or orange with some red shades. It is mainly consumed fresh, although it is also used to make some by-products like stewed fruit, jams, juices and dried apricots. Apricots have a high concentration of vitamin C. This variety of apricot tree is quite hardy, and they can be planted in stony soil.

A stout tree bearing crispy green pears, pear trees are notable for requiring high amounts of water and cool weather, and also doing quite well in shade. While at first similar to apples, pears are slightly more fibrous and less sweet.

Spherical green seeds tucked safely away in a fleshy pod. Pea plants are known to be quite thirsty, and will benefit greatly from rainfall. Peas are also notable for having potentially long shelf-lives when dried properly.

Tiny, spicy, crimson pods often used in sauces, relishes, or powders. While the flavor itself is mild the capsaicin contained inside means humans find them oddly addicting, if painful when consumed in mass. Animals are known to avoid eating them entirely.

The Damson plum tree, also archaically called the "damascene", is a bushy tree bearing a relatively small ovoid fruit with a distinctive, somewhat astringent taste, widely used for culinary purposes, particularly in fruit preserves and jams. Many cultures also dry-age plums into prunes for long-term preservation.

One of the biggest varieties of pumpkin, the mammoth gold can grow to between 25 and 50 Kg of weight. Technically a giant berry, pumpkins are renowned for being trivial to both sow and harvest. However, their durability means added weight and mass - Too cumbersome for hydroponics basins.

Hard apples, with a light green skin and crisp, juicy flesh. The flavour is tart and acidic. It remains firm when baked, making it a very popular cooking apple used in pies, where it can be sweetened.

Yellow, elongated berries. These bananas have been genetically modified to resist the fungal infections that nearly caused their extinction in old Terra. The Gros Michel variety has a distinct taste, different from that of Cavendish variety bananas.

An herb-like vegetable widely cultivated for humans and livestock alike. Though these look like ordinary beets from the outside, on the inside they have distinct red-and-white-striped flesh. These are highest on the sweetness scale.
A large round vegetable that has a hard white centre surrounded by green leaves, naturally high in fiber and B-vitamins.

Small cherries that do not have much flesh beyond the seed within. Due to their bitter taste, sakuranbo are not traditionally eaten raw, but these fruits have been genetically modified to have a sweeter taste.

A bulbous, tender, tasty purplish-black fruit of the nightshade family. Aubergines or eggplants are great in a variety of dishes, and the Black Beauty variety is delicious grilled, baked and stir-fried.
A strong and pungent variety of onions, perfect for flavourful salads or for general cooking applications when strong flavours are desired.
